docs.title: GeniXCMS Documentation
docs.text:
Welcome to the official documentation for GeniXCMS — a modern, PHP-based Content Management System and Framework that is lightweight, extensible, and developer-friendly.

Built on the rock-solid foundations of Bootstrap and powered by the high-performance Latte templating engine, GeniXCMS provides the ultimate toolset for building everything from simple blogs to complex, custom web applications.

docs.title: 🧭 Quick Navigation
docs.text: Topic Description 🚀 Installation Get GeniXCMS up and running on your server in minutes. ⚙️ Configuration Set up site name, email, permalinks, and localization. 📝 Managing Content Master the art of creating and publishing blog posts. 🎨 Theme Development Craft beautiful frontend experiences using Latte & Hooks. 🔌 Module Creation Extend core functionality with modular administrative panels. 🏷️ Shortcodes Guide Create dynamic, sanitizer-proof tags for posts and pages. 🖼️ Media Integration Use the native GxMedia selector for cross-module asset selection. ⚡ Asset Bundling Use Vite, SCSS, and Tailwind for state-of-the-art styling. 🗃️ Query Builder Fluent, chainable interface for secure database operations. 🏗️ ORM / Models Active Record layer for intuitive data manipulation. 📡 RESTful API (v1) Transform your site into a Headless CMS powerhouse. 🐹 Go API Service Boost read performance with a dedicated high-speed Go backend. 🛡️ Security Infrastructure Deep dive into CSP headers and hardening standards. 🏺 Modular Widgets Build dynamic, parameter-driven widget blocks using AJAX. 🛠️ System Maintenance Monitor server health and manage ecosystem updates.

docs.title: ✨ Core Ecosystem Features
docs.text:
GeniXCMS is designed with both performance and security in mind.

  • Fast & Secure Rendering: Powered by Latte, featuring automated XSS protection and context-aware escaping.
  • Smart URL Routing: Decoupled, regex-based path matching for perfect SEO-friendly URLs.
  • Global Localization: Native multi-language support (i18n) for international audiences.
  • Extensible Hook System: Highly flexible Action & Filter hooks for non-invasive core extensions.
  • Enterprise Security: Built-in Content Security Policy (CSP), CSRF protection, and hardened HTTP headers.
  • Modern Asset Pipeline: Complete integration with Vite for HMR-powered frontend development.
  • Fine-Grained ACL: Robust group-based permissions management through an intuitive matrix.
  • headless Ready: Built-in JSON API engine to power mobile apps or single-page applications.

docs.title: 📦 System Stack Support
docs.text: Requirement Supported Versions PHP Engine 8.1, 8.2, 8.3 & 8.4+ Databases MySQL 5.7+, MariaDB 10.3+, SQLite3, PostgreSQL Server Apache (with mod_rewrite), Nginx, Litespeed

docs.title: 🤝 Community & Support
docs.text:
Join the community and help us build the future of open-source CMS development!

  • 🌐 Official Website: genixcms.web.id
  • 💻 Source Code: GeniXCMS GitHub Repository
  • 📧 Technical Support: genixcms@gmail.com

[!NOTE] This documentation is currently in v2.0.0 stage. Some features may evolve rapidly as we move toward the stable release.

docs.title: Privacy Policy
docs.text:
This privacy policy has been compiled to better serve those who are concerned with how their 'Personally Identifiable Information' (PII) is being used online. PII, as described in US privacy law and information security, is information that can be used on its own or with other information to identify, contact, or locate a single person, or to identify an individual in context. Please read our privacy policy carefully to get a clear understanding of how we collect, use, protect or otherwise handle your Personally Identifiable Information in accordance with our website.

What personal information do we collect from the people that visit our blog, website or app? When ordering or registering on our site, as appropriate, you may be asked to enter your name, email address, phone number or other details to help you with your experience.

docs.title: When do we collect information?
docs.text: We collect information from you when you register on our site or enter information on our site.

docs.title: How do we use your information?
docs.text: We may use the information we collect from you when you register, make a purchase, sign up for our newsletter, respond to a survey or marketing communication, surf the website, or use certain other site features in the following ways: • To personalize your experience and to allow us to deliver the type of content and product offerings in which you are most interested.

docs.title: How do we protect your information?
docs.text:
• We do not use vulnerability scanning and/or scanning to PCI standards.
  • We only provide articles and information. We never ask for credit card numbers.
  • We use regular Malware Scanning.
  • Your personal information is contained behind secured networks and is only accessible by a limited number of persons who have special access rights to such systems, and are required to keep the information confidential. In addition, all sensitive/credit information you supply is encrypted via Secure Socket Layer (SSL) technology.
  • We implement a variety of security measures when a user enters, submits, or accesses their information to maintain the safety of your personal information. All transactions are processed through a gateway provider and are not stored or processed on our servers.

docs.title: Do we use 'cookies'?
docs.text:
Yes. Cookies are small files that a site or its service provider transfers to your computer's hard drive through your Web browser (if you allow) that enables the site's or service provider's systems to recognize your browser and capture and remember certain information. For instance, we use cookies to help us remember and process the items in your shopping cart. They are also used to help us understand your preferences based on previous or current site activity, which enables us to provide you with improved services. We also use cookies to help us compile aggregate data about site traffic and site interaction so that we can offer better site experiences and tools in the future.

We use cookies to:

  • Help remember and process the items in the shopping cart.
  • Understand and save user's preferences for future visits.
  • Keep track of advertisements.
  • Compile aggregate data about site traffic and site interactions in order to offer better site experiences and tools in the future. We may also use trusted third-party services that track this information on our behalf.

You can choose to have your computer warn you each time a cookie is being sent, or you can choose to turn off all cookies. You do this through your browser settings. Since browser is a little different, look at your browser's Help Menu to learn the correct way to modify your cookies.

If you turn cookies off, some features will be disabled. It won't affect the user's experience that make your site experience more efficient and may not function properly.

However, you will still be able to place orders .

docs.title: Third-party disclosure
docs.text: We do not sell, trade, or otherwise transfer to outside parties your Personally Identifiable Information.

docs.title: Third-party links
docs.text: Occasionally, at our discretion, we may include or offer third-party products or services on our website. These third-party sites have separate and independent privacy policies. We therefore have no responsibility or liability for the content and activities of these linked sites. Nonetheless, we seek to protect the integrity of our site and welcome any feedback about these sites.

docs.title: Google
docs.text: Google's advertising requirements can be summed up by Google's Advertising Principles. They are put in place to provide a positive experience for users. https://support.google.com/adwordspolicy/answer/1316548?hl=en

docs.title: We use Google AdSense Advertising on our website.
docs.text:
Google, as a third-party vendor, uses cookies to serve ads on our site. Google's use of the DART cookie enables it to serve ads to our users based on previous visits to our site and other sites on the Internet. Users may opt-out of the use of the DART cookie by visiting the Google Ad and Content Network privacy policy.

We have implemented the following:

  • Remarketing with Google AdSense
  • Demographics and Interests Reporting
  • DoubleClick Platform Integration

We, along with third-party vendors such as Google use first-party cookies (such as the Google Analytics cookies) and third-party cookies (such as the DoubleClick cookie) or other third-party identifiers together to compile data regarding user interactions with ad impressions and other ad service functions as they relate to our website.

docs.title: Opting out:
docs.text: Users can set preferences for how Google advertises to you using the Google Ad Settings page. Alternatively, you can opt out by visiting the Network Advertising Initiative Opt Out page or by using the Google Analytics Opt Out Browser add on.

docs.title: California Online Privacy Protection Act
docs.text: CalOPPA is the first state law in the nation to require commercial websites and online services to post a privacy policy. The law's reach stretches well beyond California to require any person or company in the United States (and conceivably the world) that operates websites collecting Personally Identifiable Information from California consumers to post a conspicuous privacy policy on its website stating exactly the information being collected and those individuals or companies with whom it is being shared. - See more at: http://consumercal.org/california-online-privacy-protection-act-caloppa/#sthash.0FdRbT51.dpuf

docs.title: According to CalOPPA, we agree to the following:
docs.text:
Users can visit our site anonymously. Once this privacy policy is created, we will add a link to it on our home page or as a minimum, on the first significant page after entering our website. Our Privacy Policy link includes the word 'Privacy' and can be easily be found on the page specified above.

You will be notified of any Privacy Policy changes:

  • On our Privacy Policy Page

Can change your personal information:

  • By logging in to your account

docs.title: How does our site handle Do Not Track signals?
docs.text: We honor Do Not Track signals and Do Not Track, plant cookies, or use advertising when a Do Not Track (DNT) browser mechanism is in place.

docs.title: Does our site allow third-party behavioral tracking?
docs.text: It's also important to note that we allow third-party behavioral tracking

docs.title: COPPA (Children Online Privacy Protection Act)
docs.text: When it comes to the collection of personal information from children under the age of 13 years old, the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA) puts parents in control. The Federal Trade Commission, United States' consumer protection agency, enforces the COPPA Rule, which spells out what operators of websites and online services must do to protect children's privacy and safety online.

docs.title: We do not specifically market to children under the age of 13 years old.
docs.text:
Fair Information Practices The Fair Information Practices Principles form the backbone of privacy law in the United States and the concepts they include have played a significant role in the development of data protection laws around the globe. Understanding the Fair Information Practice Principles and how they should be implemented is critical to comply with the various privacy laws that protect personal information.

In order to be in line with Fair Information Practices we will take the following responsive action, should a data breach occur: We will notify you via email

  • Within 7 business days

We will notify the users via in-site notification

  • Within 1 business day

We also agree to the Individual Redress Principle which requires that individuals have the right to legally pursue enforceable rights against data collectors and processors who fail to adhere to the law. This principle requires not only that individuals have enforceable rights against data users, but also that individuals have recourse to courts or government agencies to investigate and/or prosecute non-compliance by data processors.

docs.title: CAN SPAM Act
docs.text: The CAN-SPAM Act is a law that sets the rules for commercial email, establishes requirements for commercial messages, gives recipients the right to have emails stopped from being sent to them, and spells out tough penalties for violations.

docs.title: We collect your email address in order to:
docs.text:
• Send information, respond to inquiries, and/or other requests or questions
  • Market to our mailing list or continue to send emails to our clients after the original transaction has occurred.

docs.title: To be in accordance with CANSPAM, we agree to the following:
docs.text:
• Not use false or misleading subjects or email addresses.
  • Identify the message as an advertisement in some reasonable way.
  • Include the physical address of our business or site headquarters.
  • Monitor third-party email marketing services for compliance, if one is used.
  • Honor opt-out/unsubscribe requests quickly.
  • Allow users to unsubscribe by using the link at the bottom of each email.

If at any time you would like to unsubscribe from receiving future emails, follow the instructions at the bottom of each email. and we will promptly remove you from ALL correspondence.

docs.title: Contacting Us
docs.text:
If there are any questions regarding this privacy policy, you may contact us using the information below.

GeniXCMS
- genixcms.web.id
- genixcms@gmail.com
Last Edited on 2024-01-16

docs.title: About GeniXCMS
docs.text:
GeniXCMS is a high-performance, PHP-based Content Management System and Framework (CMSF). It is meticulously designed to be lightweight, secure, and infinitely extensible, serving as both a robust standalone CMS and a flexible application framework.

Whether you are building a simple blog, a corporate portal, or a complex custom web application, GeniXCMS provides the foundational architecture needed to accelerate your development cycle without the overhead of bulkier systems.

docs.title: 💎 Why Choose GeniXCMS?
docs.text:
GeniXCMS is built for developers who value simplicity and control. It acts as a Modern Starter Engine for online applications.

  • Developer-Centric: Perfect for intermediate to advanced PHP developers looking for a clean, non-obfuscated codebase.
  • Built-in Power: Comes pre-equipped with essential modules like User Management, Content Engine (Posts/Pages), Menu Builder, and Theme Options.
  • Intuitive UI: A user-friendly administrative dashboard built on standard modern web technologies.
  • Latte Engine: Uses the industry-standard Latte templating engine for ultra-fast rendering and automated security.

docs.title: 🏗️ Technical Stack
docs.text: Component Technology Role Language PHP 8.1+ Core logic and server-side processing. Templating Latte Safe, contextual escaping and lightning-fast rendering. Front-End Bootstrap 5 Responsive, mobile-first administrative and theme UI. Database MySQL / SQLite / Multi-DB Flexible storage via a modern Database Abstraction Layer. Routing Smart URL SEO-friendly, regex-based URL routing system.

docs.title: 🔗 Project Ecosystem
docs.text:
Stay connected with the core development and explore live demonstrations.

  • 🌐 Project Repository: GeniXCMS on GitHub
  • 🚀 Live Showcase: Experience GeniXCMS Demo
  • 🏢 Developer Site: Official GeniXCMS Website
  • ⚖️ Licensing: Distributed under the permissive MIT License.

[!TIP] Ready to dive in? Check out the Installation Guide to get your first GeniXCMS project up and running in minutes.

docs.title: Credits & Acknowledgements
docs.text: GeniXCMS is built upon the collective intelligence of the global open-source community. We gratefully acknowledge the authors and contributors of the following libraries, frameworks, and tools that have made this project possible.

docs.title: 🎨 Frontend Ecosystem
docs.text:
The administrative dashboard and default themes are powered by these exceptional user interface libraries.

Library Purpose Bootstrap 5 Core responsive CSS framework. AdminLTE High-level administrative dashboard layout. Latte Secure, high-performance templating engine. Summernote Lightweight WYSIWYG editor for content creation. FontAwesome Comprehensive iconography system. jQuery & UI Foundation for DOM manipulation and animations. HighlightJS Elegant syntax highlighting for code blocks. Select2 Enhanced select boxes with searching and tagging.

docs.title: ⚙️ Backend & Infrastructure
docs.text:
The core engine relies on a stable and powerful server-side stack.

  • PHP 8.1+: The primary engine driving all system logic.
  • Composer: Dependency management and class autoloading.
  • SQL Engines: Native support for MySQL, MariaDB, SQLite, and PostgreSQL.
  • Asset Pipeline: Optional integration with Vite for modern bundling.

docs.title: 🔌 Major Dependencies (via Composer)
docs.text:
GeniXCMS integrates these industry-standard packages to provide rich feature sets.

  • Symfony Components: Routing and foundation utilities.
  • GuzzleHttp: Robust HTTP client for API interactions.
  • PHPMailer: Reliable email delivery services.
  • Intervention Image: High-level image manipulation and optimization.
  • elFinder: Integrated file and media management engine.
  • Omnipay: The engine behind the multi-gateway payment system.
  • Google reCAPTCHA: Enhanced security for forms and logins.

docs.title: 🌍 Community Mentors
docs.text:
Finally, we thank the thousands of developers worldwide who contribute to the protocols and standards (HTTP, HTML5, CSS3, JSON) that serve as the bedrock of the modern web.

[!NOTE] Are you an author of a library used in GeniXCMS? If you'd like to be featured or have your link updated, please open an issue on our GitHub repository.

docs.title: Acl Class Documentation
docs.text:
The Acl (Access Control List) class provides the granular authorization engine for GeniXCMS. Unlike the simple numeric levels of User::access(), the ACL system allows for named permission keys that can be toggled on or off for specific user groups via the administrative dashboard.

It enables a highly flexible security model where different roles (Editors, Authors, etc.) can be granted or denied specific capabilities like POSTS_DELETE or THEMES_MANAGE.

docs.title: ⚡ How It Works
docs.text:
Permission checks in GeniXCMS follow a specific order of precedence:

  1. Administrator (Level 0) Override: Users with Level 0 always pass any ACL check, regardless of settings.
  2. Database Override: The system checks the permissions table for an explicit status (Allowed/Denied) for the user's group.
  3. Code-Defined Default: If no database setting exists, the system falls back to the default allowed groups specified when the permission was registered in the code.

docs.title: Acl::check(string $permission)
docs.text:
Performs a permission check for the currently authenticated user.

  • Returns: bool — true if operation is permitted, false otherwise.

docs.title: Acl::register(string $key, string $label, array $default_groups = [0, 1, 2])
docs.text:
Registers a new capability into the system. Modules should use this to add their own security hooks.

Parameter Type Default Description $key string ✅ Unique key (e.g., 'MOD_GALLERY_UPLOAD'). $label string ✅ Human-readable name shown in ACL Manager. $default_groups array [0, 1, 2] Group IDs (0-6) allowed by default.

docs.title: Acl::checkGroup(string $permission, int|string $group)
docs.text:
Checks if a specific user group has a particular permission.

if (Acl::checkGroup('POSTS_DELETE', 2)) {
    echo "Editors can delete posts.";
}

docs.title: Acl::set(int|string $group_id, string $permission, int $status)
docs.text:
Updates or inserts a permission record into the database.

  • $status: 1 for Allow, 0 for Deny.

docs.title: Acl::getAllPermissions()
docs.text: Returns an associative array of all registered permissions and their metadata. Used primarily for rendering the ACL Matrix in the admin panel.

docs.title: 🛡️ Core Permissions Reference
docs.text:
GeniXCMS registers several dozen core permissions by default during the init() phase.

Category Typical Keys Default Access Posts POSTS_VIEW, POSTS_ADD, POSTS_EDIT, POSTS_DELETE Admin, Supervisor, Editor, Author Pages PAGES_VIEW, PAGES_ADD, PAGES_EDIT, PAGES_DELETE Admin, Supervisor, Editor Media MEDIA_VIEW, MEDIA_UPLOAD, MEDIA_DELETE Admin, Supervisor, Editor, Author System SETTINGS_MANAGE, THEMES_MANAGE, MODULES_MANAGE Administrator Only Users USERS_VIEW, USERS_ADD, USERS_EDIT, USERS_DELETE Admin, Supervisor

docs.title: 1. Registration
docs.text:
In your module's init hook or constructor:

Acl::register('BOOKING_CANCEL', 'Cancel customer bookings', [0, 1]);

docs.title: 2. Implementation
docs.text:
Inside your module's controller/action logic:

if (Acl::check('BOOKING_CANCEL')) {
    // Proceed with logic...
} else {
    Control::error('noaccess');
}

docs.title: 3. Masking UI Elements
docs.text:
In your views (Latte templates):

{if Acl::check('BOOKING_CANCEL')}
    <button class="btn btn-danger">Cancel Booking</button>
{/if}

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• User Class — How user levels and groups are defined.
  • ACL Manager Guide — Graphical interface for managing these permissions.
  • Acl Model — Underlying model and table structure.

docs.title: AdminMenu Class API Reference
docs.text:
The AdminMenu class is a programmable registry for the GeniXCMS administrative dashboard. It centralizes all core and module-registered navigation items, allowing developers to inject menu entries without modifying template files.

[!IMPORTANT] Registration Phase: Always register your custom menu items during the init hook. This ensures that all classes are loaded and the system is ready to receive new navigation nodes.

Hooks::attach('init', function(){
    AdminMenu::add([ ... ]);
});

docs.title: AdminMenu::add(array $item): void
docs.text:
Registers a new top-level or standalone admin menu item.

  • $item Keys:
    • id: Unique string identifier (required for parent/child mapping).
    • label: Display text. Use _('Label') for localization.
    • icon: Bootstrap Icons class (e.g., bi bi-box).
    • url: The target link (e.g., index.php?page=my_page).
    • access: Minimum user level (0=Admin ... 6=Member). Default: 6.
    • position: main (Top), management (Middle), settings (Bottom), or external.
    • order: Numeric sort weight. Lower values appear higher. Default: 50.
    • aliases: (Optional) Array of URLs that should also trigger the active state for this item.
    • children: (Optional) Array of child item definitions.

docs.title: AdminMenu::addChild(string $parentId, array $child): void
docs.text:
Injects a single sub-menu item into an existing parent menu.

  • $parentId: The id used when the parent was registered (e.g., posts).
  • $child: Array with label, url, and optionally access and icon.

docs.title: AdminMenu::addChildren(string $parentId, array $children): void
docs.text: Injects multiple sub-menu items at once.

docs.title: AdminMenu::remove(string $id): void
docs.text: Deregisters and removes a menu item from the registry by its ID.

docs.title: AdminMenu::getItems(?string $position = null): array
docs.text: Retrieves registered items, sorted by their order value. - $position: Filter by a specific group (main, management, settings, external). If null, returns all items.

docs.title: AdminMenu::renderSidebar(?string $position = null): string
docs.text:
Generates the HTML <li> structure for the GeniXCMS admin sidebar.

  • Behavior: It automatically checks user access for each item and applies the active class based on clever URL matching logic.
  • Logic: Handles nested nav-tree structures automatically for items with children.

docs.title: AdminMenu::renderTopNav(?string $position = null, bool $nested = false): string
docs.text: Generates Bootstrap-compatible dropdown navigation for the horizontal top bar.

docs.title: 🔍 Internal Logic: Active States
docs.text:
The AdminMenu class uses a precise URL matching engine to highlight the active menu item:

  1. ID Match: If the current $_GET['page'] or $_GET['mod'] matches the menu id.
  2. URL Match: It parses the url and compares parameters (page, mod, view, act, type, sel) with the current request.
  3. Aliases: Supports an aliases array for additional URL patterns that trigger the active state (e.g., matching the "Add" and "Edit" pages for a menu item).
  4. Child Inheritance: A parent menu is automatically marked as active if any of its children are currently viewed.

docs.title: 🛠️ Core Menu Structure
docs.text:
The system automatically boots several core menu groups:

ID Position Label Children posts main Posts All Posts, Categories, Tags pages main Pages - comments main Comments - media main Media - users management Users All Users, ACL Manager themes management Appearance Themes, Menus, Widgets modules management Modules - system management System System Health, Updates devtools management Dev Tools Asset Inspector, Hook Inspector settings settings Settings Global, Media, Multilanguage, etc.

docs.title: 1. Registering a Modular Parent with Children
docs.text:
Ideal for new modules that need their own category in the sidebar.

AdminMenu::add([
    'id'       => 'inventory',
    'label'    => _('Inventory'),
    'icon'     => 'bi bi-box-seam',
    'url'      => 'index.php?page=mods&mod=inventory',
    'access'   => 1, // Supervisor+
    'position' => 'main',
    'order'    => 45,
    'children' => [
        ['label' => _('All Products'), 'url' => 'index.php?page=mods&mod=inventory'],
        ['label' => _('Warehouses'),   'url' => 'index.php?page=mods&mod=inventory&view=stocks'],
        ['label' => _('Suppliers'),    'url' => 'index.php?page=mods&mod=inventory&view=suppliers'],
    ]
]);

docs.title: 2. Injecting into a Core Menu
docs.text:
Use this to add specific tools to existing GeniXCMS categories.

// Add a "SEO Audit" link under the standard "Settings" menu
AdminMenu::addChild('settings', [
    'label'  => _('SEO Audit'),
    'url'    => 'index.php?page=mods&mod=seonix&act=audit',
    'access' => 0, // Admin Only
    'icon'   => 'bi bi-shield-check'
]);

docs.title: 3. Removing Navigation for Specific Roles
docs.text:
You can dynamically hide core features if they are not needed for certain site architectures.

// Hide the "Comments" menu globally if not used
AdminMenu::remove('comments');

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Admin Menu Registration Guide — Step-by-step implementation guide for modules.
  • ACL Manager Guide — Understanding the access level requirements.

docs.title: AJAX Service Classes
docs.text: GeniXCMS 2.3.0 introduces a suite of specialized AJAX service classes located in inc/lib/. These classes power various administrative and builder-related features using a standardized JSON response format.

docs.title: 📡 ApiAjax
docs.text: The ApiAjax class provides high-level data services, primarily used by the Dynamic Builder and frontend widgets to retrieve content without page reloads.

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
• recent_posts(): Returns a JSON list of latest posts.
  • random_posts(): Returns a randomized list of published content.
  • categories(): Retrieves taxonomies for a specific post type.
  • tags(): Provides autocomplete-ready tag suggestions.
  • authors(): Returns a list of users who have published content.

docs.title: 🖼️ SaveimageAjax
docs.text: Handles the server-side processing of images uploaded via the editor or Dynamic Builder.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Automated Resizing: Generates thumbnails based on system settings.
  • Secure Uploads: Validates MIME types and sanitizes filenames.
  • Media Integration: Automatically registers uploaded files into the media table.

docs.title: 🏷️ TagsAjax
docs.text: A dedicated service for managing post tags.

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
• search(): Returns tags matching a search term for use in autocomplete fields.
  • add(): Programmatically creates new tags during post composition.

docs.title: 🔄 VersionAjax
docs.text: Powers the system update checker in the GeniXCMS maintenance dashboard.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Remote Check: Communicates with the GeniXCMS central repository to verify the latest stable release.
  • Changelog Retrieval: Fetches release notes for the available update.

docs.title: 🛒 MarketplaceAjax
docs.text: Integrates the administrative panel with the official GeniXCMS Marketplace.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Discovery: Searches for available themes and modules.
  • Installation: Handles the secure download and extraction of ecosystem extensions.

docs.title: � UpdatesAjax
docs.text: Provides comprehensive system update checking for core, modules, and themes.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Core Updates: Checks GeniXCMS core version against marketplace repository.
  • Module Updates: Scans installed modules and compares versions with available updates.
  • Theme Updates: Verifies theme versions and provides download links for updates.
  • Batch Processing: Efficiently handles multiple update checks in a single request.
  • Go Service Integration: Can proxy to high-performance Go service for faster filesystem scanning.

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: • index(): Performs complete system update check and returns structured JSON response.

docs.title: 👥 UserAjax
docs.text: Handles administrative user management operations.

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
• list_users(): Returns paginated list of users with filtering and search capabilities.
  • Parameters: offset, num, q (search), group, status
  • Features: Role-based filtering, email/username search, user detail joins
  • Go Service: Supports high-performance proxying for large user datasets.

docs.title: �🛠️ Implementation Pattern
docs.text:
All AJAX service classes follow this implementation pattern:

class MyServiceAjax {
    public function my_action($param = null) {
        // 1. Auth Check
        if (!Ajax::auth($param)) return Ajax::error(401, 'Unauthorized');

        // 2. Business Logic
        $data = MyModel::get();

        // 3. Standard Response
        return Ajax::response([
            'status' => 'success',
            'data' => $data
        ]);
    }
}

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:

docs.title: Ajax Class API Reference
docs.text: The Ajax class is the central orchestration engine for handling asynchronous requests in GeniXCMS. It provides a standardized way to dispatch requests to specialized handlers and return consistent JSON responses.

docs.title: Ajax::dispatch(string $resource, string $action = null, mixed $param = null): mixed
docs.text:
The primary entry point for all AJAX requests. It dynamically routes the request to a corresponding handler class or legacy control file.

  • $resource: The name of the resource (e.g., 'nixomers'). The class will look for a class named ucfirst($resource) . 'Ajax' (e.g., NixomersAjax).
  • $action: The method name to call within the handler class. Defaults to index.
  • $param: Optional additional parameters passed from the Router.

Routing Logic:

  1. Checks if a class ResourceAjax exists. If yes, it instantiates it and calls the requested method.
  2. If the class does not exist, it looks for a legacy control file at inc/lib/Control/Ajax/resource-ajax.control.php.
  3. If neither is found, it returns a standardized 404 Not Found error.

docs.title: Ajax::auth(mixed $param = null): bool
docs.text:
Validates the security token for the current request. It checks for a token in the following order:

  1. $_REQUEST['token']
  2. HTTP_X_GX_TOKEN header
  3. Smart URL path (if enabled).

Returns true if the token is valid, false otherwise.

docs.title: Ajax::response(array $data, int $code = 200): void
docs.text:
Sends a standardized JSON response to the client and terminates execution.

  • $data: The associative array to be encoded as JSON.
  • $code: The HTTP response code (default: 200).

Behavior: Automatically sets Content-Type: application/json and uses JSON_PRETTY_PRINT for readability.

docs.title: Ajax::error(int $code, string $message): void
docs.text:
A helper method to send a standardized error response.

{
    "status": "error",
    "code": 404,
    "message": "Resource not found"
}

docs.title: Creating a Custom Handler
docs.text:
To create a new AJAX service, define a class in inc/lib/ or within a module's library:

class AnalyticsAjax {
    public function get_stats($param = null) {
        // 1. Authenticate
        if (!Ajax::auth($param)) {
            return Ajax::error(401, 'Unauthorized');
        }

        // 2. Process Data
        $stats = [
            'views' => 1250,
            'orders' => 42
        ];

        // 3. Send Response
        return Ajax::response([
            'status' => 'success',
            'data' => $stats
        ]);
    }
}

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• PostsAjax Class Reference — The core handler for AJAX dashboards.
  • Ajax Service Classes — Overview of available AJAX services in GeniXCMS.

docs.title: API Engine Class
docs.text: The Api class is the central nervous system for GeniXCMS RESTful API. It provides a robust framework for handling incoming requests, enforcing security through API key validation, and delivering consistent JSON responses to client applications.

docs.title: 🛡️ Authentication Architecture
docs.text:
The API engine supports multi-layered authentication to ensure secure data access.

Method Key / Header Description Header GX-API-KEY Recommended. Pass the key in the HTTP_GX_API_KEY header. Query api_key Optional. Pass as a URL parameter for simple GET requests. Session logged_in Fallback. Uses the current admin session if available.

docs.title: static Api::dispatch($resource, $id, $action)
docs.text:
The entry point for all API requests. It automatically routes the request to the corresponding Resource Controller.

  • Workflow:
  • Inits: Sets headers to application/json.
  • Auth: Validates access permissions.
  • Route: Discovers the {Resource}Api class.
  • Execute: Maps HTTP verbs (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) to class methods.

docs.title: static Api::success($data, $message)
docs.text:
Generates a standardized success payload. Use this for all successful operations to maintain a consistent frontend consumption experience.

{
    "status": "success",
    "message": "Operation successful",
    "data": { "id": 101, "title": "Example Post" }
}

docs.title: static Api::error($code, $message)
docs.text:
Generates a structured error response. Automatically sets the correct HTTP response code (e.g., 404, 401, 500).

{
    "status": "error",
    "code": 401,
    "message": "Unauthorized access [API Key Invalid]"
}

docs.title: 🛠️ Internal Request Flow
docs.text:
The Api class is tightly integrated with the Router system. When a URL prefix matches api/v1/, the router hands off the execution to this class.

// Internal Routing Example
'api/v1/(.*)/(.*)' => [
    'api' => 'v1', 
    'resource' => 1, 
    'identifier' => 2
]

[!IMPORTANT] Extending the API: To create a new endpoint, simply add a new class in inc/lib/Control/Api/ following the ResourceApi naming convention. The engine will handle the rest!

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• RESTful API Overview — Learn about endpoint structures and standard resources.
  • Router Class — Understanding how API URLs are mapped.
  • Security Guidelines — Best practices for protecting your API keys.

docs.title: ApiAjax Class
docs.text: The ApiAjax class provides a set of AJAX endpoints for retrieving system data such as posts, categories, tags, and authors. It is designed to be used by dynamic builders or frontend components that require asynchronous data fetching.

docs.title: index($param = null)
docs.text:
The main entry point for ApiAjax. It dispatches the request to a specific action based on the action parameter in the URL.

  • Endpoint: index.php?ajax=api&action=ACTION_NAME
  • Actions: recent_posts, random_posts, custom_posts, categories, tags, authors.

docs.title: recent_posts()
docs.text:
Retrieves the most recent posts.

  • URL Parameters:
    • num (int): Number of posts to retrieve (default: 5).
    • type (string): Post type (default: 'post').
    • cat (int): Category ID (optional).

docs.title: random_posts()
docs.text:
Retrieves a random set of posts.

  • URL Parameters:
    • num (int): Number of posts to retrieve (default: 5).
    • type (string): Post type (default: 'post').

docs.title: custom_posts()
docs.text:
Retrieves specific posts by their IDs.

  • URL Parameters:
    • ids (string): Comma-separated list of post IDs (e.g., '1,2,3').

docs.title: categories()
docs.text:
Retrieves a list of categories.

  • URL Parameters:
    • type (string): Category type (default: 'post').

docs.title: tags($param = null)
docs.text:
Retrieves a list of tags matching a search term.

  • URL Parameters:
    • term (string): Search term for the tag name.
  • Security: Requires a valid token.

docs.title: authors($param = null)
docs.text:
Retrieves a list of unique authors who have published posts.

  • Security: Requires a valid token.

docs.title: 🛡️ Security & Authentication
docs.text:
• Token Validation: Sensitive actions (tags, authors) require a valid system token passed in the request.
  • XSS Protection: All input parameters are sanitized using Typo::cleanX() or Typo::int().

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Ajax Class — The main AJAX dispatcher.
  • Posts Class — The underlying post retrieval engine.
  • Token Class — Handling security tokens.

docs.title: Archives Class API Reference
docs.text: The Archives class handles the generation, caching, and rendering of time-based content archives (grouped by Month and Year). It features an automated cache synchronization engine that listens to database changes.

docs.title: 🏗️ Lifecycle & Caching
docs.text:
The class is initialized in System::__construct(), where it automatically attaches itself to post lifecycle hooks.

  • Auto-Sync: When a post is added, updated, or deleted via the standard API, the Archives class automatically triggers a cache refresh to ensure the navigation stays accurate.
  • Cache Policy: Archive data is stored as a JSON blob in the options table. It is automatically recalculated if the data is missing or older than 24 hours.

docs.title: Archives::getList(int $max, string $type = 'post'): string
docs.text:
Renders a standardized Bootstrap-ready HTML list of archives.

  • $max: Maximum number of months to display.
  • $type: The content type to fetch (default is post).
  • Return: A <ul> list with links and count badges.

docs.title: Archives::validate(int|string $month, int|string $year): bool
docs.text:
Checks if the database contains any published content for the specific month and year.

  • Use Case: Often used in archivers' per-page controllers to prevent 404 pages or empty results.

docs.title: Archives::generate(string $type = 'post'): void
docs.text:
Checks the health of the archive cache and triggers a refresh if it's stale or empty.

  • Note: This is an idempotent "lazy-load" method called automatically by getList().

docs.title: Archives::doUpdate(string $type): void
docs.text: Forces an immediate recalculation of the archive data from the database.

docs.title: 📋 Database Structure (JSON Cache)
docs.text:
The archive list is stored in the archives_list option with the following structure: 

{
  "post": {
    "2026": {
      "04": 12,
      "03": 8
    },
    "2025": {
      "12": 45
    }
  }
}

docs.title: 💡 Practical Theme Example
docs.text:
To display a sidebar widget with the last 10 months of post archives:

<div class="widget">
    <h3><?= _('Daily Memories') ?></h3>
    <?= Archives::getList(10) ?>
</div>

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Url Class — How the Url::archive() helper creates links for this class.
  • Posts Class — Understanding content types.

docs.title: Asset Class API
docs.text: Asset is the central management class for CSS, JS, and raw HTML assets in GeniXCMS 2.0. It simplifies dependency management, ensures correct loading order via priorities, and provides context-aware filtering to optimize performance.

docs.title: register()
docs.text:
Registers a new asset in the system registry. Registration does not automatically load the asset; it must be enqueued later.

public static function register(
    string $id, 
    string $type, 
    string $src, 
    string $pos = 'footer', 
    array  $deps = [], 
    int    $priority = 20, 
    string $context = 'admin'
)
Parameter Type Default Description $id string Required A unique identifier for the asset (e.g., 'jquery', 'my-module-js'). $type string Required The type of asset: 'js', 'css', or 'raw'. $src string Required The URL to the asset file. For type 'raw', this is the actual HTML content. $pos string 'footer' Where to render the asset: 'header' or 'footer'. $deps array [] List of asset IDs that must be loaded before this asset. $priority int 20 Loading order. Lower numbers load earlier. $context string 'admin' Where the asset is allowed: 'admin', 'frontend', or 'all'.

docs.title: enqueue()
docs.text:
Adds a registered asset to the queue for the current page request. 

public static function enqueue(string|array $id)
  • Dependency Resolution: When you enqueue an asset, all its dependencies are automatically enqueued.
  • Priority Sync: If a dependency has a higher priority number (loads later) than the asset requiring it, the system automatically lowers the dependency's priority number to ensuring it loads first.
  • Context Validation: Enqueueing will be ignored if the asset's context does not match the current environment (checked via the GX_ADMIN constant).

docs.title: dequeue()
docs.text:
NEW in v2.0.1 — Removes a previously registered or enqueued asset from the loading queue. Useful for removing conflicting default assets without modifying core files.

public static function dequeue(string $id) : bool
Parameter Type Description $id string The unique asset identifier to remove from the queue

Returns: bool - true if asset was successfully removed, false if asset not found

When to Use:

  1. Remove Bootstrap CSS from frontend (when using TailwindCSS or alternative framework) 

    // In your theme's function.php
if (!GX_ADMIN) {
    Asset::dequeue('bootstrap-css');
}

  2. Replace bundled library with different version 

    Asset::dequeue('jquery');
Asset::load('jquery-custom', 'js', '/assets/vendor/jquery-3.6.4.min.js', 'footer', [], 10, 'all');

  3. Conditional asset removal based on context 

    // Remove analytics on homepage for faster load
if ($current_page_type === 'homepage') {
    Asset::dequeue('analytics-script');
}

Key Features: - Safe: Returns false if asset doesn't exist (no errors) - Works with dependencies: Removes asset even if other assets depend on it - Can dequeue before or after enqueue (order doesn't matter) - Theme-friendly: Recommended over modifying core asset registration

docs.title: load()
docs.text:
A shorthand method that registers and enqueues an asset in a single call.

public static function load(
    string $id, 
    string $type = null, 
    string $src = null, 
    string $pos = 'footer', 
    array  $deps = [], 
    int    $priority = 20, 
    string $context = 'admin'
)
Note: If only $id is provided, it behaves exactly like enqueue($id) for an already registered asset.

docs.title: get()
docs.text:
Returns the rendered HTML string for all queued assets at a specific position.

public static function get(string $pos = 'header') : string
- Used by: Site::loadLibHeader() and Site::loadLibFooter(). - Deduplication: This method tracks rendered assets internally to prevent duplicate output if called multiple times in one request.

docs.title: render()
docs.text:
Directly echoes the result of get($pos).

public static function render(string $pos)

docs.title: getRegistry()
docs.text:
NEW in v2.4.0 — Returns the full asset registry (all registered assets).

public static function getRegistry() : array

Returns an associative array [id => ['type', 'src', 'pos', 'deps', 'priority', 'context']]. Useful for developer tooling and debugging.

docs.title: getQueue()
docs.text:
NEW in v2.4.0 — Returns the current enqueue queue for header and footer.

public static function getQueue() : array

Returns ['header' => [id => priority, ...], 'footer' => [id => priority, ...]].

docs.title: js
docs.text: Renders a <script src="..."> tag.

docs.title: css
docs.text: Renders a <link rel="stylesheet" href="..."> tag.

docs.title: raw
docs.text: Renders the literal string provided in the $src parameter. Useful for inline scripts, styles, or SVG blocks.

docs.title: 1. Registering with Dependencies
docs.text:
Ensures that your module's script only loads after jQuery.

Asset::register(
    'my-mod-script', 
    'js', 
    Site::$url . '/inc/mod/my-mod/assets/app.js', 
    'footer', 
    ['jquery'], // Dependency
    50
);

docs.title: 2. Context-Aware Loading
docs.text:
Register an asset that should only appear on the frontend, even if enqueued from a core class.

Asset::register(
    'theme-styles', 
    'css', 
    Url::theme() . '/style.css', 
    'header', 
    [], 
    10, 
    'frontend' // Context
);

docs.title: 3. Rendering Inline Scripts (Raw Type)
docs.text:
Useful for passing PHP variables to JavaScript.

$config = json_encode(['api_url' => Url::api('v1')]);
Asset::register('js-config', 'raw', "<script>var GxConfig = {$config};</script>", 'header', [], 5);
Asset::enqueue('js-config');

docs.title: Context-Aware Asset Loading (v2.0.1+)
docs.text: Starting with GeniXCMS 2.0.1, Bootstrap CSS is no longer loaded on the frontend by default. This allows modern themes using TailwindCSS or other frameworks to avoid CSS conflicts.

docs.title: Bootstrap CSS Loading Behavior
docs.text: Context Loaded? Reason Admin (GX_ADMIN = true) ✅ Yes Required for admin interface dropdowns and forms Frontend (GX_ADMIN = false) ❌ No Avoid conflicts with modern CSS frameworks

docs.title: Opting In to Bootstrap on Frontend
docs.text:
If your theme uses Bootstrap, explicitly enqueue it:

// In inc/themes/mytheme/function.php

if (!GX_ADMIN) {
    // Your theme uses Bootstrap on frontend
    Asset::enqueue('bootstrap-css');
    Asset::enqueue('bootstrap-js');
}

docs.title: Pre-defined Core Assets
docs.text:
GeniXCMS registers several core assets by default during Asset::init().

ID Context Position Loaded by Default? jquery all header ✅ Yes bootstrap-css admin header ✅ Yes (admin only) bootstrap-js admin footer ✅ Yes (admin only) bootstrap-icons all header ✅ Yes fontawesome all header ✅ Yes inter-font all header ✅ Yes chartjs admin header Registered, enqueued on demand gx-toast-js all footer ✅ Yes

[!NOTE] Offline Mode: When OFFLINE_MODE=true in config.php, all CDN URLs above are automatically replaced with local files from assets/js/vendor/, assets/css/vendor/, and assets/fonts/. No code changes needed — just flip the constant.

[!TIP] Pro Tip: Use dequeue() in your theme to cleanly remove conflicting assets instead of modifying core configuration files. This keeps your theme maintainable across version updates.

// In your theme's function.php
if (!GX_ADMIN) {
    Asset::dequeue('bootstrap-css'); // Clean removal
}

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Asset Management Guide
  • How to Use Hooks
  • Vite Integration

docs.title: Backend Class
docs.text:
# filename: Backend.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 2.0.0
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

Info

This class is currently a placeholder and is still under development.

The Backend class is reserved for future use as an administrative utility class within GeniXCMS. In the current version, it is an empty shell that will eventually house backend-specific helper methods that don't belong in a controller.

docs.title: Current State
docs.text:
class Backend
{
    public function __construct(){
        // no initialization yet
    }
}

Warning

This class currently contains no implemented methods. Any future backend-specific functionality that needs to be shared across multiple admin controllers will be implemented here.

docs.title: Intended Purpose
docs.text: Based on the system architecture, this class is expected to handle: - Shared admin authentication checks. - Common menu and breadcrumb generation for the admin panel. - Permission verification helpers.

docs.title: BaseControl Class API Reference
docs.text: The BaseControl is the abstract foundation for all frontend controllers in GeniXCMS. it centralizes the initialization of the Latte Template Engine, manages common view data, and provides standardized rendering methods.

docs.title: 🏗️ Core Architecture
docs.text: This class cannot be instantiated directly. Every frontend controller (for Posts, Pages, Search, etc.) must extend BaseControl.

docs.title: Primary Properties
docs.text:
• $latte: Instance of the Latte\Engine.
  • $data: Global state array passed to all templates.
  • $db, $user, $system: Core system objects injected via the Container.

docs.title: render(string $view, array $viewData = []): void
docs.text:
The primary method to output a page to the browser.

  1. Merges Data: Combines $this->data with local $viewData.
  2. Meta Generation: Automatically generates SEO metadata via Site::meta().
  3. Orchestration: Renders the Header, the Main View, and the Footer sequentially.
  4. Layout Detection: If layout is defined in $viewData, it searches for partials using the pattern header-$layout.latte and footer-$layout.latte.

docs.title: initLatte(): void
docs.text:
Initializes the Latte engine with GeniXCMS-specific configurations:

  • Extensions: RawPhpExtension and a custom TranslatorExtension linked to Typo::translate().
  • Caching: Temp directory is set to GX_CACHE . '/temp'.
  • Custom Filters: Includes nl2br and stripHtml (which also strips GeniXCMS shortcodes).

docs.title: initCommonData(): void
docs.text:
Automatically populates the $data array with essential site information:

  • site_name, site_url, site_cdn.
  • site_logo (processed with dynamic height from options).
  • token (for frontend forms).
  • tag_cloud and archives_list.

docs.title: 🧭 Global Data Contract
docs.text:
When you extend BaseControl, your templates automatically have access to:

Variable Description $site_name The website title. $theme_url Full URL to the active theme directory. $website_lang 2-character language code (e.g., en). $site_meta Fully rendered SEO <meta> and <title> tags. $archives_list Pre-rendered HTML list of archives.

docs.title: 💡 Implementation Example
docs.text:
class MyCustomController extends BaseControl
{
    public function index()
    {
        // Add custom data
        $my_data = ['title' => 'Special Page'];

        // Render 'custom.latte' with 'dashboard' layout
        // Looks for: header-dashboard.latte, custom.latte, footer-dashboard.latte
        $this->render('custom', [
            'layout' => 'dashboard',
            'custom_val' => 'Hello World'
        ]);
    }
}

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Control Layer — The overall MVC architecture.
  • Theme Class — How themes are managed in GeniXCMS.

docs.title: Cache Class
docs.text: The Cache class provides page-level output caching to improve website performance. it supports both file-based and Redis-backed caching strategies.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Caching is configured via the system settings in the admin dashboard:

  • cache_enabled: Set to 'on' to enable the cache.
  • cache_type: 'file' (default) or 'redis'.
  • cache_path: Directory for file-based cache.
  • cache_timeout: TTL for cached items in seconds (default: 3600).
  • redis_*: Connection details for Redis (host, port, pass, db).

docs.title: Start Method
docs.text:
Usage: Cache::start();

Call this method at the very beginning of the page execution (before any output). It:

  1. Checks if a valid cached version of the current URL exists.
  2. If found, it outputs the cache content and terminates the script immediately.
  3. If not found, it starts output buffering (ob_start).

docs.title: End Method
docs.text:
Usage: Cache::end();

Call this method at the very end of the page execution. It:

  1. Captures the buffered output content.
  2. Stores the content in the cache (file or Redis) with the configured timeout.
  3. Flushes the buffer to the browser.

docs.title: Logic
docs.text: The cache key is uniquely generated based on the current URL (host + request URI + query string) using an MD5 hash. This ensures that different pages and different query parameters result in separate cache entries.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• File Cache: Requires write permissions to the configured cache_path.
  • Redis Cache: Requires the php-redis extension to be installed and enabled on the server.

docs.title: Categories Class
docs.text: The Categories class manages content taxonomies, supporting hierarchical structures (parent/child) and different taxonomy types (Standard Categories, Tags, etc.).

docs.title: name()
docs.text: Retrieves the human-readable name of a category. echo Categories::name(int $id);

docs.title: id()
docs.text: Finds a category ID based on its name or slug. $id = Categories::id(string $slug);

docs.title: slug()
docs.text: Retrieves the unique slug for a given category ID. $slug = Categories::slug(int $id);

docs.title: delete()
docs.text: Removes a category. Associated posts are automatically re-assigned to the parent category (or root if no parent exists). Categories::delete(int $id);

docs.title: dropdown()
docs.text:
Renders a Bootstrap-compatible HTML <select> menu. Supports hierarchical indentation for child categories. 

echo Categories::dropdown([
    'name'     => 'post_category',
    'selected' => 2,
    'type'     => 'post',   // Filter by taxonomy type
    'class'    => 'form-select rounded-3'
]);

docs.title: lists()
docs.text: Renders a hierarchical Bootstrap Accordion list showing parent categories and their sub-items. echo Categories::lists(['type' => 'post']);

docs.title: Type Customization
docs.text: Similar to Posts, you can define custom labels for different taxonomy types.

docs.title: setTypeLabel()
docs.text:
Categories::setTypeLabel('product_cat', [
    'new_item'   => 'New Collection',
    'name_label' => 'Collection Name'
]);

docs.title: getParent()
docs.text: Retrieves the parent category record for a specific ID. $parent = Categories::getParent(int $id);

docs.title: exist()
docs.text: Checks if a category exists in the database. if (Categories::exist(5)) { ... }

docs.title: Comments Class
docs.text: The Comments class manages the reader feedback system in GeniXCMS. It handles comment submission, moderation, spam protection, and threaded rendering for posts and pages.

docs.title: 🚀 Quick Start (Frontend)
docs.text:
To display the comment system on a post or page template, use the following methods:

// 1. Display the count of comments
echo Db::$num_rows; // After showList() is called

// 2. Display the list of comments
echo Comments::showList(['max' => 10]);

// 3. Display the comment submission form
echo Comments::form();

docs.title: Comments::form()
docs.text:
Renders the complete HTML comment form (Bootstrap-based).

  • Features:
    • Automatically pre-fills name/email for logged-in users.
    • Integrates Google reCAPTCHA if enabled.
    • Includes client-side validaton via Comments::validateJsComment().
    • Supports parent-child threading via a "Reply" button that sets a hidden parent ID.

docs.title: Comments::showList(array $vars)
docs.text:
Renders the threaded comment list and pagination for the current post.

Parameter Type Required Description $vars['max'] int ✅ The number of top-level comments to show per page.

Example: echo Comments::showList(['max' => 5]);

docs.title: Comments::recent(array $vars)
docs.text:
Fetches a list of recent comments across the site.

Key Type Default Description num int 10 Number of comments to fetch. type string 'post' The post type to filter (e.g., 'post', 'page'). post_id int null Optional, filter by a specific post.

docs.title: Comments::addComment(array $vars)
docs.text:
Processes the submission of a new comment (typically from $_POST).

  • Validation:
    • CSRF Protection (using Token).
    • Spam Word filtering.
    • Rate Limiting (default 60s delay between comments).
    • Captcha verification (if enabled).
  • Default Status: Logged-in users' comments are published automatically (status=1), while guests' comments are set to pending (status=2).

docs.title: 🛡️ Spam Protection & Security
docs.text:
The class includes three primary lines of defense:

  1. Bad Words Filter: Scans content against a list of ~300+ spam phrases (see Comments::spamWord()).
  2. Rate Limiting: Users are prevented from posting more than one comment every 60 seconds (configurable via Comments::checkLastComment($delay)).
  3. Captchas: Supports Google v2 ReCaptcha via the Xaptcha class integration.

docs.title: 📂 Administrative Methods
docs.text:
These methods are typically used in the backend (inc/lib/Control/Backend/comments.control.php):

Method Description Comments::publish($id) Set status to 1 (Published). Comments::unpublish($id) Set status to 0 (Unpublished/Spam). Comments::pending($id) Set status to 2 (Pending Moderation). Comments::delete($id) Permanently removes a comment record. Comments::isEnable() Returns true if comments_enable is 'on' in site options.

docs.title: 📐 Database Schema (comments table)
docs.text: Field Description userid Unique ID if the commenter is logged in. name Display name. email Contact email (gravatar compatible). comment Sanitized HTML content. parent The ID of the parent comment (0 for top-level). status 0=Unpublished/Spam, 1=Published, 2=Pending.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Posts Class — How comments relate to content.
  • Xaptcha Class — Anti-spam captcha engine.
  • Options Class — Comment system configuration.

docs.title: CommentsAjax Class
docs.text: The CommentsAjax class provides asynchronous access to the system's comments. It is primarily used to populate the administrative dashboard's comment management table with advanced filtering and search capabilities.

docs.title: list_comments($param = null)
docs.text:
Retrieves a paginated list of comments based on the provided query parameters.

  • URL Parameters:
    • num (int): Number of comments per page (default: 10).
    • offset (int): Number of comments to skip (default: 0).
    • q (string): Search term to filter by comment text, name, email, or IP address.
    • status (int): Filter by comment status (0 for pending, 1 for approved).
    • post_id (int): Filter comments for a specific post.

Response Structure:

  • status: 'success' or 'error'.
  • headers: An array of column headers for the dashboard table.
  • data: An array of comment row objects processed via Comments::getDashboardRow().
  • total: Total number of matching comments.
  • limit: The current page size.
  • offset: The current starting offset.

docs.title: 🛡️ Security & Authentication
docs.text:
• Authentication: Requires a valid system token passed in the request. The class calls Ajax::auth($param) internally.
  • Unauthorized Access: Returns a 401 Unauthorized error if the token is invalid or missing.
  • XSS Protection: All query parameters are sanitized using Typo::int() and Typo::cleanX().

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Ajax Class — The main AJAX dispatcher.
  • Comments Class — The core comment management engine.
  • Token Class — Handling security tokens.

docs.title: Dependency Injection Container
docs.text:
The Container class is a lightweight Dependency Injection (DI) Container in v2.0.0. It provides a central registry for core GeniXCMS services, decouping them from global states and hardcoded static calls.

This architecture transition enables easier unit testing, service mocking, and improved maintainability of the codebase.

docs.title: ⚡ Core Principles
docs.text:
GeniXCMS uses a Service Registry pattern:

  1. Centralization: All major services (Database, Session, User, etc.) are registered during system initialization.
  2. Decoupling: Classes do not need to know how to instantiate their dependencies; they just request them from the Container.
  3. Singleton Nature: Each service key typically points to a persistent instance for the duration of the request.

docs.title: 🏗️ Initialization & Lifecycle
docs.text:
Core services are registered by the System class constructor at the very beginning of the application lifecycle:

// inc/lib/System.class.php
Container::set('db', new Db());
Container::set('hooks', new Hooks());
Container::set('user', new User());
// ... and more

docs.title: Registered Core Services
docs.text: Key Class Description db Db PDO-based database connectivity. hooks Hooks System-wide action and filter registry. user User Authentication and role-based access engine. session Session Encapsulated session management. options Options Site configuration manager. http Http Request and response abstraction. router Router URL scraping and routing logic. language Language Localization and i18n support.

docs.title: 1. Basic Retrieval
docs.text:
You can fetch any service instance manually if you are working outside the standard controller flow:

// Manual retrieval
$db = Container::get('db');
$rows = $db->query("SELECT * FROM `posts`")->result();

docs.title: 2. Static Shortcut Methods
docs.text:
For cleaner code, the Container provides static helpers for the most frequently used services:

$db    = Container::db();
$user  = Container::user();
$hooks = Container::hooks();

docs.title: 3. Usage in Controllers (Preferred)
docs.text:
All controllers extending BaseControl are automatically injected with these services via the constructor. Avoid using static class calls inside controllers and instead use internal properties:

class MyControl extends BaseControl
{
    public function run($param)
    {
        // ❌ BAD: Static call (hard to test)
        $posts = Posts::fetch(); 

        // ✅ GOOD: Using injected service property
        if ($this->user->isLoggedin()) {
            $data = $this->db->query("...")->fetch();
        }
    }
}

docs.title: Container::set(string $key, object $instance)
docs.text:
Maps a service key to an object instance. If the key already exists, it will be overridden.

Container::set('mailer', new MyCustomMailer());

docs.title: Container::get(string $key)
docs.text: Retrieves the object associated with the key. Returns null if not found.

docs.title: 🧪 Benefits for Testing
docs.text:
The primary advantage of the Container is the ability to swap real services with Mocks during unit tests:

// In a test environment
$mockDb = $this->createMock(Db::class);
$mockDb->method('query')->willReturn(...);

// Inject the mock into the container
Container::set('db', $mockDb);

// Now, any code calling Container::db() will receive the mock!

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• BaseControl Class — How core services are injected into controllers.
  • System Class — The location where services are initialized.
  • Db Class — Reference for the core database service.

docs.title: Control Layer Reference
docs.text: The Control Layer is the collection of individual controller files stored in inc/lib/Control/. These files are not called directly — they are loaded by the Control class dispatcher based on the current URL or admin page selection.

docs.title: Directory Structure
docs.text:
inc/lib/Control/
├── Frontend/     — Public-facing page controllers (class-based, extend BaseControl)
├── Backend/      — Admin dashboard controllers (procedural)
├── Ajax/         — JSON API / AJAX endpoint controllers
├── Api/          — RESTful Resource controllers (class-based)
├── Error/        — Error page handlers
└── Install/      — Installation wizard controller

docs.title: Frontend Controllers
docs.text:
All frontend controllers are class-based and extend BaseControl, which provides the Latte templating engine and common data. Each file instantiates its class and calls run($param) at the bottom.

How they are called: 

// GxMain calls:
Control::frontend();
// Which calls:
Control::incFront('post', $param);
// Which includes:
inc/lib/Control/Frontend/post.control.php

File Class URL Pattern Description default.control.php DefaultControl / or /paging/N/ Homepage with paginated post listing post.control.php PostControl /slug.html Single post or page detail view page.control.php PageControl /slug.html (type=page) Static page with optional module support cat.control.php CatControl /category/ID/slug/ Category archive listing tag.control.php TagControl /tag/slug/ Tag archive listing author.control.php AuthorControl /author/username/ Author post archive archive.control.php ArchiveControl /YYYY/MM/ Date-based monthly archive search.control.php SearchControl /search/ Full-text search results login.control.php LoginControl /login/ User login form and processing register.control.php (procedural) /register/ User self-registration with email verification and account activation forgotpass.control.php (procedural) /forgotpass/ Password reset — generates new password and emails it to user logout.control.php (procedural) /logout/ Destroy session and redirect to home mod.control.php ModControl /mod/MODULE_NAME.html Custom module frontend page loader (extends BaseControl) rss.control.php (procedural) /rss/ RSS feed XML output sitemap.control.php (procedural) /sitemap.xml XML sitemap output thumb.control.php (procedural) /thumb/type/SIZE/ALIGN/file Dynamic image thumbnail via Image::thumbFly

docs.title: Standard Frontend Controller Pattern
docs.text:
class PostControl extends BaseControl
{
    public function run($param)
    {
        // 1. Extract URL parameters
        $data = Router::scrap($param);

        // 2. Fetch & prepare content
        $posts = Posts::fetch(['id' => $post_id]);
        $data['posts'] = Posts::prepare($posts);

        // 3. Trigger hooks
        $data['content'] = Hooks::run('post_content_before_action', $data);
        $data['content'] .= Posts::content($post->content);

        // 4. Render through BaseControl (header + view + footer via Latte)
        $this->render('single', $data);

        // 5. Track page view
        Stats::addViews($post_id);
    }
}

// Instantiate and run at end of file:
$control = new PostControl();
$control->run($param);

docs.title: Backend Controllers
docs.text:
Backend controllers are procedural files with no class definition. They are loaded by Control::incBack(). All operations are protected by User::access(level).

How they are called: 

// Admin URL: /admin/index.php?page=posts
Control::backend();
// Which calls:
Control::incBack('posts');
// Which includes:
inc/lib/Control/Backend/posts.control.php

File ?page= Value Min Access Level Description default.control.php (dashboard) 1 Main admin dashboard with stats posts.control.php posts 3 Blog post CRUD (add, edit, delete, bulk actions) pages.control.php pages 3 Static page CRUD categories.control.php categories 1 Post category add/edit/delete tags.control.php tags 1 Tag management comments.control.php comments 1 Comment moderation menus.control.php menus 1 Navigation menu builder modules.control.php modules 1 Module listing and install/uninstall mods.control.php mod 1 Individual module backend page loader media.control.php media 1 Media file browser (elFinder) themes.control.php themes 1 Theme listing and activation users.control.php users 1 User management (CRUD, roles) widgets.control.php widgets 1 Widget placement management settings.control.php settings 1 General site settings settings-cache.control.php settings-cache 1 File/Redis cache settings settings-comments.control.php settings-comments 1 Comment system settings settings-media.control.php settings-media 1 Image, watermark, and media settings settings-multilang.control.php settings-multilang 1 Multi-language configuration settings-permalink.control.php settings-permalink 1 Permalink / URL structure settings permissions.control.php permissions 0 User role and access permission settings

docs.title: User Access Levels
docs.text: Level Role Notes 0 Administrator Full access — including system settings, themes, modules, and permissions 1 Supervisor Site-wide management (all backend areas except system-level settings) 2 Editor Content management and moderation 3 Author Manage their own content only 4 Contributor Post creation (review required before publish) 6 General Member Default site member — frontend access only

docs.title: Standard Backend Controller Pattern
docs.text:
if (User::access(1)) {
    // Sanitize inputs
    $name = Typo::cleanX($_POST['name']);
    $token = Typo::cleanX($_POST['token']);

    // Validate CSRF token
    if (!Token::validate($token)) {
        $alertDanger[] = _("Token not exist or expired.");
    }

    // Validate required fields
    if (empty($name)) {
        $alertDanger[] = _("Name cannot be empty.");
    }

    if (!isset($alertDanger)) {
        // Database operation
        Db::insert("INSERT INTO `table` VALUES (...)");
        $data['alertSuccess'][] = _("Saved successfully.");
        Token::remove($token);
    } else {
        $data['alertDanger'] = $alertDanger;
    }

    // Render admin template
    Theme::admin('header', $data);
    System::inc('view_name', $data);
    Theme::admin('footer');
} else {
    Theme::admin('header');
    Control::error('noaccess');
    Theme::admin('footer');
}

docs.title: Ajax Controllers
docs.text:
Ajax controllers are procedural files that return JSON responses. They are loaded by Control::ajax() when the URL matches /ajax/{endpoint}/{token}.

How they are called: 

// URL: /ajax/api/TOKEN
Control::ajax('api', $param);
// Which includes:
inc/lib/Control/Ajax/api-ajax.control.php

File Endpoint Token Required Description api-ajax.control.php api Optional (per action) REST-like CMS data API (posts, categories, tags) elfinder-ajax.control.php elfinder Yes elFinder file manager backend saveimage-ajax.control.php saveimage Yes Saves images pasted into the WYSIWYG editor tags-ajax.control.php tags Yes Tag autocomplete suggestions version-ajax.control.php version Yes Checks for latest GeniXCMS version

docs.title: Public API Endpoints (api-ajax)
docs.text:
Some actions are publicly accessible without a token:

Action (?action=) Token Parameters Returns recent_posts Not required num, type, cat Array of post objects with image, excerpt categories Not required type Array of category objects tags Required term Array of matching tag objects

Example Request: 

GET /ajax/api/TOKEN?action=recent_posts&num=5

Example Response: 

{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "id": 42,
            "title": "Hello World",
            "url": "https://example.com/hello-world.html",
            "image": "https://example.com/thumb/type/square/size/300/...",
            "excerpt": "Short preview..."
        }
    ]
}

docs.title: API Controllers (REST Resource)
docs.text:
API controllers are class-based files located in inc/lib/Control/Api/. These controllers follow a standard RESTful architecture, mapping HTTP verbs to specific class methods. They are dispatched by the Api class.

How they are called: 

// URL: /api/v1/posts/123 (GET)
Control::api('posts', '123'); 
// Which triggers Api::dispatch('posts', '123') 
// and calls PostsApi->index('123')

File Class Endpoint Methods Support PostsApi.class.php PostsApi /api/v1/posts GET, POST, PUT, DELETE PagesApi.class.php PagesApi /api/v1/pages GET, POST, PUT, DELETE

docs.title: Standard API Controller Pattern
docs.text:
All methods return a JSON response via Api::success() or Api::error().

class PostsApi
{
    /**
     * Maps to GET /api/v1/posts/ or GET /api/v1/posts/{id}
     */
    public function index($id = null)
    {
        if ($id) {
            $post = Posts::find($id);
            return $post ? Api::success($post) : Api::error(404, 'Post not found');
        }
        $posts = Posts::all();
        return Api::success($posts);
    }

    /**
     * Maps to POST /api/v1/posts/
     */
    public function submit()
    {
        $data = json_decode(file_get_contents('php://input'), true);
        $res = Posts::create($data);
        return $res ? Api::success(['id' => Db::$last_id]) : Api::error(500, 'Failed creation');
    }

    /**
     * Maps to PUT or PATCH /api/v1/posts/{id}
     */
    public function update($id)
    {
        $post = Posts::find($id);
        if (!$post) return Api::error(404, 'Post not found');

        $data = json_decode(file_get_contents('php://input'), true);
        return $post->save($data) ? Api::success(null, 'Updated') : Api::error(500, 'Failed');
    }

    /**
     * Maps to DELETE /api/v1/posts/{id}
     */
    public function delete($id)
    {
        $post = Posts::find($id);
        return $post && $post->destroy() ? Api::success(null, 'Deleted') : Api::error(500, 'Failed');
    }
}

docs.title: Error Controllers
docs.text:
Error controllers are simple include files at inc/lib/Control/Error/ triggered by Control::error($code).

File Code Description 404.control.php 404 Page not found noaccess.control.php noaccess Insufficient user permission unknown.control.php (default) Generic unknown error

docs.title: Control Class
docs.text:
The Control class is the central dispatcher of GeniXCMS. It acts as the single entry point that reads the current URL, determines the appropriate area (frontend, backend, ajax, error, or install), and includes the corresponding controller file from the inc/lib/Control/ directory.

Important

Control does not implement business logic itself. It is purely a router/dispatcher that delegates execution to the correct controller file.

docs.title: Application Bootstrap Flow
docs.text:
index.php
  └── GxMain::frontend()
        └── Control::frontend()
              ├── Control::route()       [Smart URL mode]
              │     └── Control::incFront('post', $param)
              └── Control::get()         [Non-Smart URL mode]
                    └── Control::incFront('post')

docs.title: Frontend Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::frontend();

Main dispatcher for all public-facing pages. It determines the routing mode (Smart URL vs. query string) and delegates to either route() or get().

  • Smart URL mode: Uses Router::run() to parse the URI.
  • Non-Smart URL mode: Reads from $_GET parameters directly.

docs.title: Route Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::route(array $allowed);

Used in Smart URL mode. Calls Router::run() to match the current URI, then calls incFront() with the matched controller name and parameters. Falls back to error('404') on no match.

docs.title: Get Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::get(array $allowed);

Used in non-Smart URL mode. Iterates over $_GET parameters to find a matching controller key from the $allowed list and calls incFront().

docs.title: IncFront Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::incFront(string $controller, array $param = '');

Includes a file from inc/lib/Control/Frontend/{$controller}.control.php. Falls back to error('404') if the file does not exist.

// Internally called as:
Control::incFront('post', $param);
// Includes: inc/lib/Control/Frontend/post.control.php

docs.title: Backend Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::backend();

Dispatcher for the admin dashboard. Reads $_GET['page'] and includes the matching file from inc/lib/Control/Backend/. Defaults to the dashboard (default.control.php) if no page is specified.

// URL: /admin/index.php?page=posts
// Includes: inc/lib/Control/Backend/posts.control.php

docs.title: IncBack Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::incBack(string $controller);

Includes a file from inc/lib/Control/Backend/{$controller}.control.php. Falls back to error('404', 'File Not Found') if the file does not exist.

docs.title: Ajax Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::ajax(string $endpoint, array $param = '');

Dispatches the request to the Ajax dispatcher. It identifies the target Ajax resource and its associated action.

// URL: /ajax/api/TOKEN?action=search
// Dispatches to: Ajax::dispatch('api', 'search', $param)

docs.title: Api Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::api(string $resource, string $id = '', string $action = '');

Dispatches the request to the Api dispatcher. This is used for RESTful API requests.

// URL: /api/v1/posts/123/edit
// Dispatches to: Api::dispatch('posts', '123', 'edit')

docs.title: Error Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::error(string $code = '', mixed $val = '');

Includes a file from inc/lib/Control/Error/{$code}.control.php. Common codes: 400, 403, 404, 500, db, noaccess. If the specified error file does not exist, it falls back to unknown.control.php.

// Show 404 page
Control::error('404');

// Show access denied page
Control::error('noaccess');

docs.title: Install Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::install();

Includes the installation wizard controller at inc/lib/Control/Install/default.control.php.

docs.title: Allowed Frontend Routes
docs.text:
The frontend() method allows the following route keys:

['api', 'ajax', 'post', 'page', 'cat', 'mod', 'sitemap', 'rss',
 'account', 'search', 'author', 'tag', 'thumb', 'default',
 'login', 'register', 'forgotpass', 'logout', 'archive', 'user']

docs.title: ⚠️ Deprecated Methods
docs.text: • handler($vars): This was the original loader for controllers but is no longer necessary. It is planned for removal in future updates.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• BaseControl — Abstract class extended by all frontend controllers.
  • Router — URL pattern matching, used by Control::route().
  • Ajax — The centralized AJAX dispatcher.
  • Api — The centralized API dispatcher.
  • Control Layer Reference — Full documentation of all individual controller files.

docs.title: Cron Class
docs.text: The Cron class provides a virtual cron system for GeniXCMS, allowing you to schedule tasks (hooks) to be executed at specific times or intervals. It is designed to work similarly to WP-Cron.

docs.title: ⚡ How It Works
docs.text:
GeniXCMS Cron is a "virtual" cron. It does not require a system-level crontab to function (although you can trigger it via a real cron for better reliability). Instead, it checks for due tasks on every page load.

When a task is due, the class triggers the associated Hook with the provided arguments.

docs.title: Cron::schedule(int $timestamp, string $recurrence, string $hook, array $args = [])
docs.text:
Schedules a recurring event.

Parameter Type Required Description $timestamp int ✅ Unix timestamp of the first run. $recurrence string ✅ 'hourly', 'twicedaily', 'daily', or 'weekly'. $hook string ✅ The action hook to trigger. $args array ❌ Arguments to pass to the hook.

docs.title: Cron::scheduleOnce(int $timestamp, string $hook, array $args = [])
docs.text:
Schedules a one-time event.

Cron::scheduleOnce(time() + 3600, 'my_one_time_action', ['id' => 123]);

docs.title: Cron::unschedule(int $timestamp, string $hook, array $args = [])
docs.text: Unschedules a specific event. The arguments must match exactly what was used during scheduling.

docs.title: Cron::clear(string $hook)
docs.text:
Clears ALL scheduled events for a specific hook.

Cron::clear('my_module_sync');

docs.title: Cron::getSchedules()
docs.text: Returns an array of available recurrence schedules and their intervals in seconds.

docs.title: 📅 Default Schedules
docs.text:
