  • Db Class — How the Paging engine queries the record volume.

docs.title: Params Class API Reference
docs.text: The Params class is a programmable registry for extending Post and Page forms with custom metadata fields. It leverages the global UiBuilder engine to ensure consistent visual aesthetics across the GeniXCMS admin panel.

docs.title: Params::register(array $vars): void
docs.text: Registers custom fields to the global params registry. Definitions are merged recursively.

docs.title: Params::render(string $location): void
docs.text: Automated rendering engine for registered parameters. - Integrated with UiBuilder for premium styling. - Automatically handles data persistence and value hydration. - Supports post_type filtering to show fields only on specific modules.

docs.title: ⚙️ Supported Field Types
docs.text:
The Params class supports modern input types through UiBuilder. Note that many types are automatically mapped to UiBuilder native types for consistent styling.

Type Description Mapping text Standard text input. input type="text" number Numeric input. input type="number" email Email address input. input type="email" password Password input. input type="password" url URL address input. input type="url" textarea Multi-line text block. textarea select Standard dropdown selection. select dropdown Legacy alias for select. select checkbox Modern form-switch toggle. checkbox image elFinder integrated image picker. image date Standard HTML5 date picker. input type="date" date_range Dual input for start and end dates. date_range

docs.title: Field Dependencies (require)
docs.text:
You can make a field dependent on another field's value. When the parent field changes, the child field can even fetch new data via AJAX or toggle its visibility/state.

Property Type Description require string The name of the parent parameter field to watch. require_value string The value of the parent field that triggers the dependency. Default: on. require_action string The UI action: show (default) or enable. ajax_url string Optional. An AJAX URL to fetch new options for select fields when the parent changes. 
Params::register([
    'bottom' => [
        [
            'fields' => [
                [
                    'name' => 'is_discounted',
                    'title' => 'Apply Discount?',
                    'type' => 'checkbox'
                ],
                [
                    'name' => 'discount_amount',
                    'title' => 'Discount Amount',
                    'type' => 'number',
                    'require' => 'is_discounted',
                    'require_value' => 'on',
                    'require_action' => 'show'
                ]
            ]
        ]
    ]
]);

docs.title: 1. Basic Registration
docs.text:
Params::register([
    'sidebar' => [
        [
            'grouptitle' => 'SEO Settings',
            'fields' => [
                [
                    'name' => 'meta_desc',
                    'title' => 'Meta Description',
                    'type' => 'textarea'
                ]
            ]
        ]
    ]
]);

docs.title: 2. Using Advanced Fields (Image & Date Range)
docs.text:
Params::register([
    'bottom' => [
        [
            'grouptitle' => 'Campaign Details',
            'fields' => [
                [
                    'name' => 'hero_img',
                    'title' => 'Campaign Banner',
                    'type' => 'image'
                ],
                [
                    'type' => 'date_range',
                    'title' => 'Event Duration',
                    'name_start' => 'event_start',
                    'name_end' => 'event_end'
                ]
            ]
        ]
    ]
]);

docs.title: 3. Conditional Post Type
docs.text:
Params::register([
    'sidebar' => [
        [
            'grouptitle' => 'Inventory',
            'post_type' => 'product', // Only appears on Products
            'fields' => [
                [
                    'name' => 'stock_qty',
                    'title' => 'Stock Quantity',
                    'type' => 'text'
                ]
            ]
        ]
    ]
]);

docs.title: Technical Notes
docs.text:
• Namespace: When saved, all parameters are prefixed with param[...] in the POST request.
  • Persistence: Data is automatically saved via Posts::addParam() during the post update cycle.
  • Legacy Support: dropdown type is internally aliased to select for backwards compatibility.

docs.title: Search Engine Notification Class (Pinger)
docs.text: The Pinger class is a specialized utility in GeniXCMS that automates the process of notifying search engines, aggregators, and directory services whenever new content is published or existing content is updated. It utilizes the Weblog XML-RPC Ping protocol to ensure your site's changes are indexed as rapidly as possible.

docs.title: Pinger::run($services)
docs.text:
Dispatches a notification to one or more directory services.

Parameter Type Required Description $services array\|string Yes A target URL list (array or newline-separated string).

Internal Logic: The method automatically extracts the site name, homepage URL, and RSS feed URL from the system settings to populate the ping payload.

// Dispatch to a specific list of engines
$engines = [
    'rpc.pingomatic.com',
    'blogsearch.google.com/ping/RPC2'
];

Pinger::run($engines);

docs.title: Pinger::isOn()
docs.text:
Verifies if the automated ping systems are enabled in the administrative settings.

  • Returns: bool (True if pinger_enable is set to "Yes").

docs.title: 🏗️ Technical Architecture
docs.text:
The Pinger class leverages the integrated IXR Library (Incubator XML-RPC) for network transport and XML formatting.

  1. Payload Discovery: Gathers sitename, siteurl, and rssurl from global options.
  2. Multicast Handing: If a newline-separated string of services is provided, the class automatically explodes the string into a processable array.
  3. RPC Transmission:
    • weblogUpdates.extendedPing: First, it attempts an extended ping for richer metadata.
    • weblogUpdates.ping: Falls back to a basic ping if the target service does not support extended protocols.

[!IMPORTANT] Network Requirements: For the Pinger to function correctly, your server must have cURL enabled and be able to make outbound requests to external XML-RPC endpoints on the public web.

[!TIP] SEO Performance: In the admin dashboard under Settings > General, ensure you provide a comprehensive list of ping services (like Pingomatic) to maximize your site's organic discovery speed after every new blog post.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Vendor Class — How the IXR Library is integrated into the core.
  • Posts Class — Where the Pinger is typically triggered after a successful save.

docs.title: Posts Class
docs.text: The Posts class manages all types of content entries in GeniXCMS, including blog posts and static pages. It handles insertion, retrieval, metadata management (params), and automated excerpt generation.

docs.title: insert()
docs.text:
Adds a new content entry. Automatically handles slug creation, excerpt caching, and hook triggers. 

Posts::insert([
    'title'   => 'GeniXCMS is Awesome',
    'content' => 'Full article content...',
    'author'  => 'admin',
    'cat'     => 5,
    'type'    => 'post',
    'status'  => '1',
    'date'    => date('Y-m-d H:i:s')
]);

docs.title: update()
docs.text: Updates an existing record. Requires the post ID to be present in $_GET['id'] (admin context) or passed within the logic. Posts::update(array $vars);

docs.title: delete()
docs.text: Completely removes a post, its metadata (params), and associated comments. Posts::delete(int $id);

docs.title: content()
docs.text: Returns cleaned post content. It strips legacy [[--readmore--]] tags and applies post_content_filter hooks. echo Posts::content($raw_content);

docs.title: format()
docs.text: Processes content for summary lists. If the [[--readmore--]] tag is present, it returns the text before the tag followed by a "Read More" link. echo Posts::format($raw_content, $post_id);

docs.title: excerpt()
docs.text: Retrieves a plain-text summary of the post. It uses a cached version from the database or generates a new one (caching it automatically) if missing. echo Posts::excerpt($post_id, $optional_content);

docs.title: Metadata (Parameters)
docs.text: Metadata allows you to attach custom attributes (like featured images, SEO tags, or layout settings) to any post.

docs.title: getParam()
docs.text: Posts::getParam(string $key, int $post_id);

docs.title: getParams()
docs.text: Retrieves all parameters (metadata) associated with a post as an associative array. $meta = Posts::getParams(int $post_id);

docs.title: addParam() / editParam()
docs.text: Posts::addParam($key, $value, $post_id); Posts::editParam($key, $value, $post_id);

docs.title: existParam()
docs.text: Posts::existParam(string $key, int $post_id);

docs.title: recent()
docs.text:
Fetches a list of latest posts. 

$posts = Posts::recent([
    'num'  => 5,
    'cat'  => 2,      // Optional category filter
    'type' => 'post'  // Default: 'post'
]);

docs.title: lists()
docs.text:
Renders a complex HTML list of posts (Bootstrap compatible). 

echo Posts::lists([
    'num'        => 5,
    'excerpt'    => true,
    'image'      => true,
    'image_size' => 150
]);

docs.title: dropdown()
docs.text: Generates an HTML <select> menu of content titles. echo Posts::dropdown(['name' => 'featured_post', 'type' => 'post']);

docs.title: getPageList()
docs.text: Returns an associative array of all published pages (ID => Title). Ideal for configuration dropdowns. $pages = Posts::getPageList();

docs.title: 🖥️ Dashboard UI Helpers
docs.text: These methods are used to generate the standardized GeniXCMS dashboard tables, supporting both AJAX and traditional rendering.

docs.title: getDashboardHeaders()
docs.text: Returns the array of headers for a post type table, with support for the admin_posts_table_headers (or admin_pages_*) hook. Posts::getDashboardHeaders(string $postType);

docs.title: getDashboardRow()
docs.text: Generates a formatted row for a post object, including thumbnail, action buttons, and status badges. Supports the admin_posts_table_row hook. Posts::getDashboardRow(object $pObj, string $group, string $username, string $postType);

docs.title: getTypeLabel()
docs.text: Retrieves a specific UI label for a post type (e.g., 'new_item', 'repository_title') with fallback to defaults. Posts::getTypeLabel(string $type, string $key);

docs.title: Post Type Customization
docs.text: You can customize the labels displayed in the admin interface for different post types.

docs.title: setTypeLabel()
docs.text:
Posts::setTypeLabel('portfolio', [
    'label' => 'Portfolio Item',
    'create' => 'Add Project',
    'title_placeholder' => 'Enter project name...'
]);

docs.title: PostsAjax Class
docs.text: The PostsAjax class provides the server-side logic for the modern AJAX-driven dashboards in GeniXCMS. It handles data retrieval, complex filtering, and pagination for any post type.

docs.title: list_posts()
docs.text:
The main entry point for retrieving post data via AJAX. It automatically handles authentication, query building, and formatting using the Posts class helpers.

Usage (Backend): (new PostsAjax())->list_posts();

URL Parameters (GET): | Parameter | Description | Default | | :--- | :--- | :--- | | type | Post type identifier (e.g., post, page, nixomers). | post | | num | Number of records to return per page. | 10 | | offset | Starting record index for pagination. | 0 | | q | Search query (searches title and content). | - | | cat | Category ID filter. | - | | status | Record status filter (1 for Live, 0 for Draft). | - | | from | Start date filter (YYYY-MM-DD). | - | | to | End date filter (YYYY-MM-DD). | - |

docs.title: JSON Response Structure
docs.text:
The class returns a standardized JSON response compatible with the GeniXCMS AJAX dashboard JavaScript renderer.

{
    "status": "success",
    "headers": [
        { "content": "Title", "class": "ps-4" },
        ...
    ],
    "data": [
        [
            { "content": "Row 1 HTML", "class": "p-0" },
            ...
        ]
    ],
    "total": 125,
    "limit": 10,
    "offset": 0
}

docs.title: Extensibility
docs.text:
list_posts() is designed to be highly extensible. It internally calls:

  1. Posts::getDashboardHeaders($type): Runs the admin_posts_table_headers filter.
  2. Posts::getDashboardRow($pObj, ...): Runs the admin_posts_table_row filter for each record.

This ensures that any columns added by modules or developers via hooks are automatically included in the AJAX response.

docs.title: Security
docs.text: The class uses Ajax::auth($param) to ensure that only authorized administrative users can access the data. It also uses Typo::cleanX() and Typo::int() to sanitize all incoming GET parameters before processing.

docs.title: PostsApi Class Interface
docs.text: The PostsApi class is a resource-specific controller within the GeniXCMS RESTful API framework. It handles standard CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations for Post resources via HTTP endpoints.

docs.title: Endpoint Mapping
docs.text:
The following HTTP methods are mapped to the class methods when accessing /api/v1/posts/:

Method Endpoint Action GET / List all active posts. GET /{id} Retrieve details of a specific post. POST / Create a new post. PUT /{id} Update an existing post. DELETE /{id} Permanently remove a post.

docs.title: index()
docs.text:
Handles retrieval of one or multiple post records.

Syntax: 

public function index($id = null)

  • If $id is null: Returns a list of all posts with status 1, ordered by newest first.
  • If $id is provided: Returns the specific post record or a 404 error if not found.

docs.title: submit()
docs.text: Processes a POST request to create a new post. Expects a JSON payload in the request body.

docs.title: update()
docs.text: Processes a PUT request to update specific fields of a post identified by its ID.

docs.title: delete()
docs.text: Processes a DELETE request to remove a post from the database.

docs.title: Usage in Modules
docs.text:
Modules can interact with this class internally if they need to bypass the standard Posts model logic or to extend the API behavior.

Example: Internal Execution 

$api = new PostsApi();
$result = $api->index(10); // Fetch post ID 10 via the API controller logic

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• RESTful API Reference — For authentication and global API response formats.
  • Posts Class Reference — For the underlying model used by this controller.

docs.title: Query Class (Fluent Query Builder)
docs.text: The Query class provides a fluent, expressive interface for building and executing database queries. It simplifies complex SQL construction while ensuring security through automatic parameter binding.

docs.title: 🏗️ Initialization
docs.text:
Start a query by using the static table() method.

$query = Query::table('posts');

docs.title: select($columns = '*')
docs.text:
Specify which columns to retrieve. Accepts a string or an array of column names. 

Query::table('posts')->select(['id', 'title', 'content'])->get();

docs.title: distinct()
docs.text:
Forces the query to return unique results. 

Query::table('posts')->select('author')->distinct()->get();

docs.title: 🛡️ Where Clauses
docs.text: All where methods use parameter binding to prevent SQL injection.

docs.title: where($column, $operator, $value = null)
docs.text:
Adds a basic WHERE condition. Supports operator overloading. 

// column = value (Equality)
$user = Query::table('users')->where('id', 5)->first();

// column operator value
$active = Query::table('posts')->where('status', '>', 0)->get();

docs.title: orWhere($column, $operator, $value = null)
docs.text: Adds an OR condition to the WHERE clause.

docs.title: whereIn($column, array $values)
docs.text:
Filters results by an array of values. 

Query::table('users')->whereIn('id', [1, 2, 3])->get();

docs.title: groupWhere(callable $callback) / orGroupWhere(callable $callback)
docs.text:
Nests logic within parentheses for complex AND/OR logic. 

Query::table('posts')
    ->where('type', 'post')
    ->groupWhere(function($q) {
        $q->where('status', 1)->orWhere('featured', 1);
    })->get();

docs.title: whereRaw($sql, array $bindings = [])
docs.text:
Injects a raw SQL fragment into the WHERE clause with optional bindings. 

Query::table('posts')->whereRaw("DATE(date) = ?", ['2023-01-01'])->get();

docs.title: join($table, $first, $operator, $second, $type = 'INNER')
docs.text: Performs a standard table join.

docs.title: leftJoin($table, $first, $operator, $second)
docs.text: Performs a LEFT JOIN.

docs.title: rightJoin($table, $first, $operator, $second)
docs.text:
Performs a RIGHT JOIN.

Query::table('posts')
    ->leftJoin('users', 'posts.author', '=', 'users.userid')
    ->select(['posts.*', 'users.username'])
    ->get();

docs.title: orderBy($column, $direction = 'ASC')
docs.text: Sets the sorting order.

docs.title: asc($column) / desc($column)
docs.text:
Shortcuts for orderBy($column, 'ASC') and orderBy($column, 'DESC'). 

Query::table('posts')->desc('date')->get();

docs.title: orderByRaw($expression)
docs.text:
Adds a raw ORDER BY expression. 

Query::table('posts')->orderByRaw("RAND()")->get();

docs.title: groupBy($column)
docs.text:
Groups the resulting rows. 

Query::table('posts')->groupBy('author')->get();

docs.title: limit($limit, $offset = null)
docs.text:
Constraints the number of results returned. 

Query::table('posts')->limit(10)->get();

docs.title: offset($offset)
docs.text:
Sets the starting point for the results. 

Query::table('posts')->limit(10)->offset(20)->get();

docs.title: get()
docs.text: Executes the query and returns an array of result objects.

docs.title: first()
docs.text: Returns only the first record found (as an object) or null.

docs.title: count($column = '*')
docs.text:
Returns the total number of matching records. 

$total = Query::table('posts')->where('type', 'page')->count();

docs.title: sum($column)
docs.text:
Computes the sum of a specific numeric column. 

$totalPrice = Query::table('orders')->sum('price');

docs.title: insert(array $data)
docs.text:
Inserts a new record into the table. 

Query::table('posts')->insert([
    'title' => 'New Title',
    'content' => 'Hello World',
    'date' => date('Y-m-d H:i:s')
]);

docs.title: update(array $data)
docs.text:
Updates matching records based on the defined WHERE clauses. 

Query::table('posts')->where('id', 1)->update(['status' => 0]);

docs.title: delete()
docs.text:
Deletes matching records based on the defined WHERE clauses. 

Query::table('posts')->where('status', 0)->delete();

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Db Class — The underlying database driver used by Query.
  • Model Class — Using Query Builder within Data Models.

docs.title: Headless CMS & RESTful API Engine
docs.text: GeniXCMS 2.0.0 features a powerful, built-in RESTful API Engine that allows you to use the platform as a Headless CMS. This architecture enables you to decouple your content management from your frontend delivery, serving your data to mobile apps, static site generators (like Hugo or Gatsby), or custom JavaScript frameworks.

docs.title: 🔐 Administrative Authentication
docs.text:
To interact with the API, requests must be authenticated using a valid GX-API-KEY. The system supports two primary delivery methods:

  1. Request Header (Recommended):
    • Key: GX-API-KEY
    • Value: your_secret_api_key_here
  2. Query String (Development only):
    • URL suffix: ?api-key=your_secret_api_key_here

docs.title: 🧭 Global Endpoint Structure
docs.text:
The GeniXCMS API follows standard RESTful naming conventions.

  • Base URL: https://yourdomain.com/api/v1/
  • Pattern: /{resource}/{identifier}

docs.title: 🏹 Standard Content Resources (Posts)
docs.text:
The posts resource is the primary interface for managing editorial content via the API.

Capability HTTP Method Endpoint Path List Records GET /api/v1/posts/ Fetch Single GET /api/v1/posts/{id} Create New POST /api/v1/posts/ Update Existing PUT /api/v1/posts/{id} Permanent Delete DELETE /api/v1/posts/{id}

docs.title: JSON Payload Example (POST/PUT)
docs.text:
{
    "title": "Post from Mobile App",
    "content": "<h1>Hello API</h1><p>Content body goes here...</p>",
    "status": "1",
    "type": "post",
    "author": "clark_kent"
}

docs.title: 📦 Consistent Response Architecture
docs.text: The API engine returns predictable JSON structures for both success and error states.

docs.title: Success Envelope (200 OK)
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "message": "Resource retrieved successfully",
    "records": 42,
    "data": [ { ... }, { ... } ]
}

docs.title: Error Envelope (4xx / 5xx)
docs.text:
{
    "status": "error",
    "code": 404,
    "message": "The requested resource 'member/12' does not exist."
}

docs.title: 🚦 Rate Limiting & Quotas
docs.text: To ensure platform stability and protect against brute-force attacks, the GeniXCMS API implements a Sliding Window Rate Limiter.

docs.title: Default Quotas
docs.text:
By default, the system tracks requests per IP address over a 1-hour window.

  • Global Limit: Configurable via api_rate_limit (Default: 100 requests/hour).
  • Exceeded State: If a client exceeds the quota, the API returns a 429 Too Many Requests status code.

docs.title: HTTP Response Headers
docs.text:
Clients should monitor these headers to stay within their allocated budget:

Header Description X-RateLimit-Limit The maximum number of requests allowed in the current window. X-RateLimit-Remaining The number of requests remaining for the current IP. X-RateLimit-Reset The UTC timestamp (Unix) when the current window resets.

docs.title: 🛠️ Extensibility: Creating Custom Endpoints
docs.text:
Developers can easily register new API resources by creating a specialized controller class.

  • Storage Location: inc/lib/Control/Api/
  • Naming Convention: [Resource]Api.class.php
  • Routing: The Api Class automatically detects and routes the {resource} segment of the URL to your custom class.

[!TIP] API Key Management: You can generate and manage your secure keys in the Admin Dashboard > Settings > Security. Always keep your API keys confidential to prevent unauthorized content modifications.

[!IMPORTANT] Response Headers: The API engine automatically sets the Content-Type: application/json header and handles CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) pre-flight requests if configured in your security settings.

docs.title: ⚙️ Configuring the Rate Limiter
docs.text:
Rate limiting is fully configurable from the Admin Dashboard — no code changes required.

Location: Admin > Settings > Security > RESTful API KEY

Field Description Rate Limit (Req/Hour) Maximum number of requests allowed per IP per hour. Value: 0 Disables rate limiting entirely (open access). Value: 100 Default — allows 100 requests per IP per hour.

docs.title: 429 Error Response
docs.text:
When a client exceeds their quota, the API returns:

{
    "status": "error",
    "code": 429,
    "message": "Too many requests [Rate Limit Exceeded]"
}

[!CAUTION] Disable Only for Trusted Networks: Setting the rate limit to 0 effectively removes all request throttling. Only do this for private, internal APIs. Public-facing endpoints should always have a limit configured.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Security Settings — Configuring API keys and CORS policies.
  • Posts Class — The underlying model consumed by the Posts API.

docs.title: Router (Smart URL Engine)
docs.text: The Router class is the core component responsible for parsing "Smart URLs" (Permalinks) and mapping them to specific system controllers and parameters. It supports robust, regex-based routing and allows for granular extension by third-party modules.

docs.title: 🗺️ Routing Architecture
docs.text: The router uses a predefined map of URI patterns that are matched against the incoming request. This system supports language prefixes, category structures, and custom module routes.

docs.title: 📜 Common Pattern Examples
docs.text: URI Pattern Parameter Result Description category/([0-9]+)/(.*)/ cat=ID Standard category archive archives. tag/(.*)/ tag=SLUG Tag-based content filtering. ([a-z]{2})/mod/(.*).html mod=NAME, lang=XX Module routes with language prefixes. rss/ rss Native RSS feed generation.

docs.title: Router::run()
docs.text:
The primary execution point of the routing engine. This method performs several critical tasks:

  1. URI Detection: Identifies the requested path, automatically handling installations in subdirectories.
  2. Pattern Matching: Compares the URI against the internal routing map.
  3. Parameter Extraction: Returns a structured array of matched parameters upon success.

docs.title: Router::add(array $routes)
docs.text:
Enables modules to register custom routing rules dynamically. New routes are automatically sorted to ensure that the most specific patterns are matched first.

// Registering a new custom route for a module
$my_route = [
    'product/([0-9]+)/' => [
        'product' => 1
    ]
];
Router::add($my_route);

docs.title: 🛠️ Specialized URL Handling
docs.text:
GeniXCMS's routing engine is designed with high flexibility to support various server environments and SEO requirements.

  • 🌍 Locale Support: The router automatically detects 2-letter ISO language codes (e.g., /en/, /id/) at the beginning of the URL and passes them as a lang parameter to the controller.
  • 📁 Subdirectory Intelligence: If GeniXCMS is installed in a folder (e.g., yoursite.com/cms/), the router strips the /cms/ prefix automatically before processing.
  • 🔗 Permalink Flexibility: Seamlessly falls back to index.php based routes if Apache's mod_rewrite (or equivalent Nginx configuration) is unavailable.

docs.title: ⚙️ URL Metadata & Suffixes
docs.text:
The routing engine respects the GX_URL_PREFIX constant (default: .html). This ensures that generated links and internal routes maintain consistent aesthetics across the entire application.

[!TIP] Regex Power: When adding custom routes, you can use capture groups (e.g., (.*)) and map them to their corresponding numerical indexes in your parameter array. This allows for highly dynamic and clean URL structures.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Control Layer — How the parameters returned by the router are dispatched to controllers.
  • Smart URL Guide — Configuring SEO-friendly URLs in the admin panel.

docs.title: Content Syndication Class (RSS)
docs.text: The RSS class is a specialized utility in GeniXCMS that automates the generation of XML-compliant RSS 2.0 feeds. This enables your site's content to be syndicated to external readers, news aggregators, and email notification services.

docs.title: Rss::create(int $limit = 20, ...)
docs.text:
Compiles and directly outputs a formatted XML feed to the browser.

Parameter Type Default Description $limit int 20 Maximum number of items in the XML feed. $url_method string 'post' The Url Class method used for link generation. $type string 'post' The specific content type to include (e.g., post, page, catalog).

Returns: Directly streams XML content with the Content-Type: text/xml header.

// Typically executed within an RSS controller or dedicated feed route
Rss::create(50, 'post', 'post');

docs.title: 🏗️ Technical Architecture
docs.text:
The RSS class performs several automated data transformations to ensure XML validity and SEO performance.

  1. Metadata Discovery: Retrieves core site identity parameters (Title, URL, Description) from the Site and Options classes to populate the RSS Channel headers.
  2. Dataset Acquisition: Fetches recent content using the Posts Class's recent() method.
  3. Content Sanitization:
    • Tag Extraction: Strips internal GeniXCMS shortcodes like [[--readmore--]].
    • XML Escaping: Cleanses the content body of special characters that could break the XML parser.
    • Link Resolution: Uses the Url Class to ensure all links are absolute permalinks.

docs.title: 🏹 Automated Syndication Logic
docs.text:
When a request is made to your site's RSS endpoint (usually /rss.php or /feed/), the system: - Sets the appropriate XML Headers. - Sets the Language attribute based on your global multi-language settings. - Generates a Channel containing the most recent editorial content.

[!IMPORTANT] SEO Deployment: Always include the RSS feed link in your theme's <head> section to allow browsers and perimeters to auto-discover your syndicate: <link rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml" title="RSS Feed" href="{Site::$url}feed/">.

[!TIP] Performance Hint: RSS feeds are excellent candidates for Caching. Combined with the GeniXCMS cache engine, your feed can be delivered as a static XML file, significantly reducing server load during aggregator crawls.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Posts Class — The underlying model consumed by the RSS engine.
  • Url Class — How the system generates absolute permalinks for feed items.
  • Cache Settings — Optimizing feed delivery performance.

docs.title: SaveimageAjax Class
docs.text: The SaveimageAjax class is a specialized endpoint for handling image uploads from frontend or backend editors (such as EditorJS, Summernote, or custom implementations). It supports both standard multi-part form uploads and base64-encoded data.

docs.title: index($param = null)
docs.text:
The primary endpoint for image uploads. It processes the incoming file, saves it to the assets/media/images/ directory, and performs automatic compression.

  • Endpoint: index.php?ajax=saveimage&token=TOKEN
  • Request Types:
    • Form Data: Accepts files with keys image, file, or upload.
    • Base64: Accepts file and file_name in a POST request.

Workflow:

  1. Authentication: Verifies the system token and ensures the user has at least level 2 access.
  2. Directory Check: Ensures the assets/media/images/ directory exists and is writable.
  3. Extension Validation: Only allows 'png', 'jpg', 'jpeg', 'gif', and 'webp'.
  4. Filename Sanitization: Generates a unique filename using a timestamp and sanitized original name.
  5. Compression: Automatically compresses the saved image using Image::compressPng() or Image::compressJpg() based on the format.
  6. Response: Returns a JSON object with the image URL and relative path, compatible with various editor requirements (e.g., returns success: 1 for EditorJS).

docs.title: 🛡️ Error Handling
docs.text:
The class provides detailed feedback for common upload failures:

  • PHP Limits: Handles upload_max_filesize and MAX_FILE_SIZE violations.
  • Incomplete Uploads: Detects partial or missing file uploads.
  • Disk Issues: Reports failures to write to the temporary directory or destination path.
  • Format Errors: Rejects invalid file extensions or corrupted base64 data.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Ajax Class — The main AJAX dispatcher.
  • Image Class — The underlying compression engine.
  • Upload Class — General purpose file upload utility.

docs.title: Security Class Interface
docs.text: The Security class provides system-level safety checks, including automated scanning of uploaded packages for malicious or dangerous patterns. It is a critical component used during the installation of themes and modules to prevent unauthorized code execution and data breaches.

docs.title: Security::scanZip(string $zipPath) : array
docs.text:
Scans the entire contents of a ZIP file for dangerous PHP or Javascript patterns. This method iterates through all files in the archive (skipping directories) and analyzes content based on file extensions.

Parameters:

  • $zipPath (string): The absolute path to the local ZIP file to be scanned.

Returns:

  • array: Evaluation result:
    • status (bool): true if no threats were found, false otherwise.
    • errors (array): A list of specific security violations found (e.g., "Security Alert: Dangerous function 'eval' found in 'script.php'").

Supported Extensions:

  • PHP: .php, .phtml, .php5, .php7, .php8.
  • Javascript: .js.

docs.title: 🛡️ Internal Scanning Logic
docs.text: The class implements specialized scanners for different content types to identify common attack vectors.

docs.title: PHP Pattern Protection
docs.text:
The PHP scanner (scanPhp) blocks critical execution functions and shell access:

  • Execution Functions: eval(), passthru(), shell_exec(), system(), exec(), popen(), proc_open(), pcntl_exec().
  • Dynamic Creation: create_function(), assert().
  • Obfuscation Helpers: base64_decode(), gzinflate().
  • Shell Backticks: Detects execution via backtick operators (e.g., `ls -la`).

docs.title: Javascript Pattern Protection
docs.text:
The Javascript scanner (scanJs) identifies patterns often used in XSS or malicious redirects:

  • Dynamic Execution: eval().
  • Encoding/Decoding: atob(), String.fromCharCode(), unescape().
  • Obfuscation Detection: Checks for heavily obfuscated code by analyzing the ratio of alphanumeric characters in large files.

docs.title: 💡 Implementation Example
docs.text:
This class is automatically invoked by the Marketplace class, but can be used manually in any module handling ZIP uploads:

$zipFile = GX_PATH . '/inc/cache/temp_plugin.zip';
$scan = Security::scanZip($zipFile);

if (!$scan['status']) {
    // Block installation and display errors
    foreach ($scan['errors'] as $error) {
        echo '<div class="alert alert-danger">' . $error . '</div>';
    }
} else {
    // Safe to proceed with extraction
}

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Security Infrastructure Guide — Information on CSP, HTTP hardening, and general CMS security.
  • Marketplace Class — How the CMS uses this class for automated installations.
  • Upload Class — Handling the initial ingestion of files.

docs.title: Security Infrastructure
docs.text: GeniXCMS implements a multi-layered security architecture designed to protect both the administration dashboard and custom themes from a wide range of web vulnerabilities. By enforcing modern standards like Content Security Policy (CSP), CSRF protection, and HTTP Header Hardening, the framework provides a secure-by-default environment for developers.

docs.title: 🛡️ Content Security Policy (CSP)
docs.text: CSP is the primary defense against Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. GeniXCMS dispatches a robust CSP header with every response.

docs.title: 📜 Default Policy Directives
docs.text: Directive Policy Description default-src 'self' Disallows all external resources by default. script-src 'self', 'unsafe-inline', trusted CDNs Allows internal scripts and whitelisted providers (JSDelivr, JQuery CDN, Cloudflare). style-src 'self', inline styles, trusted CDNs Allows internal CSS and whitelisted style providers. img-src 'self', data:, * (HTTPS) Allows internal images, base64 data URIs, and any secure external image. font-src 'self', data:, Google Fonts Allows internal fonts and trusted font providers (gstatic.com). object-src 'none' Completely disables plugins like Flash or Silverlight.

docs.title: 🔌 Extending the Whitelist
docs.text: If your custom theme or module requires resources from a new third-party CDN (e.g., Swiper, Chart.js, or Google Maps), you must register the domain using the system_security_headers_args filter hook.

docs.title: Example: Whitelisting a New API & CDN
docs.text:
Hooks::attach('system_security_headers_args', function($rules) {
    // Allow scripts from a new CDN
    $rules['script-src'][] = "https://cdn.my-library.com";

    // Allow AJAX/Fetch requests to an external API
    $rules['connect-src'][] = "https://api.my-data-source.com";

    return $rules;
});

docs.title: 🔒 HTTP Header Hardening
docs.text:
In addition to CSP, GeniXCMS automatically injects the following security-focused headers into every response:

  • X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff: Prevents browser-side MIME sniffing, forcing strict adherence to the declared Content-Type.
  • X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN: Protects against Clickjacking by preventing the site from being embedded in an <iframe> on external domains.
  • X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block: Enforces the built-in XSS filter in modern browsers.
  • Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin: Enhances user privacy by limiting referrer data sent to third-party sites.

docs.title: 🔑 CSRF Protection
docs.text:
Cross-Site Request Forgery is mitigated using a robust, token-based validation system.

  • Automated Generation: Managed by the Token class.
  • Controller Validation: Integrated into all core controllers.
  • Dashboard Constant: The TOKEN constant is globally available in the admin panel for easy form integration.

docs.title: Implementation Example:
docs.text:
<form action="/submit" method="POST">
    <input type="hidden" name="token" value="{$token}">
    <!-- Form fields... -->
    <button type="submit">Complete Operation</button>
</form>

docs.title: 🛠️ Troubleshooting Violations
docs.text:
If you see "Refused to load..." or "Content Security Policy..." errors in your browser's developer console:

  1. Check Protocol: Ensure you are using https://. GeniXCMS blocks insecure http:// external assets by default.
  2. Verify Domain: Is the domain in our Default Whitelist? If not, use the system_security_headers_args hook.
  3. AJAX Connectivity: If your JavaScript fetch() or XMLHttpRequest is failing, ensure the API domain is added to the connect-src directive.

[!WARNING] While unsafe-inline and unsafe-eval are currently permitted for compatibility with legacy components (like elFinder), developers are encouraged to move towards nonce-based or hash-based script execution for custom developments.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Hooks Reference — For a deep dive into using policy filtering hooks.
  • Vendor Management — How integrated libraries are secured.

docs.title: Session Management & Storage Class
docs.text: The Session class is the primary state management engine for GeniXCMS. It provides a secure, abstraction layer for handling user sessions, offering support for both standard filesystem storage and persistent, high-performance database-backed sessions via the SessionHandlerInterface.

docs.title: Session::start(int $lifetime = 1)
docs.text:
Initializes the session engine with custom security parameters.

  • Security: Sets the httponly and samesite (Lax) cookie attributes to prevent Cross-Site Scripting (XSS).
  • Automation: Automatically groups all GeniXCMS session data under the $_SESSION['gx_sess'] namespace to avoid collisions with external modules.

docs.title: Session::set($key, $value = '')
docs.text:
Commits data to the active session.

Input Type Usage Description string Session::set('role', 'admin'); Sets a single key-value pair. array Session::set(['A' => '1', 'B' => '2']); Batch updates multiple session keys.

docs.title: Session::val(string $key)
docs.text:
Retrieves a specific value from the session namespace.

$is_authenticated = Session::val('is_logged_in');

docs.title: ⚙️ Persistent Database Sessions
docs.text: GeniXCMS 2.0.0 supports offloading session storage to the database for improved reliability in load-balanced environments.

docs.title: Configuration (inc/config/config.php)
docs.text: Constant Default Description SESSION_DB false Set to true to store sessions in the sessions table. SESSION_EXPIRES 1 The session lifetime in hours.

docs.title: 🏗️ Lifecycle & Sanitation
docs.text:
The class implements the full SessionHandlerInterface for database interaction:

  • read() / write(): Decodes and encodes serialized session data into the sessions table.
  • gc(int $max_lifetime): Automated Garbage Collection that purges expired sessions to maintain database performance.
  • logout(): Destroys the entire session registry, clears local variables, and invalidates the session cookie.

docs.title: 🛠️ Security Best Practices
docs.text:
• Namespace Isolation: GeniXCMS uses the gx_sess prefix to ensure your site's authentication remains isolated from other applications on the same domain.
  • Encryption: When SESSION_DB is enabled, sensitive session data is stored in the database, reducing the risk of local session hijacking in shared hosting environments.

[!CAUTION] Session Destruction: Calling Session::logout() is irreversible for the current request. Ensure all required data has been persisted to the database before executing a logout command.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• User Class — How the authentication system interacts with sessions.
  • Db Class — The underlying database provider for persistent sessions.

docs.title: Shortcode Parser & Attribute Extraction Class
docs.text: The Shortcode class (introduced in GeniXCMS 2.0.0) provides a standardized, high-performance engine for parsing dynamic tags and extracting their attributes within a text block. It is specifically designed to bypass aggressive backend sanitizers (like HTML Purifier) by embedding layout properties directly within a bracketed string (e.g., [toc title="My List" float="right"]).

docs.title: ⚡ Core Operational Concepts
docs.text: Shortcodes are a powerful way to inject dynamic content or complex UI components into posts, pages, or any text field.

docs.title: 🛡️ Why Use Shortcodes?
docs.text:
• Immutability: Unlike HTML data- attributes, content inside [...] is treated as simple text by sanitizers, meaning your attributes (lebar, tinggi, title) won't be stripped.
  • Flexibility: Supports multiple attribute formats (quoted, unquoted, single-quote).
  • Extensibility: Allows modules to register their own tags easily.

docs.title: Shortcode::parse($tag, $content, $callback)
docs.text:
Scans the provided content for a specific tag and executes a callback for every match found.

// In your module or hook callback
$content = Shortcode::parse('my_tag', $content, function($attrs, $original) {
    // $attrs is an associative array of key="value" pairs
    $name = $attrs['name'] ?? 'Stranger';
    return "Hello, <strong>{$name}</strong>!";
});

docs.title: Shortcode::parseAttributes($attrStr)
docs.text: A utility method to convert a raw attribute string (e.g., id="123" color='red' width=100%) into a clean associative array.

docs.title: 🧪 Implementation Workflow
docs.text: Method Role Input Output parse($tag, $content, $fn) The Engine Tag name, raw content, and rendering logic. Processed string with rendered shortcodes. parseAttributes($str) The Parser A raw string of attributes. An associative key => value array.

docs.title: 🏹 Native Shortcodes in GxEditor
docs.text:
The GeniXCMS official block editor utilizes this class for several high-impact components:

  • [toc ...]: Table of Contents with dynamic heading detection.
  • [image ...]: Robust image layout with alignment and styling attributes.
  • [post id="X"]: Previews a single post by its ID.
  • [recent_posts count="5"]: Lists the most recent content.

docs.title: 🛠️ Developer Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Defaults: Always provide fallback values for your attributes (e.g., $count = $attrs['count'] ?? 5;).
  2. HTML Safety: When returning HTML from a shortcode callback, ensure you escape any user-provided attributes if necessary using htmlspecialchars().
  3. Global Filters: It is recommended to attach your shortcode parsing logic to the post_content_filter hook to ensure it runs during frontend rendering.

[!TIP] Performance: Shortcode::parse uses a pre-check with strpos before executing regex, making it extremely efficient for large pieces of content that may not contain the tag.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Hooks Class — How to attach your shortcode parser to system filters.
  • Using Shortcodes Guide — Step-by-step tutorial on building a shortcode-based module.

docs.title: Global Website & SEO Class
docs.text: The Site class is the primary data container and orchestration engine for your website's global identity. It manages core site properties, automated SEO metadata generation, structured data (JSON-LD), and on-the-fly asset optimization, ensuring your frontend remains search-engine optimized and high-performing.

docs.title: ⚡ Core Identity Properties
docs.text:
The class provides static, global access to your site's foundational data.

Property Access Description Site::$name static The primary Website Name. Site::$url static The absolute Base URL (e.g., https://site.com/). Site::$desc static Global Meta Description for the homepage. Site::$slogan static Catchy marketing slogan or tagline. Site::$email static Administrative contact email address.

docs.title: 🌎 Automated SEO Engine
docs.text: GeniXCMS 2.0.0 features an intelligent metadata aggregator that handles complex head-section logic.

docs.title: Site::meta(array $data)
docs.text:
Generates a comprehensive suite of SEO and social metadata tags.

  • Standard Tags: Charset, Generator, and Viewport.
  • SEO Optimization: Automated Page Titles, Meta Keywords, and Canonical URLs.
  • Social Graph: Generates Open Graph (Facebook) and Twitter Cards metadata based on post featured images and excerpts.
{* In your theme's header.php *}
{Site::meta($data)|noescape}

docs.title: Site::jsonLD(array $data)
docs.text:
Automatically generates Schema.org compliant JSON-LD scripts for search engine rich results.

  • BlogPosting: Injected on single post pages for improved article discovery.
  • WebPage: Injected on static pages for structural clarity.
  • Organization: Global schema for corporate/site identity.

docs.title: Site::logo(int $w, int $h, string $class)
docs.text:
Retrieves the site's primary logo with automated sizing and responsive class support.

// Render a 150px wide logo with a custom class
echo Site::logo(150, 0, 'navbar-brand-img');

docs.title: 🚀 Performance & Optimization
docs.text:
The Site class includes a high-performance Minification Engine for on-the-fly resource compression.

Method Role Logic minifyHTML($html) Compression Strips redundant whitespace and comments from the final rendered page. minifyCSS($css) Optimization Compresses raw CSS strings for faster transmission. minifyJS($js) Optimization Compresses JavaScript code for improved load times.

[!TIP] SEO Performance: The Site::title() helper automatically appends your site name to the page title (e.g., Blog Post | My Site) to ensure consistent branding across all search engine results.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Options Class — How core site properties are persistently stored.
  • Router Class — How the system resolves absolute URLs.
  • General Settings — Configuring site identity in the dashboard.

docs.title: Search Engine Sitemap Class
docs.text: The Sitemap class is a specialized utility in GeniXCMS designed to automate the generation of XML-compliant Sitemaps. This functionality is critical for modern SEO, as it provides search engine crawlers with a structured map of your site's content, ensuring all posts, pages, and module-specific resources are indexed accurately.

docs.title: ⚡ Index & Map Generation
docs.text: GeniXCMS supports both individual content maps and master sitemap indexes for larger installations.

docs.title: Sitemap::createIndex()
docs.text:
Generates a master Sitemap Index file. This is the primary entry point for search engine crawlers (e.g., sitemap.xml).

  • Logic: It dynamically lists available content-type sitemaps (Posts, Pages, Categories).
  • Format: Directly streams XML with the Content-Type: text/xml header.

docs.title: Sitemap::create(string $type, ...)
docs.text:
Generates an individual content-specific map list.

Parameter Type Default Description $type string 'post' The specific content type to include (e.g., post, page). $limit int 20 Maximum number of items in the XML feed. $url_method string 'post' The Url Class method used for link generation. 
// Typically executed within a sitemap controller or dedicated route
Sitemap::create('post', 100, 'post');

docs.title: 🏗️ Technical Architecture
docs.text:
The Sitemap class performs automated data transformations to ensure XML compliance and SEO efficacy.

  1. Metric Acquisition: Fetches recent content updates using the Posts Class.
  2. Metadata Injection:
    • lastmod: Automatically calculates the last modification date based on the post timestamp.
    • changefreq: Defaults to daily to encourage frequent re-crawling.
    • priority: Assigns relative priority to different content types (Home > Post > Category).
  3. Link Resolution: Uses the Url Class to ensure all links are absolute permalinks, including Smart URL support.

docs.title: 🔌 Extensibility: Registering Custom Maps
docs.text:
Modules can register their own custom content types into the sitemap engine using the Sitemap::addMap() method.

// Registering a Custom Product Module Sitemap
$new_map = [
    'product' => [
        'class' => 'ProductUrl', // The class handling the URL logic
        'url'   => 'detail'      // The specific method to call
    ]
];
Sitemap::addMap($new_map);

[!IMPORTANT] Crawler Submission: Once your sitemap is live (usually at /sitemap.xml or /sitemap/), ensure you submit the URL to Google Search Console and Bing Webmaster Tools to jumpstart your site's index discovery.

[!TIP] Performance Hint: Sitemaps are excellent candidates for Caching. Large sitemaps with thousands of entries should be cached as static XML files to prevent unnecessary database load during crawler visits.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Url Class — How the system generates absolute permalinks for the sitemap.
  • Posts Class — The underlying model consumed by the map engine.
  • Cache Settings — Optimizing XML delivery performance.

docs.title: System Analytics & Statistics Class
docs.text: The Stats class is the primary data-aggregation engine for GeniXCMS. It provides a comprehensive set of methods for gathering system-wide intelligence, including content volume, user growth metrics, and advanced post-view tracking with built-in heuristic bot detection.

docs.title: ⚡ Content Metrics
docs.text:
The following methods are critical for populating administration dashboards and site-wide report summaries.

Method Role Logic totalPost($type) Volume Total record count for the specified type (e.g., post, page). activePost($type) Production Counts only published and public records (Status 1). inactivePost($type) Moderation Counts drafts, hidden, or scheduled records (Status 0). totalCat($type) Taxonomy Counts all active categories for the specified context.

docs.title: 👥 User Engagement Analytics
docs.text:
Monitor your community's growth and administrative overhead.

  • Stats::totalUser(): Returns the total number of registered general members.
  • Stats::totalAdmin(): Returns the count of accounts with Level 0 (Administrator) access.
  • Stats::activeUser(): Counts verified and active user profiles.
  • Stats::pendingUser(): Counts users currently awaiting email verification steps.

docs.title: 📈 Content Popularity Tracking
docs.text: GeniXCMS features a robust, human-focused view tracking system.

docs.title: Stats::addViews(int $post_id)
docs.text:
Increments the views counter for a specific content record.

  • Bot Protection: The method automatically filters and discards requests from over 50 common search engine crawlers (Googlebot, Bingbot, etc.) to ensure your analytics represent real human engagement.
  • Implementation: Typically called at the beginning of your theme's single.php.

docs.title: Stats::mostViewed(int $limit, ...)
docs.text:
Retrieves a collection of post objects ranked by their accumulated view counts.

// Fetch the top 5 most popular blog posts
$trending = Stats::mostViewed(5, 'post');

docs.title: 💬 Comment & Interaction Metrics
docs.text:
Method Description totalComments() Cumulative count of all reader submissions. activeComments() Counts approved and live comments. pendingComments() Counts submissions currently held in the moderation queue.

[!IMPORTANT] Performance Architecture: The Stats class methods are optimized to perform single-metric SQL queries. When building heavy report dashboards, consider Caching the returned values if your database volume is extremely high.

[!TIP] Dashboard Utility: Use these methods in your custom Programmable Dashboard widgets to provide your clients with real-time site updates.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Posts Class — The model where content metrics are derived.
  • Dashboard Guide — How these metrics appear in the UI.
  • Programmable Dashboard — Building custom reporting widgets.

docs.title: Bootstrap Core (System) Class
docs.text: The System class is the foundational bootstrap core of GeniXCMS. It is responsible for the entire orchestration of the application lifecycle — from low-level environment checks and dependency loading to module registration, session initialization, and final output buffering.

docs.title: 🏗️ The Bootstrap Sequence
docs.text:
The System constructor initializes the framework in a strict, sequential order to ensure all dependencies are available when needed.

  1. Environment Setup: Detects proxied HTTPS (HTTP_X_FORWARDED_PROTO) and normalizes the $_SERVER array.
  2. Configuration Load: Calls System::config('config') to load database credentials and constants.
  3. Security Headers: Dispatches System::securityHeaders() to set hardened HTTP response headers.
  4. Core Container Init: Registers Db, Hooks, Http, Options, Cache, Token, Date, Site, Session, and User in the service Container.
  5. Language Init: Initializes gettext locale via System::lang() and registers the Language service.
  6. Router & Extensions: Registers Router, Vendor, Sitemap, Mod, Params, and Archives.
  7. Theme Config: Instantiates Theme early to load function.php before hooks are fired.
  8. Initialization Hook: Fires the init action hook. Modules and Themes can participate here.
  9. Cron: Calls Cron::run() to execute any pending scheduled tasks.
  10. Admin Menu: Instantiates AdminMenu to finalize the rendering context.
  11. Admin Hooks: Attaches System::alert() to admin_page_notif_action and System::loadAdminAsset() to admin_footer_action.
  12. Toolbar Cache: Pre-renders the editor toolbar and caches in System::$toolbar.

docs.title: System::securityHeaders()
docs.text:
Sets hardened HTTP security headers on every request (skipped on CLI).

Sets the following standard headers: - X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff - X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN - X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block - Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin

Also generates and sends a Content Security Policy (CSP) header. The default policy permits scripts from common CDNs (jsDelivr, jQuery, Cloudflare, Tailwind) and can be extended via a hook:

// In a module or theme, extend the CSP policy:
Hooks::add_filter('system_security_headers_args', function($csp_rules) {
    $csp_rules['script-src'][] = 'https://my-trusted-cdn.com';
    return $csp_rules;
});

[!IMPORTANT] Hook Name: system_security_headers_args is a Filter hook that receives and must return the full CSP rules array.

docs.title: System::lib(string $class)
docs.text:
A secure library loader for manual dependency injection.

  • Storage: Automatically looks for .class.php files within the inc/lib/ directory.
  • Usage: System::lib('MyCustomLib');

docs.title: System::lang(string $locale)
docs.text:
Sets up the GNU gettext locale for the system interface translation.

  • On Linux: Uses setlocale(LC_MESSAGES, ...).
  • On Windows: Uses putenv("LC_ALL=...").
  • Binds the genixcms text domain to the inc/lang/locale/ directory.

docs.title: System::alert(array $data)
docs.text:
Dispatches elegant notification toasts to the administrative dashboard.

Key Visual Style Best Use Case alertSuccess Green (Success) Completed transactions and settings saves. alertDanger Red (Error) Validation failures and database errors. alertWarning Yellow (Warning) Security notices and configuration tips. alertInfo Blue (Info) Informational messages. 
$notification = [
    'alertSuccess' => ['User profile updated successfully.'],
    'alertWarning' => ['Please check your SMTP settings.']
];
System::alert($notification);

docs.title: System::adminAsset(string $asset)
docs.text:
Registers an HTML string (typically a <script> or <style> tag) to be injected into the admin footer area. Useful for modules needing per-page scripts without overriding the theme.

System::adminAsset('<script src="my-module.js"></script>');

docs.title: System::loadAdminAsset()
docs.text: Outputs all assets registered via System::adminAsset(). Called automatically via the admin_footer_action hook.

docs.title: System::v()
docs.text: Returns the semantic version string of the current installation (e.g., 2.0.0).

docs.title: System::versionCheck()
docs.text:
Orchestrates a background integrity check against the official GeniXCMS repository.

  • Caching: Checks are cached for 24 hours in the system_check option to avoid repeated HTTP requests.
  • Alerting: If a newer version is detected, an alert banner is pinned to the admin header.

docs.title: System::gZip() / System::Zipped()
docs.text: Handles output buffering and optional gzip compression to accelerate page delivery.

docs.title: System::toolbar(string $mode = 'mini')
docs.text:
Returns pre-configured JSON toolbar definition strings for the Summernote WYSIWYG editor.

Mode Description mini Bold, italic, underline, lists, and code block only. light Adds headings, links, media embed, and fullscreen. full All formatting options including tables, colors, and elFinder file browser.

docs.title: System::toolbarMode(string $mode)
docs.text:
Sets System::$toolbar_mode at bootstrap time before the toolbar is compiled. Allows themes to override the default mini mode.

// In a theme's functions.php, set mode before System compiles it:
System::$toolbar_mode = 'full';

[!IMPORTANT] Kernel Integrity: The System class should never be manually modified by theme or module developers. All custom startup logic should be implemented via the init hook: Hooks::add_action('init', 'my_custom_init_function');.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Hooks Class — How the bootstrap process exposes hooks to extensions.
  • Options Class — How the system loads its boot configuration.
  • Container Class — The service registry populated during bootstrap.
  • AdminMenu Class — Registered as the last step of the bootstrap sequence.
  • Admin Dashboard — Where system alerts and version notices appear.

docs.title: Post Tagging & Taxonomy Class
docs.text: The Tags class is a specialized extension of the GeniXCMS taxonomy system. While it leverages the underlying table structure of the Categories class, it provides specific logic for managing flat descriptors (Tags), batch parsing, existence checks, and the dynamic generation of "Tag Cloud" UI components for your themes.

docs.title: Tags::add(string $tags_list)
docs.text:
Parses and commits a collection of tag entities to the database from a comma-separated string.

  • De-duplication: Checks existence with Tags::exist() before performing an INSERT.
  • Slug Generation: Auto-generates a URL slug via Typo::slugify().
  • Type Assignment: All entries are automatically assigned type = 'tag' in the cat table.
// Batch insert newly discovered tags
Tags::add("industrial, premium, latte, vite");

docs.title: Tags::exist(string $tag)
docs.text: Checks if a tag already exists in the database (by name or slug). Returns true or false.

docs.title: Tags::delete(int $tag_id)
docs.text: Permanently removes a tag and its associated metadata from the system.

docs.title: Tags::search(string $q)
docs.text:
Returns an array of tag objects matching a partial name query (used for autocomplete). Results are limited to 10.

// Used by the AJAX tag autocomplete endpoint
$results = Tags::search('ph'); // Returns matches like 'php', 'phpunit'

docs.title: Tags::count(string $tag)
docs.text: Returns the number of posts associated with a specific tag name.

docs.title: Tags::cloud()
docs.text:
Generates a semantic HTML Tag Cloud suitable for theme sidebars or footer widgets.

  • Process: Fetches all entities of type tag, calculates the frequency of each using Tags::count(), sorts by frequency descending, and returns linked items.
  • Output: Anchor tags with .tag-item class and post count in .tag-count span.
{* In your theme sidebar *}
<div class="widget tag-cloud">
    {Tags::cloud()|noescape}
</div>

docs.title: 🏗️ Technical Architecture & Wrapper Methods
docs.text:
The Tags class acts as a high-level wrapper for several Categories Class utilities, pre-configured for the tag context.

Method Role Logic dropdown($vars) Selection UI Generates a <select> list of all active tags. lists($vars) Collection Returns a raw array of all tag objects. name($id) Identity Resolves the plain-text name for a specific tag ID. slug($id) Routing Returns the SEO-friendly URL slug for the tag. id($name) Lookup Returns the database ID for a given tag name. type($id) Metadata Returns the type field value for a given ID. getParent($id) Hierarchy Returns parent data (always 0 for flat tags).

docs.title: 📂 Database Schema
docs.text:
Tags are stored in the core cat table, distinguished by the type field value set to 'tag'. This unified architecture allows tags to benefit from the same slug-generation and URL routing as standard categories.

Column Value for Tags name The display label (e.g., PHP) slug URL-safe version (e.g., php) parent Always 0 (flat, no hierarchy) type Always 'tag'

[!TIP] SEO Performance: Use Categories for broad, hierarchical groupings (e.g., News, Products) and use Tags for specific attributes (e.g., PHP, Vite, White). This creates a logical, data-rich map for search engine spiders.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Categories Class — The underlying taxonomy engine.
  • Posts Class — How tags are associated with content records.
  • User Guide: Categories — Managing your site's taxonomy in the dashboard.

docs.title: TagsAjax Class
docs.text: The TagsAjax class provides a simple AJAX endpoint for tag auto-completion. It searches the system's category table for existing tags matching a user's search term.

docs.title: index($param = null)
docs.text:
The primary endpoint for retrieving tags.

  • Endpoint: index.php?ajax=tags&term=SEARCH_TERM&token=TOKEN
  • URL Parameters:
    • term (string): The search keyword to filter tag names.
    • type (string): The post type context (default: 'post'). This determines if it searches for standard tags or post-type-specific tags (e.g., 'page_tag').

Workflow:

  1. Authentication: Verifies the system token and ensures the user has at least level 2 access.
  2. Querying: Searches the cat table where type is 'tag' (or the specific post-type tag) and the name matches the term.
  3. Response: Returns a JSON array containing only the names of the matching tags.

docs.title: 🛡️ Security & Authentication
docs.text:
• Authentication: Requires a valid system token passed in the request.
  • Access Level: Restricted to users with access(2) (Authors) or higher to prevent unauthorized information disclosure.
  • XSS Protection: Sanitizes the search term using Typo::cleanX().

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Ajax Class — The main AJAX dispatcher.
  • Categories Class — The core category and tag management engine.
  • Token Class — Handling security tokens.

docs.title: Presentation & Theme Architecture Class
docs.text: The Theme class is the primary orchestrator for the visual presentation layer of GeniXCMS. It manages the discovery, activation, and rendering of themes, providing standardized methods for including structural components (Headers, Footers, Sidebars) and handling theme-specific logic through function.php and options.php integrations.

docs.title: ⚡ Structural Rendering Methods
docs.text: GeniXCMS themes follow a modular architecture. These methods allow you to bridge your PHP logic with your presentation templates.

docs.title: Theme::header($data) / Theme::footer($data)
docs.text:
These are the foundational entry/exit points for every theme file.

  • header(): Sends SEO meta-tags and cache headers, then includes the theme's header.php.
  • footer(): Includes footer.php and executes the footer_load_lib hook for library injection.
  • Data Flow: The $data array passed to these methods is globally accessible within the theme files.

docs.title: Theme::theme(string $file, ...)
docs.text:
A secure loader for including sub-templates from the active theme directory.

// Includes: inc/themes/{active_theme}/sidebars/main-blog.php
Theme::theme('sidebars/main-blog');

docs.title: 🛠️ Theme Infrastructure & Logic
docs.text:
The Theme class facilitates the separation of design and business logic through specialized entry points.

File Role Automated Loading function.php Custom helper functions and hooks. Loaded automatically during System boot. options.php The theme's administration settings panel. Included via the Admin > Appearance menu. themeinfo.php Metadata (Name, Version, Author). Parsed by Theme::data() for the dashboard.

docs.title: 🏗️ Administrative Management
docs.text:
These methods are primarily used by the core dashboard to manage the theme lifecycle.

  • Theme::activate(string $name): Sets the specified folder as the system's active theme and executes theme_activate and {theme_slug}_activate hooks.
  • Theme::delete(string $name): Permanently removes a theme from disk and executes theme_delete and {theme_slug}_delete hooks before deletion.
  • Theme::thmList(): Scans the inc/themes/ directory for valid theme packages.
  • Theme::data(string $name): Extracts versioning and credit information from a theme's metadata file.

docs.title: Theme::editor(string $mode, ...)
docs.text:
Configures and injects the Summernote WYSIWYG editor for use within theme-driven forms or module interfaces.

  • Modes: mini, light, or full toolsets.
// Render a compact editor in your theme's frontend form
Theme::editor('mini', 200);

docs.title: 📂 Internal Constants
docs.text:
Upon initialization, the class defines the THEME constant, which contains the folder name of the currently active theme. This is used system-wide for absolute path resolution.

[!TIP] Performance Hint: To ensure your theme remains high-performing, always use Theme::theme() instead of standard PHP include. This method uses an internal registry to prevent redundant disk I/O when including the same component multiple times.

[!IMPORTANT] Theme Security: Never hardcode your theme's path. Always use the Url Class: Url::theme() to retrieve the public-facing URL of your theme's assets (CSS, JS, Images).

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Url Class — Retrieving absolute paths for theme assets.
  • OptionsBuilder Class — Building professional settings panels within options.php.
  • Theme User Guide — Managing appearance from the dashboard.

docs.title: Security Token (CSRF Protection) Class
docs.text: The Token class is a critical security layer in GeniXCMS designed to prevent Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) attacks. Recently redesigned for maximum reliability, it utilizes a One-Token-Per-Session model that provides robust security without sacrificing usability, especially during deep AJAX operations (like the elFinder media manager).

docs.title: 🛡️ Core Protection Architecture (Session-Based)
docs.text:
GeniXCMS implements a robust Session-Paired token strategy.

  1. Persistent Session Token: Upon initialization, the system generates a single, secure 20-character token that persists across the user's active session. This eliminates race conditions caused by rapid AJAX requests.
  2. Global Constant: The active token is assigned to the TOKEN global constant.
  3. Security Fingerprinting (Pairing): Instead of locking a token to a specific URL (which creates issues in dynamic SPAs and background uploads), the token is cryptographically bound to the user's Context Fingerprint, which combines:
    • User IP Address
    • Browser User-Agent
    • SITE_ID

If the token is leaked but the attacker is on a different network or uses a different browser, the validation fails.

docs.title: Token::create()
docs.text:
Initializes or retrieves the persistent session token.

  • Process: Checks if $_SESSION['gx_token'] exists. If not, it creates a new 20-character random string and generates a Context Fingerprint using Token::pairing().
  • Self-Healing AI: If an incoming request provides a valid token within the query string (especially for AJAX routes) and the session is unexpectedly empty, the engine will "adopt" that token to preserve application state.
  • Returns: The string value of the active token.

docs.title: Token::validate(string $token, bool $is_ajax = false) (also Token::isValid())
docs.text:
The primary validation method for incoming state changes.

  • Verification: It compares the provided string strictly against $_SESSION['gx_token'].
  • Fallback Promotion: If the system detects a legacy array-based token, it handles validation and "promotes" the token to the primary session scope, cleaning up old data arrays.
  • Returns: bool (True if the handshake matches).

docs.title: Token::pairing()
docs.text:
Generates the cryptographic context fingerprint.

  • Returns: An MD5 hash of User IP + User Agent + SITE_ID.

docs.title: 🎨 Form Implementation Pattern
docs.text: To secure any HTML form, include the token as a hidden input field.

docs.title: Frontend (HTML)
docs.text:
<form action="save_profile.php" method="POST">
    <!-- CSRF Protection Field -->
    <input type="hidden" name="token" value="<?= TOKEN; ?>">

    <label>Username</label>
    <input type="text" name="username">
    <button type="submit">Save Changes</button>
</form>

docs.title: Backend (PHP Controller)
docs.text:
if (Http::isPost()) {
    if (!Token::isValid($_POST['token'])) {
        // Log potential attack and terminate
        die("Security Breach Detected: Invalid CSRF Token.");
    }
    // Proceed with safe processing...
}

docs.title: 🔒 Deep AJAX Considerations
docs.text:
When building custom module interfaces using raw AJAX (e.g., File Uploads), GeniXCMS natively allows for tokens to be transmitted directly within the URL route if configured correctly.

Using the Url::ajax() helper automatically formats the token. Example: Url::ajax('elfinder/' . Token::create()) translates to http://domain.com/ajax/elfinder/[token_hash].

The Token system will automatically intercept this segment during boot sequences, ensuring controllers like elFinder instantly recognize the session without requiring the token in POST body payloads.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Http Class — Handling request methods (POST/GET).
  • Security Infrastructure — Managing global security policies.

docs.title: Typography & Security Sanitization Class
docs.text: The Typo class is the primary security filter and data manipulation engine for GeniXCMS. It provides comprehensive utilities for input sanitization (XSS protection), cryptographic token generation, URI slugification, and automated content formatting.

docs.title: 🛡️ Security & Sanitization
docs.text: GeniXCMS implements a "Sanitize on Input, Escape on Output" philosophy using the following methods:

docs.title: Typo::cleanX(string $input)
docs.text:
The primary, high-level sanitizer used for every form submission in the dashboard.

  • Process: Employs HTMLPurifier to recursively strip malicious JavaScript, handles deep HTML encoding, and executes secondary XSS attribute filtering.
  • Best Use Case: Processing post content, comments, and site settings.

docs.title: Typo::filterXss(string $raw_html)
docs.text: A lightweight, fast filter targeted specifically at removing dangerous event listeners (e.g., onclick, alert) and URI schemes (e.g., javascript:, data:) while preserving safe HTML structure.

docs.title: ⚡ Data Manipulation & Slugs
docs.text:
Convert raw text into SEO-friendly identifiers or legible document fragments.

Method Role Logic slugify($text) SEO Utility Transforms "Hello World!" into "hello-world" with ASCII normalization. url2link($text) Auto-Linker Scans content for URIs and automatically wraps them in <a> tags. nl2p($text) Formatting Converts double-newlines into semantic <p> paragraphs. translate($str) Localization Wrapper for the internal _() Gettext localization engine.

docs.title: 🔐 Cryptography & Handshakes
docs.text:
The Typo class provides several layers of randomness for security tokens and password resets.

  • Typo::getToken(int $len): Generates a standard, high-entropy alphanumeric token.
  • Typo::crypto_rand_secure($min, $max): Returns a cryptographically secure random integer suitable for sensitive operations.
  • Typo::createToken(...): An advanced generator with granular control over uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols.

docs.title: ✅ Validation Utilities
docs.text:
• Typo::validateEmail(string $email): Returns a bool based on strict RFC format verification.
  • Typo::int($var): Force casts and cleanses a variable into a valid integer.
  • Typo::float($var): Formats a numerical value as a float with 2 decimal precision.

[!CAUTION] Data Integrity: When using Typo::Xclean() to decode content for editing, remember that the data is no longer "safe" for direct browser rendering. Always re-apply Typo::cleanX() before committing the edited content back to the database.

[!IMPORTANT] Localisation Integrity: The Typo::translate() method relies on the theme's .mo or .po files. If a translation is missing, it will gracefully fall back to the original English string.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Token Class — How typo tokens are used for CSRF protection.
  • Database Class — How sanitized data is persistently stored.
  • Language Class — Managing multi-lingual site content.

docs.title: UiBuilder Class API Reference
docs.text: The UiBuilder class is a high-level UI orchestration engine in GeniXCMS. It allows developers to define complex administrative interfaces using structured PHP arrays (schemas), which are then rendered into professional, responsive HTML.

docs.title: new UiBuilder(array $schema = [])
docs.text:
Initializes a new instance of the builder with a given UI schema.

  • $schema: An associative array defining headers, tabs, and layout elements.
  • Behavior: The constructor automatically detects the active tab from the URL &tab= parameter or falls back to the default_tab defined in the schema.

docs.title: renderHeader(): void
docs.text:
Renders the standardized GeniXCMS module header.

  • Output: Generates a sticky-top row containing the module title, subtitle, version badge, and action buttons.
  • Shrink Logic: Automatically injects the JavaScript needed to shrink the header when the user scrolls down, preserving vertical screen real estate.
  • Note: This method is called internally by render(), but can be called manually if you only need the header.

docs.title: render(): void
docs.text:
The primary entry point to generate the full module interface.

  • Functionality:
    1. Wraps everything in a container-fluid.
    2. Calls renderHeader().
    3. Renders the tab navigation bar (if tabs are present).
    4. Orchestrates visibility: For tab_mode => 'js', it renders all panes; for link mode, it only renders the active tab's content.
    5. Renders any global content or modals defined outside the tab system.

docs.title: renderElement(array $el, bool $return = false): string
docs.text:
Recursive engine used to render individual UI components.

  • $el: The individual element definition array (e.g., ['type' => 'card', ... ]).
  • $return:
    • false (default): Echoes the HTML directly to the output buffer.
    • true: Returns the rendered HTML as a string (uses ob_start() internally).
  • Supported Elements: Handles everything from simple inputs to complex nested grids, loops, and statistical visualizations.

docs.title: 📋 Root Schema Reference
docs.text:
When defining your $schema, the following top-level keys control the UiBuilder behavior:

Key Type Description header array Page identity: title, subtitle, icon, version, and buttons. tabs array A list of tab definitions. Each tab must have a label and content. tab_mode string link (reloads page) or js (client-side switch). tab_style string pills (default), tabs, or modern (underlined). card_wrapper bool If true, the entire tab content area is wrapped in a shadowed card. default_tab string The ID of the tab to show by default. content array Global elements rendered at the bottom (e.g., Modals).

docs.title: 🎨 Card Element Extended Options
docs.text:
The card element ('type' => 'card') supports advanced layout properties:

Property Type Description title string The title displayed in the card header. icon string Bootstrap icon class for the header (e.g., bi bi-gear). no_padding bool Removes padding from .card-body. Ideal for tables. full_height bool Adds h-100 class to the card. header_action string Raw HTML/Elements to be placed on the right side of the card header. footer string Raw HTML content for the card footer. footer_class string Custom CSS classes for the card footer (e.g., bg-transparent border-0). footer_no_padding bool Removes internal padding from the footer container.

docs.title: 🛠️ Supported Element Types
docs.text:
The following table lists all element types supported by the renderElement() engine:

Element Type Description Key Properties Layout & Containers row Standard Bootstrap row for column layouts. items (array of columns) grid Flexbox-based grid container. content (array), class column / col Column within a row or grid. content (array), class card Standard shadowed container for content. title, subtitle, icon, body_elements, footer modal Bootstrap modal dialog window. id, title, header, body_elements, footer, size accordion Collapsible accordion sections. id, items (array of header/body_elements) Forms & Inputs form Standard HTML form container. action, method, fields (array), hidden (array) input / text Standard text input field. name, label, value, placeholder, help number Numeric input field. name, label, value, min, max email Email validation input field. name, label, value password Secure password input field. name, label date Native browser date picker. name, label, value url URL validation input field. name, label, value color Native browser color picker. name, label, value file File upload input. name, label hidden Invisible data storage input. name, value textarea Multi-line text area (supports Rich Editors). name, label, value, rows, class select / dropdown Single or multi-select dropdown menu. name, label, options (assoc array), selected checkbox Toggle switch or checkbox control. name, label, checked (bool) media Integrated GeniXCMS Media Selector. name, label, value (image URL) repeater Dynamic row repeater for complex arrays. name, label, fields (array), value button Action button (Submit/Link). label, btn_type, icon, class Data & Visualization table Responsive data table with hover support. headers (array), rows (array), empty_message stat_cards Dashboard-style summary cards. items (array), style (classic/modern), size chart Interactive Chart.js visualization. id, chart_type, chart_data, chart_options, height recap_stats Horizontal statistics row with percentages. items (array of label/value/percent) progress Single horizontal progress bar. label, value (0-100), color progress_group Grouped progress bars with labels. title, items (array of label/value/max) Navigation & UI tabs_nav Renders a tab navigation bar. id, tabs (array of label/icon) tab_content Renders a specific tab's pane. id, body_elements (array), active (bool) breadcrumb Navigation breadcrumb trail. items (array of label/url/active)
