  • nixomers_transactions_table_headers / nixomers_transactions_table_row / nixomers_transactions_action_menu

docs.title: Adding a Custom Column to Posts Table
docs.text:
// 1. Add Header
Hooks::attach('admin_posts_table_headers', function($headers) {
    // Insert before the LAST item (Selection/Checkboxes)
    array_splice($headers, -1, 0, ['content' => 'Custom Info', 'class' => 'text-center']);
    return $headers;
});

// 2. Add Row Data
Hooks::attach('admin_posts_table_row', function($row, $p) {
    // Insert your data at the same index
    array_splice($row, -2, 0, [['content' => 'My Data', 'class' => 'text-center']]);
    return $row;
}, 10, 2);

docs.title: Adding an Action to the Action Menu
docs.text:
Hooks::attach('admin_posts_action_menu', function($menu, $p) {
    $menu['my_sync'] = [
        'label' => 'Sync to Market',
        'icon'  => 'bi bi-arrow-repeat',
        'href'  => 'index.php?page=my_sync&id=' . $p->id,
        'class' => 'btn btn-light btn-sm rounded-circle border text-info'
    ];
    return $menu;
}, 10, 2);

docs.title: Priority
docs.text:
Attach multiple callbacks to the same hook. Lower priority numbers run first.

Hooks::attach('footer_load_lib', ['Analytics', 'load'], 5);  // runs first
Hooks::attach('footer_load_lib', ['Ads', 'load'], 20);        // runs later

docs.title: Registering Hooks in a Theme
docs.text:
For a modern theme implementation, put all hook registrations inside a dedicated Theme Class within your function.php. This approach keeps your code organized and allows for clean access to theme utilities like the Asset manager.

<?php
class MyModernTheme
{
    public function __construct()
    {
        // 1. Efficiently load assets (Alternative to footer_load_lib echo)
        $this->setupAssets();

        // 2. Attach filters (Title & Content)
        Hooks::attach('post_title_filter',   [self::class, 'injectTitleIcon'], 10);
        Hooks::attach('post_content_filter', [self::class, 'formatContent'], 20);

        // 3. Register Widget zones specifically for this theme
        Widget::addLocation('sidebar_landing', 'Landing Page Sidebar');
    }

    private function setupAssets()
    {
        // Load default Bootstrap from CDN
        Asset::enqueue(['bootstrap-css', 'bootstrap-js', 'bootstrap-icons']);

        // Register theme-specific JS/CSS
        Asset::register('theme-css', 'css', Url::theme() . '/css/style.css', 'header');
        Asset::register('theme-js',  'js',  Url::theme() . '/js/app.js',     'footer', ['jquery']);

        Asset::enqueue(['theme-css', 'theme-js']);
    }

    public static function injectTitleIcon($title)
    {
        // Add a book icon to every post title
        return '📖 ' . $title;
    }

    public static function formatContent($content)
    {
        // Automatically add a "Related Posts" section after content
        $related = '<hr><p class="fw-bold">Don\'t forget to share this post!</p>';
        return $content . $related;
    }
}
new MyModernTheme();

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Hooks Class API — Full method reference.
  • Create a Theme — Where to register hooks in a theme.
  • Create a Module — Using hooks in modules.

docs.title: Practical Guide to OptionsBuilder
docs.text: The OptionsBuilder is a high-level UI engine specifically designed for theme and module settings panels. It automatically synchronizes backend selections with frontend CSS.

docs.title: 🎨 Case 1: Theme Branding & Identity
docs.text:
Use a combination of text, select_media, and color pickers to define your site's visual identity.

$schema = [
    [
        'id'    => 'branding',
        'label' => 'Branding',
        'icon'  => 'fa fa-palette',
        'group' => 'Design',
        'cards' => [
            [
                'title'  => 'Logos & Icons',
                'fields' => [
                    ['type' => 'text', 'name' => 'site_logo', 'label' => 'Logo URL', 'hint' => 'Upload your logo in Media Manager and paste the URL here.'],
                    ['type' => 'text', 'name' => 'favicon', 'label' => 'Favicon URL'],
                ]
            ],
            [
                'title'  => 'Global Colors',
                'cols'   => 3,
                'fields' => [
                    ['type' => 'color', 'name' => 'link_color', 'label' => 'Primary Accent'],
                    ['type' => 'color', 'name' => 'link_hover_color', 'label' => 'Hover Color'],
                    ['type' => 'color', 'name' => 'body_background_color', 'label' => 'Background'],
                ]
            ]
        ]
    ]
];

docs.title: ✍️ Case 2: The Typography Engine
docs.text:
The typo_row is a special element. When used with a supported prefix, the OptionsBuilder::generateFrontendCSS() method automatically injects Google Fonts and CSS rules.

$schema = [
    [
        'id'    => 'typography',
        'label' => 'Typography',
        'icon'  => 'fa fa-font',
        'group' => 'Design',
        'cards' => [
            ['type' => 'typo_row', 'prefix' => 'typo_body', 'label' => 'Body Text'],
            ['type' => 'typo_row', 'prefix' => 'typo_h1', 'label' => 'Main Headings (H1)'],
            ['type' => 'typo_row', 'prefix' => 'typo_nav', 'label' => 'Navigation Links'],
        ]
    ]
];

Resulting Front-end CSS (Automatic): 

body { font-family: "Inter" !important; font-size: 16px; color: #333; }
h1 { font-family: "Poppins" !important; font-weight: 700; }

docs.title: 📏 Case 3: Layout & Spacing Controls
docs.text:
Use range and number fields to allow users to control the physical dimensions of the theme.

$schema = [
    [
        'id'    => 'layout',
        'label' => 'Layout',
        'icon'  => 'fa fa-columns',
        'group' => 'Settings',
        'cards' => [
            [
                'title'  => 'Container Dimensions',
                'fields' => [
                    ['type' => 'range', 'name' => 'container_width', 'label' => 'Max Width', 'min' => 1000, 'max' => 1600, 'step' => 10, 'unit' => 'px'],
                    ['type' => 'range', 'name' => 'content_border_width', 'label' => 'Card Border', 'min' => 0, 'max' => 10, 'unit' => 'px'],
                ]
            ],
            [
                'title' => 'Visibility Toggles',
                'cols'  => 2,
                'fields' => [
                    ['type' => 'toggle', 'name' => 'show_sidebar', 'label' => 'Show Sidebar', 'yes' => 'Visible', 'no' => 'Hidden'],
                    ['type' => 'toggle', 'name' => 'sticky_nav', 'label' => 'Sticky Header'],
                ]
            ]
        ]
    ]
];

docs.title: ⚡ Case 4: Presets & Custom CSS
docs.text:
You can add a "Custom Code" tab to allow power users to override your theme's styles and a "Presets" tab for one-click styling.

$schema = [
    [
        'id'    => 'presets',
        'label' => 'Presets',
        'icon'  => 'fa fa-magic',
        'type'  => 'presets', // Uses the internal preset renderer
        'group' => 'Advanced'
    ],
    [
        'id'    => 'custom_code',
        'label' => 'Custom CSS',
        'icon'  => 'fa fa-code',
        'group' => 'Advanced',
        'cards' => [
            [
                'title' => 'Extra Styles',
                'fields' => [
                    ['type' => 'textarea', 'name' => 'custom_css', 'label' => 'Additional CSS', 'rows' => 15, 'hint' => 'Do not include <style> tags.']
                ]
            ]
        ]
    ]
];

docs.title: 🛠️ Summary of Configuration
docs.text:
When instantiating the builder, use the config array to customize the aesthetic:

$config = [
    'brandName'  => 'Nexus Pro',
    'brandVer'   => 'v2.0',
    'brandAbbr'  => 'NP',      // Text logo
    'brandIcon'  => 'fa fa-rocket', // Icon logo (overrides text)
    'brandColor' => '#f43f5e', // Primary gradient color
    'saveKey'    => 'save_config'
];

$builder = new OptionsBuilder($saved_data, $presets, $palettes, $config);
$builder->render($schema);

docs.title: Pro Tip: Asset Optimization
docs.text: When calling OptionsBuilder::generateFrontendCSS(), the engine automatically detects if you have enabled Site::minifyCSS() in the system settings and applies it to the output, ensuring your theme stays fast.

docs.title: Creating and Using Shortcodes in GeniXCMS
docs.text:
Shortcodes are a powerful feature in GeniXCMS version 2.0.0 that allow you to create dynamic, parameterized content within posts and pages. Unlike plain HTML, shortcodes are processed on the server at render time, making them ideal for injecting complex UI components like Table of Contents, Product Sliders, or Recent Posts.

This guide explains how to programmatically create and use shortcodes in your Modules and Themes.

docs.title: 🏗️ Basic Shortcode Architecture
docs.text:
A shortcode is a tag wrapped in square brackets, such as [my_tag key="value"]. To implement a shortcode in GeniXCMS, you need two things:

  1. A Callback Function: Logic that generates the HTML for the shortcode.
  2. A Hook Connection: Attaching your logic to the post_content_filter filter.

docs.title: 🛠️ Step 1: Create a Callback Function
docs.text:
In your module's index.php or theme's functions.php:

function my_module_render_shortcode($content) {
    // We use the Shortcode::parse library class
    return Shortcode::parse('hello', $content, function($attrs, $original) {
        // Step 1: Set defaults
        $name = $attrs['name'] ?? 'World';
        $color = $attrs['color'] ?? 'primary';

        // Step 2: Return the HTML to replace the [hello ...] string
        return "<span class=\"badge bg-{$color}\">Hello, {$name}!</span>";
    });
}

docs.title: 🏹 Step 2: Connect via Hooks
docs.text:
To make your shortcode work globally on all post/page content, attach your function to the post_content_filter hook.

// Register the filter during module/theme init
Hooks::attach('post_content_filter', 'my_module_render_shortcode');

docs.title: 🧪 Advanced Example: Fetching Content
docs.text:
Imagine a shortcode [user_info id="123"] that fetches a user's name:

$content = Shortcode::parse('user_info', $content, function($attrs) {
    $uid = (int)($attrs['id'] ?? 0);
    $user = User::fetch(['id' => $uid]);

    if (empty($user)) return "<em>User not found</em>";

    return "<strong>" . $user[0]->username . "</strong>";
});

docs.title: 🎛️ Implementation Table (Best Practices)
docs.text: Action Best Practice Rationale Parsing Use Shortcode::parse It is built-in, optimized, and supports complex attribute patterns. Attributes Always set defaults Prevents undefined index notices and ensures consistent UI. Output Escape user data Use htmlspecialchars() on any user-provided attribute used in HTML. Sanitization Avoid HTML tags Shortcodes are for dynamic content; keep the raw tag clean of HTML.

docs.title: 🔒 Handling Aggressive Sanitizers
docs.text:
GeniXCMS uses HTML Purifier to clean post content on save. If you use custom HTML attributes like data-color="red", they might be stripped out by the security filter.

[!IMPORTANT] Use Shortcode Attributes (e.g., [my_tag color="red"]) instead of HTML data- attributes. Shortcode text is preserved during the sanitization process because it's treated as plain content, whereas HTML attributes are often scrubbed.

docs.title: 📝 Checklists for Developers
docs.text:
• Does your shortcode tag have a unique name?
  • Have you attached your logic to post_content_filter?
  • Are you returning the content (not echoing)?
  • Have you documented the available attributes for your users?

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Shortcode Class API — Technical details of the Shortcode::parse method.
  • Hooks API — Documentation for filters and actions.
  • Creating Modules — Comprehensive guide on module development.

docs.title: Practical Guide to UiBuilder
docs.text: The UiBuilder class is the standard engine for building professional administrative interfaces in GeniXCMS. This guide provides detailed examples for common use cases.

docs.title: 🚀 Scenario 1: The Analytics Dashboard
docs.text:
A dashboard typically requires a mixture of overview statistics, quick actions, and data summaries.

$schema = [
    'header' => [
        'title'    => 'System Analytics',
        'subtitle' => 'Real-time monitoring of site performance',
        'icon'     => 'bi bi-speedometer2',
        'button'   => ['label' => 'Download Report', 'url' => '...', 'icon' => 'bi bi-download']
    ],
    'content' => [
        // 1. KPI Stats Cards
        ['type' => 'stat_cards', 'items' => [
            ['label' => 'Total Views', 'value' => '45.2k', 'color' => 'primary', 'icon' => 'bi bi-eye'],
            ['label' => 'New Orders', 'value' => '128', 'color' => 'success', 'icon' => 'bi bi-basket'],
            ['label' => 'Support Tickets', 'value' => '12', 'color' => 'warning', 'icon' => 'bi bi-ticket'],
            ['label' => 'Server Load', 'value' => '14%', 'color' => 'info', 'icon' => 'bi bi-cpu'],
        ]],

        // 2. Grid Layout with Details
        ['type' => 'row', 'items' => [
            ['width' => 8, 'content' => [
                ['type' => 'card', 'title' => 'Recent Activity', 'icon' => 'bi bi-clock-history', 'body_elements' => [
                    ['type' => 'table', 'headers' => ['User', 'Action', 'Time'], 'rows' => [
                        ['Admin', 'Updated Settings', '2m ago'],
                        ['Editor', 'Published Post', '15m ago'],
                    ]]
                ]]
            ]],
            ['width' => 4, 'content' => [
                ['type' => 'card', 'title' => 'Quick Stats', 'body_elements' => [
                    ['type' => 'recap_stats', 'items' => [
                        ['label' => 'Bounce Rate', 'value' => '32%', 'percent' => '+5%', 'color' => 'danger'],
                        ['label' => 'Conversion', 'value' => '4.2%', 'percent' => '+1%', 'color' => 'success'],
                    ]]
                ]]
            ]]
        ]]
    ]
];

docs.title: 📦 Scenario 2: Data Listing (CRUD)
docs.text:
Listing interfaces require search functionality, bulk actions, and pagination to manage large datasets.

$schema = [
    'header' => [
        'title' => 'Customer Directory',
        'icon'  => 'bi bi-people',
        'button' => ['label' => 'Add New Customer', 'url' => '...', 'icon' => 'bi bi-plus-lg']
    ],
    'content' => [
        ['type' => 'card', 'no_padding' => true, 'body_elements' => [
            // Internal Header with Search
            ['type' => 'row', 'class' => 'p-4 border-bottom align-items-center', 'items' => [
                ['width' => 6, 'content' => [
                    ['type' => 'bulk_actions', 'options' => [
                        '' => 'Bulk Actions', 'delete' => 'Delete Permanently', 'export' => 'Export CSV'
                    ]]
                ]],
                ['width' => 6, 'content' => [
                    ['type' => 'search_group', 'placeholder' => 'Search by email or name...']
                ]]
            ]],

            // The Data Table
            ['type' => 'table', 'headers' => [
                ['content' => '<input type="checkbox">', 'width' => '40px'],
                'Customer Name', 'Email Address', 'Status', 'Actions'
            ], 'rows' => [
                [
                    '<input type="checkbox">',
                    '<strong>John Doe</strong>',
                    'john@example.com',
                    '<span class="badge bg-success bg-opacity-10 text-success rounded-pill px-3">Active</span>',
                    '<div class="btn-group">
                        <a href="..." class="btn btn-light btn-sm"><i class="bi bi-pencil"></i></a>
                        <a href="..." class="btn btn-light btn-sm text-danger"><i class="bi bi-trash"></i></a>
                    </div>'
                ]
            ]],

            // Pagination Footer
            ['type' => 'row', 'class' => 'p-4 border-top', 'items' => [
                ['width' => 12, 'content' => [
                    ['type' => 'pagination', 'html' => $paging_generated_by_class]
                ]]
            ]]
        ]]
    ]
];

docs.title: 📝 Scenario 3: Complex Module Settings
docs.text:
Use a multi-tabbed interface with various form elements to keep settings organized.

$schema = [
    'header' => [
        'title' => 'Newsletter Settings',
        'icon'  => 'bi bi-envelope-paper',
        'button' => ['type' => 'button', 'name' => 'save_settings', 'label' => 'Save Changes']
    ],
    'tab_mode' => 'js', // Fast client-side switching
    'card_wrapper' => true, // Wrap tab content in a professional shaded card
    'tabs' => [
        'general' => [
            'label' => 'General', 'icon' => 'bi bi-gear', 'content' => [
                ['type' => 'input', 'name' => 'sender_name', 'label' => 'Sender Name', 'help' => 'This name appears in the recipient inbox.'],
                ['type' => 'select', 'name' => 'smtp_secure', 'label' => 'Security Protocol', 'options' => [
                    'none' => 'None', 'ssl' => 'SSL', 'tls' => 'TLS'
                ]],
                ['type' => 'checkbox', 'name' => 'enable_logging', 'label' => 'Enable Activity Logs']
            ]
        ],
        'templates' => [
            'label' => 'Email Template', 'icon' => 'bi bi-file-earmark-code', 'content' => [
                ['type' => 'heading', 'text' => 'Welcome Message', 'subtitle' => 'Sent to new subscribers'],
                ['type' => 'textarea', 'name' => 'welcome_body', 'label' => 'Message Content', 'class' => 'form-control editor', 'rows' => 10]
            ]
        ]
    ]
];

docs.title: 🔔 Scenario 4: Modal Integration
docs.text:
Modals can be defined directly in the schema and triggered by buttons.

$schema = [
    'header' => [
        'title' => 'Security Audit',
        'button' => [
            'label' => 'Quick Scan', 'icon' => 'bi bi-shield-check',
            'attr' => 'data-bs-toggle="modal" data-bs-target="#scanModal"'
        ]
    ],
    'content' => [
        // Modal Definition
        ['type' => 'modal', 'id' => 'scanModal', 'header' => 'Deep Security Scan', 'body_elements' => [
            ['type' => 'alert', 'style' => 'info', 'content' => 'Scanning your repository for potential vulnerabilities...'],
            ['type' => 'progress', 'label' => 'Analysis Progress', 'value' => 65, 'color' => 'primary']
        ], 'footer' => '
            <button type="button" class="btn btn-secondary rounded-pill px-4" data-bs-dismiss="modal">Cancel</button>
            <button type="button" class="btn btn-primary rounded-pill px-4">Start Scan</button>
        ']
    ]
];

docs.title: The row Element
docs.text: Items in a row can take a width (Bootstrap grid columns, total 12). - Use width => 6 for 2 columns on desktop. - Use width => 4 for 3 columns on desktop.

docs.title: The card Element
docs.text:
• full_height => true: Ensures the card occupies 100% of its container's height.
  • no_padding => true: Removes padding from card-body (perfect for tables).
  • header_action => [...]: Allows placing elements (like buttons or search bars) inside the card header.

docs.title: Formatting Values
docs.text:
Since UiBuilder is schema-driven, you should format your data before passing it to the array:

  • Use Url::theme() or Url::mod() for paths.
  • Wrap statuses in badges: <span class="badge bg-info">New</span>.
  • Use htmlspecialchars() for raw text inputs if manually building HTML content.

docs.title: Vite for Theme Asset Bundling
docs.text: GeniXCMS 2.0.0 introduces native, first-class support for Vite. This integration transforms your frontend development workflow, allowing you to leverage modern tools like SCSS, Tailwind CSS, TypeScript, and ES Modules with near-instant Hot Module Replacement (HMR).

docs.title: 🏗️ Prerequisites
docs.text:
Ensure your local development environment is equipped with:

  • ✅ Node.js (v18.0.0 or higher)
  • ✅ Package Manager (NPM, PNPM, or Yarn)
  • ✅ GeniXCMS v2.0.0 installed and running.

docs.title: 📂 Project Structure
docs.text:
To utilize the Vite engine, your theme should follow this standardized asset organization:

inc/themes/my-modern-theme/
├── assets/
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── main.js         ← Entry Point (JS/TS)
│   │   └── style.scss      ← Main Stylesheet
│   └── dist/               ← Build output (auto-generated)
├── index.php
├── package.json            ← Dependencies & Scripts
└── vite.config.js          ← Bundler Configuration

docs.title: ⚙️ Vite Configuration
docs.text:
The vite.config.js file is the brain of your asset pipeline. Use the following baseline configuration to ensure seamless integration with the CMS:

import { defineConfig } from 'vite';

export default defineConfig({
  build: {
    manifest: true,
    outDir: 'assets/dist',
    emptyOutDir: true,
    rollupOptions: {
      input: 'assets/src/main.js',
    },
  },
  server: {
    cors: true,
    strictPort: true,
    port: 5173,
    hmr: {
        host: 'localhost',
    }
  },
});

docs.title: 🔌 Theme Integration
docs.text:
GeniXCMS provides a dedicated Vite helper class to manage asset injection. In your theme's header.php, simply add:

<!-- Inject Vite HMR client or Manifest-based production bundle -->
<?= Vite::client('assets/src/main.js'); ?>

[!IMPORTANT] Dynamic Loading: The Vite::client() helper is intelligent. It detects if Dev Mode (HMR) is enabled in your site settings. If enabled, it connects to the Vite dev server; otherwise, it resolves hashed filenames from manifest.json.

docs.title: 🚀 Development Workflow
docs.text:
Follow these steps to experience modern theme development:

  1. Dependencies: Install the necessary Node packages. 
    npm install
  2. Toggle HMR: Go to Settings > General and ensure Hot Reloading is enabled.
  3. Start Dev Server: Launch the Vite server in your theme directory. 
    npm run dev
  4. Develop: Edit your SCSS or JS files. Your changes will refresh instantly in the browser without a full page reload.

docs.title: 📦 Production Deployment
docs.text:
When your theme is ready for the world, compile it for maximum performance:

  1. Build Assets: Generate optimized, minified files. 
    npm run build
  2. Disable HMR: In Settings > General, turn off Hot Reloading.
  3. Deploy: GeniXCMS will now automatically serve versioned, cache-basted assets from assets/dist/.

[!TIP] Tailwind CSS Integration: To use Tailwind, simply run npx tailwindcss init -p, configure your template paths in tailwind.config.js, and @import Tailwind into your style.scss. Vite's PostCSS plugin will handle all the heavy lifting!

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Vite Helper API — Detailed reference for the Vite PHP class.
  • Theme Development Guide — How to build a full theme around your assets.

docs.title: Access Control List (ACL) Management
docs.text: GeniXCMS 2.0.0 features a robust, fine-grained Access Control List (ACL) that allows you to define exactly what each user group can see and do within the administration dashboard.

docs.title: 🧭 Group-Based Permissions
docs.text:
The ACL system is built around the 7 core system groups, though individual access levels can be specified for more granular control.

System Group Access Level Description Administrator 0 Full access to every core and module feature. Supervisor 1 Has site-wide management permissions. Editor 2 Manages content and user interactions. Author 3 Focused on content creation. Contributor 4 Can submit content for review. VIP Member 5 Premium subscriber level. General Member 6 Basic frontend interaction and profile.

docs.title: 🏗️ Modifying Permissions (ACL Matrix)
docs.text:
To fine-tune permissions for your site's human resources:

  1. Navigate to Admin > Users > ACL Manager.
  2. The ACL Matrix will display a grid of all registered permission hooks (e.g., POST_ADD, THEME_ACTIVATE, SETTINGS_EDIT).
  3. Configuring: Use the dropdowns to set the Minimum Access Level required for each permission.
  4. Save: Click Update Permissions to apply the new security rules across the application.

docs.title: 🏹 Built-in Security Hooks
docs.text:
GeniXCMS registers several dozen security hooks by default.

  • Post Management: POST_ADD, POST_EDIT, POST_DELETE.
  • System Admin: SETTINGS_EDIT, THEME_ACTIVATE, MODULE_INSTALL.
  • User Governance: USER_ADD, USER_EDIT, ACL_EDIT.

docs.title: 🔌 Modules & Custom ACLs
docs.text:
Third-party modules can safely register their own custom permissions to the matrix.

  • Developer Hook: To register a new permission, use Acl::register('MOD_MYPERMISSION', 'Description'); in your module's constructor.
  • Check Access: Always call if (Acl::check('MOD_MYPERMISSION')) before executing any sensitive logic in your code.

[!CAUTION] Privilege Escalation: Be extremely careful when lowering the requirements for ACL_EDIT. Only Level 0 (Administrator) accounts should typically have the capability to modify the ACL matrix.

[!TIP] Audit Tip: Regularly audit your ACL matrix to ensure that no lower-level group has gained access to sensitive system commands during a new module installation.

docs.title: Output Caching & Performance Settings
docs.text: GeniXCMS's built-in Cache Engine significantly reduces your site's load times and server resource consumption by storing previously rendered pages as static files.

docs.title: 🎨 Managing Output Delivery
docs.text:
To configure your site's caching behavior:

  • Navigate to Settings > General in the administrative sidebar.
  • Look for the Caching and Performance configuration blocks.

docs.title: 🏗️ Activating Static Caching
docs.text:
Static file-based caching is a first-class feature that avoids complex database lookups for repeat visitors.

  1. Toggle: Set Enable Caching to "Yes" to start archiving your site's static assets and pages.
  2. Lifetime: Configure how long each cached version remain valid (default: 3600 seconds).
  3. HMR Support: In Dev Mode, caching is automatically bypassed to ensure developers see live changes immediately.

docs.title: 📐 Caching Architecture
docs.text: Resource Type Cache Strategy Static Pages Indefinite (Updated only on manual invalidation). Blog Posts Timed (Automatically invalidated on new comment or edit). Category Archives Timed (Invalidated on content updates).

docs.title: 🛠️ Security & Management
docs.text:
• Storage Path: All cached files are archived in the assets/cache/ directory.
  • Manual Invalidation: You can clear the entire system cache through the Settings Dashboard whenever you perform a major update.
  • Access Control: Admins can specify which user levels (e.g., Level 0) have permission to clear the cache.

[!CAUTION] Disk Space: Large sites with thousands of pages can consume significant storage in the assets/cache/ directory. Monitor your site's storage capacity regularly.

[!TIP] Performance Hint: Combined with Vite asset bundling and a high-performance PHP 8.⅜.4 engine, GeniXCMS's caching results in sub-second page loads.

docs.title: Organizing Content with Categories
docs.text: Categories provide the primary structural hierarchy for your site's content. They allow you to group related posts together, improving your site’s navigation, user experience, and search engine discoverability.

docs.title: 🧭 Managing Your Taxonomy
docs.text: To access the category management interface: - Navigate to the Categories menu in the left administrative sidebar. - The Category Manager dashboard will display your current organizational tree.

docs.title: 🏗️ Creating a New Category
docs.text:
It is recommended to define your core categories before you begin publishing posts.

  1. Add New: Click the Add Category button in the header section.
  2. Modal Settings: A configuration window will appear.
  3. Parenting: Choose a Parent Category if you are creating a sub-category. Leave as "None" for primary, top-level categories.
  4. Identification: Enter a clear, descriptive Category Name.
  5. Save: Click Save Changes to commit your new category to the database.

docs.title: 🌳 Understanding Category Hierarchy
docs.text:
Categories in GeniXCMS support a nested, tree-like structure:

  • Main Category: Primary topics that define your site's main sections.
  • Sub-Category: Specialized niches within a main category (e.g., "Web Dev" under "Programming").

docs.title: 🛠️ Modifying & Refining
docs.text:
• Naming: Ensure your category names are concise.
  • Organization: You can re-parent categories at any time to reorganize your site's structure as it grows.
  • Deletion: When deleting a category, ensure you reassess the posts currently assigned to it.

[!TIP] SEO Best Practice: Use categories for broad grouping (e.g., News, Tutorials) and use Tags for specific details (e.g., PHP, Laravel, MkDocs). This creates a logical, searchable map for both users and search engines.

docs.title: GeniXCMS CLI Console (genix) Documentation
docs.text: genix is the built-in Command-Line Interface (CLI) for GeniXCMS, designed to help administrators and developers manage the system without accessing the backend dashboard. Tasks such as database management, module and theme scaffolding, user creation, and cache clearing can be executed directly through this console.

docs.title: How to Use
docs.text:
This CLI script is located in the root directory of your GeniXCMS installation. You can run it via the terminal using your PHP binary.

Basic Command: 

php genix <command> [arguments]

To view the full list of available commands directly from your terminal, you can type: 

php genix help

docs.title: 1. ⚙️ System Utility
docs.text:
A collection of commands to view and manage core features and utilities of the application.

Command Arguments Description serve [port] Starts the built-in PHP development server at http://localhost:8000 (or a port based on your provided argument). The site will instantly load similar to using XAMPP/Laragon. Use Ctrl-C to stop the server. sys:info - Displays environment diagnostics such as the PHP version, server OS, Server Time, and detailed Database Client identifier. Very useful for initial troubleshooting. options:list - Displays all System Options in your database table along with their values (partially truncated if too long). Good for peeking at hidden configurations. options:set <key> <value> Forcibly changes an option or adds a new one to GeniXCMS. Example: php genix options:set sitename "New Website" cache:clear - Clears data cache stack as well as Latte template cache files in the assets/cache folder. Frequently used after modifying a template if changes are not immediately visible.

docs.title: 2. 🏗️ Developer Scaffolding Commands
docs.text:
Special commands for developers to generate boilerplates (raw code structure), eliminating the need to manually create directories and files.

Command Required Arguments Description make:theme <name> Generates a new Latte Engine-based theme file structure in /inc/themes/. This command automatically creates the assets folder and template files (header.php, footer.php, index.php, single.php, page.php, and themeinfo.php). Example: php genix make:theme e-commerce make:module <name> Generates scaffolding for a new custom module (plugin) inside the /inc/mod/ directory. PHP classes and docblock comments/metadata are immediately validated and automatically created. Example: php genix make:module payment

docs.title: 3. 👥 User Management
docs.text:
Functions similarly to account management via the dashboard panel, but manually through the command-line.

Command Required Arguments / Options Description user:create <u> <e> <p> [g] Registers a new user by strictly providing arguments in order (username, email, raw password). Includes an optional argument for the Access Group level (0 = Administrator, 4 = Specific Contributor, etc). Example: php genix user:create admin admin@web.com passMe123 0 user:list - Displays all registered user entities complete with their ID, Username, Email, and Action Group Name details natively in a console table. user:delete <id> Permanently removes a user from the database (gx_user and gx_user_detail) based on their numerical unique ID. Example: php genix user:delete 3

docs.title: 4. 🎨 Theme & Module
docs.text:
Terminal-based functions to forcibly toggle Extensions registered in the site repository. Helpful if your site encounters a white screen due to a theme error and you must disable it from the backend server.

Command Required Arguments Description theme:list - Polls the database to list all available system themes, their active status, and release versions out of their respective metadata configurations. theme:activate <folder_name> Switches your Active Theme to the targeted slug folder. Example: php genix theme:activate gneex module:list - Lists all installed modules completely with their respective activity status. module:activate <folder_name> Activates a specific module. Example: php genix module:activate seonix module:deactivate <folder_name> Deactivates a module from its running execution. Example: php genix module:deactivate seonix

docs.title: 5. 🗄️ Database Migrations
docs.text:
This feature tracks the chronological migration history of database schemas and data (utilizing GeniXCMS's installation and upgrade process).

Command Required Arguments Description migrate - Recursively executes and records all unexecuted historical files located within inc/migrations/. migrate:rollback - Unrolls/Rolls back the last executed batch of completed migration files. migrate:create FileName Generates a table installation module boiler script with automatic Timestamping and a ready-to-fill PHP class in inc/migrations/. Example: php genix migrate:create UpdateUserTable

docs.title: Comment Settings & Interactivity
docs.text: GeniXCMS's built-in Comment Engine provides a robust way for your audience to engage with your content. It features a full moderation suite to prevent spam and ensure the quality of user interactions.

docs.title: 🎨 Managing Audience Engagement
docs.text: To configure your site's comment behavior: - Navigate to Settings > General in the administrative sidebar. - Look for the Comments and Moderation configuration blocks.

docs.title: 🏗️ Activating Reader Feedback
docs.text:
Feedback is a powerful tool for community building and SEO.

  1. Toggle: Set Enable Comments to "Yes" to activate the comment form on all post types.
  2. Moderation: Choose whether comments are published instantly or require approval from a Level 0 (Administrator).
  3. HMR Support: Using modern themes with Vite HMR allows for instant visual updates of comment sections during development.

docs.title: 📐 Moderation Workflow
docs.text:
GeniXCMS features a specialized management dashboard for handling feedback.

Status Capability Pending Content that is held back for review before being publicly visible. Approved Public comments that are now live on your posts. Marked as Spam Content that is flagged and removed from the public view.

docs.title: 🛠️ Security & Integrity
docs.text:
• CAPTCHA Support: GeniXCMS's Xaptcha class provides a simple, anti-spam challenge to ensure that commenters are human.
  • XSS Protection: All user-generated content is sanitized through HTMLPurifier to prevent malicious code execution.
  • Access Control: You can allow or disallow commenting capabilities based on user registration status.

[!CAUTION] Anti-Spam Strategy: It is highly recommended to enable Mandatory Moderation for first-time commenters to protect the reputation and integrity of your site.

[!TIP] Developer Hint: Customizing your comment layout? Use the {Hooks::run('post_comment_display')|noescape} hook in your theme to inject a custom feedback interface.

docs.title: System Configuration
docs.text: GeniXCMS provides a centralized management interface to fine-tune your website's behavior, SEO metadata, and technical integrations. Access these tools by navigating to Settings in the main administration sidebar.

docs.title: 🌐 General Settings
docs.text:
This section defines the core identity of your website.

Field Purpose Site Name The primary title of your website. Site Slogan A brief, catchy phrase that appears next to your site name. Keywords Comma-separated terms for search engine indexing. Description A concise summary of your site's content for SEO snippets. Site E-Mail The default address used for system notifications and "From" headers.

docs.title: 📧 E-Mail & SMTP
docs.text: Configure how your server dispatches outgoing communications.

docs.title: 📤 PHP Mail (Default)
docs.text: Recommended for simple hosting environments. It uses the server's internal mail binary to send messages. No additional configuration is required.

docs.title: 🚀 SMTP Server
docs.text: Recommended for high-deliverability requirements (e.g., using Gmail, SendGrid, or Mailgun). - SMTP Host: The address of your mail provider (e.g., smtp.gmail.com). - SMTP Port: Usually 465 (SSL/TLS) or 587 (STARTTLS). - Authentication: Input your full email address and the corresponding password or App Password.

docs.title: 🔗 Permalinks (Smart URL)
docs.text:
The Permalink system allows you to transform complex PHP queries into clean, SEO-friendly paths.

  • Standard: yoursite.com/index.php?post=123
  • Smart URL: yoursite.com/my-awesome-post.html

[!IMPORTANT] Server Config: Enabling Smart URLs requires mod_rewrite on Apache (handled via .htaccess) or specific location blocks on Nginx. If your site returns a 404 after enabling this, check your server's rewrite engine status.

docs.title: 🎨 Branding (Logo & Favicon)
docs.text:
Personalize your site's visual identity.

  • Primary Logo: Upload a high-quality (but optimized) image or provide a direct external URL.
  • Favicon: The small icon appearing in browser tabs. Always provide a full, absolute URL to avoid path resolution issues during routing.
  • Logo Mode: Switch between displaying the Image Logo or the Text-based Site Name.

docs.title: 📚 Library & Frontend CDN
docs.text:
GeniXCMS relies on several industry-standard frontend libraries. You can manage their loading behavior here.

  • jQuery: Enter the specific version number (e.g., 3.7.1) to load from a global CDN.
  • FontAwesome: Enable or disable the FontAwesome icon stack.
  • Bootstrap: Toggle the core layout framework.
  • Summernote: Enable the rich-text editor for the dashboard and frontend forms.

docs.title: 📝 Content & Pagination
docs.text:
Define how your posts and pages are presented to visitors.

  • Posts Per Page: The maximum number of items visible on archive and search pages.
  • Pagination Style:
    • Numbered: Displays a list of page numbers (1, 2, 3...).
    • Pager: Displays simple Previous and Next navigation links.
  • Frontpage Static Content: Allows you to replace the default "Recent Posts" landing page with a static page from your library.
    • Select a page from the dropdown menu to set it as the root of your website.
    • The system will automatically use the Layout Model defined within that specific page's metadata.
  • Search Engine Pinger: A list of RPC-XML pinger services to notify search engines whenever you publish new content.

docs.title: 🛡️ Security & Performance
docs.text:
• Output Caching: Enable to store rendered pages in the assets/cache directory for faster load times.
  • Maintenance Mode: Take your site offline for visitors while you perform updates.
  • CSP Headers: Toggle the Content Security Policy layers (see Security API for technical details).

docs.title: ⚙️ Advanced Configuration Constants
docs.text: These constants are defined in inc/config/config.php and control low-level system behavior.

docs.title: OFFLINE_MODE
docs.text:
!defined('OFFLINE_MODE') ? define('OFFLINE_MODE', false) : null;
When true, all framework assets (jQuery, Bootstrap, Bootstrap Icons, Font Awesome, Inter font, EditorJS tools, etc.) are loaded from local files in assets/js/vendor/, assets/css/vendor/, and assets/fonts/ instead of CDN. Ideal for local development or air-gapped environments.

Value Behavior false (default) Load assets from CDN true Load assets from local vendor files

docs.title: DEVELOPER_MODE
docs.text:
!defined('DEVELOPER_MODE') ? define('DEVELOPER_MODE', false) : null;
When true, reveals the Dev Tools menu in the admin panel with two inspector pages: - Asset Inspector (?page=devtools-assets) — lists all registered/enqueued assets - Hook Inspector (?page=devtools-hooks) — lists all hooks and their callbacks

[!WARNING] Never enable DEVELOPER_MODE in production. It exposes internal system information.

docs.title: ADMIN_THEME
docs.text:
!defined('ADMIN_THEME') ? define('ADMIN_THEME', 'default') : null;
Selects the active admin panel theme. Themes are located in gxadmin/themes/{name}/. Available built-in themes:

Value Description default Classic sidebar layout with Bootstrap moluka Clean SaaS-style, white sidebar, accent #00A3EA fresco Compact financial-dashboard style, lime-green accent ribbon MS Office-style ribbon toolbar layout

See Admin Themes Guide for creating custom themes.

docs.title: ADMIN_DIR
docs.text:
!defined('ADMIN_DIR') ? define('ADMIN_DIR', 'gxadmin') : null;
The directory name of the admin panel. Change this to rename your admin URL for security through obscurity.

[!TIP] SEO Tip: Always fill in the Website Description and Keywords in General Settings. These are the primary sources for meta tags generated in the <head> of your theme.

docs.title: Administrative Dashboard
docs.text: The Dashboard is the command center of your GeniXCMS installation. It provides a real-time, high-level overview of your site's content performance, user engagement, and system health immediately after authentication.

docs.title: 🚀 Accessing the Workspace
docs.text: To enter the dashboard, navigate to your site's administration URL: - Default path: https://yourdomain.com/gxadmin/ - Credentials: Use the administrative Username and Password defined during the installation process.

docs.title: 📊 Performance Overview
docs.text:
The dashboard features a dynamic statistics engine that tracks your most important metrics at a glance.

Metric Business Value Active Posts Total published articles driving site traffic. Static Pages Critical business infrastructure like Home, About, and Contact. Moderation Queue Total comments pending review from your audience. Registered Base The total growth of your user community.

docs.title: 🧭 Master Navigation
docs.text: The left sidebar provides a consistent, intuitive gateway to all core management modules.

docs.title: 📝 Content Management
docs.text:
• Posts: Compose, edit, and organize editorial content.
  • Pages: Manage the structural static hierarchy of your site.
  • Categories & Tags: Architect your content taxonomy for better SEO.

docs.title: 🛠️ System Control
docs.text:
• Media: Central library for images, videos, and document assets.
  • Menus: Visual builder for header, footer, and sidebar navigation.
  • Appearance: Switch between themes and configure global styles.
  • Modules: Extend core features with a robust plugin-style system.

docs.title: 👥 Governance & Settings
docs.text:
• Users: Manage administrative roles and contributor permissions.
  • Settings: Fine-tune site metadata, email dispatchers, and SEO defaults.

docs.title: 🏗️ Programmable Widgets
docs.text:
GeniXCMS 2.0+ features an extensible dashboard layout. Modules can inject custom "Widgets" to display specialized data (e.g., E-commerce sales, Visitor analytics, or Server load).

[!NOTE] Extensibility: Developers can use the admin_page_dashboard_action hook within their modules to populate the dashboard with custom diagnostic or report widgets.

docs.title: 💡 Quick Start Tips
docs.text:
• Check Updates: Monitor the footer for version numbers to ensure you are running the latest security patches.
  • Manage Widgets: Go to Appearance > Widgets to define which content appears in your theme's sidebars.
  • SEO First: Visit Settings > General to define your site's primary keywords before your first post.

[!TIP] Theme Options: Many premium themes provide a "Theme Settings" link in the sidebar under Appearance for granular design control.

docs.title: Dynamic Builder User Guide
docs.text: The Dynamic Builder is a powerful visual page-building module for GeniXCMS, powered by GrapesJS. It allows you to create complex, responsive layouts and interactive components using a simple drag-and-drop interface.

docs.title: Activating the Module
docs.text:
Before using the Dynamic Builder, you must ensure the module is active:

  1. Navigate to Modules > Manage Modules in the sidebar.
  2. Locate Page Dynamic Builder in the list.
  3. Click the Enable button (the lightbulb icon) if it's currently disabled.
  4. Once active, the builder's features will be integrated into your page management workflow.

docs.title: Accessing the Builder
docs.text:
The Dynamic Builder is integrated directly into the GeniXCMS Page management system:

  1. Go to Pages > Add New (or edit an existing page).
  2. Scroll to the bottom of the page form.
  3. You will see a blue card labeled "Design with Dynamic Builder".
  4. Click the Launch Visual Editor button to open the full-screen design interface.

[!TIP] Use the builder for complex landing pages, while the standard editor remains ideal for simple blog posts or text-heavy content.

docs.title: Standard Sections
docs.text:
These are the building blocks of your page, including:

  • Hero Sections: Premium landing page headers like the "Hero Cyber Frontier".
  • Team Sections: Showcase your leadership with interactive grids and hover effects.
  • Content Blocks: Lead paragraphs, material icons, and section wrappers.

docs.title: Interactive Widgets
docs.text:
Modules with dynamic behavior:

  • Testimonial Carousel: A multi-column slider for customer reviews with customizable column counts.
  • Counter Row: Animated numbers that count up as they scroll into view.
  • Accordion/FAQ: Collapsible sections for frequently asked questions.

docs.title: Saving Your Work
docs.text:
Once you are satisfied with your design, click the Synchronize Layout (Save) button in the top right corner. This will sync the generated HTML and CSS back to the main GeniXCMS editor as a single block of content.

[!NOTE] The Dynamic Builder acts as a high-level layout tool. Once the content is synchronized back to the main CMS, it is treated as a standard content block.

docs.title: Tips for Best Results
docs.text:
• Grid First: Always start with a Grid or Row component to establish a solid responsive layout.
  • Layers Panel: Use the Layers panel on the left to navigate complex nested structures easily.
  • Breadcrumbs: Use the breadcrumb trail at the bottom of the canvas to select parent containers (like columns or rows) specifically.

docs.title: Installation Guide
docs.text: GeniXCMS is designed for a friction-less deployment experience. Follow this structured guide to get your CMS up and running in minutes.

docs.title: 🏗️ System Requirements
docs.text: Before you begin, ensure your server environment meets the following specifications:

docs.title: ⚙️ Minimal Stack
docs.text:
• PHP Engine: 8.1 or higher (Recommended: 8.3)
  • Database: MySQL 5.7+, MariaDB 10.3+, SQLite3, or PostgreSQL
  • Extensions: pdo, mbstring, json, gd, zip, xml

docs.title: 🌐 Web Servers
docs.text:
• Nginx (Recommended) with PHP-FPM
  • Apache with mod_rewrite enabled
  • Litespeed

docs.title: 📂 Step 1: Uploading Files
docs.text:
The process is identical regardless of your hosting provider (Shared, VPS, or Dedicated).

  1. Transfer Files: Upload the GeniXCMS package to your webserver root (e.g., public_html or /var/www/html) using FTP, SFTP, or your Control Panel's File Manager.
  2. Permissions: Ensure the following directories are writable (chmod 775 or 777 depending on your server configuration):
    • inc/config/ (Required for generating config.php)
    • assets/images/
    • assets/media/
    • assets/cache/ (If applicable)

docs.title: 🗄️ Step 2: Preparing the Database
docs.text:
GeniXCMS requires a database to store your content and configurations.

  1. Create Database: Access your Database Manager (e.g., phpMyAdmin, cPanel MySQL Wizard).
  2. Assign User: Create a new database user and assign them to the database with ALL PRIVILEGES.
  3. Note Credentials: Keep the following information ready for the installation wizard:
    • Database Name
    • Database Username
    • Database Password
    • Database Host (usually localhost)

docs.title: 🚀 Step 3: Running the Wizard
docs.text:
To launch the automated installation:

  1. Navigate: Open your website URL in any modern browser.
  2. Wizard: You will be automatically redirected to the Installation Wizard.
  3. Config: Fill in the Database details and Site information as prompted.
  4. Finish: Follow the steps until the "Installation Complete" message appears.

docs.title: 🔑 Step 4: Accessing the Dashboard
docs.text:
Once finished, navigate to the administration area to begin building your site:

  • Dashboard URL: https://yourdomain.com/gxadmin/
  • Authentication: Use the Username and Password you defined during the installation process.

[!CAUTION] Security Tip: After a successful installation, it is highly recommended to change the permissions of inc/config/config.php to Read-Only (chmod 444 or 644) to protect your database credentials.

docs.title: Media Settings & File Management
docs.text: The Media settings in GeniXCMS allow you to configure how images and other assets are uploaded, processed, and served by the framework's integrated image-handling library (Intervention Image).

docs.title: 🎨 Image Processing & Optimization
docs.text:
To manage your site's visual assets:

  • Navigate to Settings > Media in the administrative sidebar.
  • The Media Settings dashboard allows you to define default behaviors for automated thumbnail generation and file storage.

docs.title: 🏗️ Managing Resource Limits
docs.text:
To ensure your server remains stable and responsive:

  1. Max File Size: Set the maximum allowed size (in MB) for individual media uploads.
  2. Allowed Types: Configure which extensions (e.g., jpg, png, zip, pdf) your users can upload to the media repository.
  3. Storage Engine: Specify the directory relative to the site root where assets should be archived (default: assets/images/).

docs.title: 📐 Dynamic Thumbnail Generation
docs.text:
GeniXCMS's Image Class can automatically generate optimized versions of your images for different site layouts.

Size ID Dimension (px) Best Use Case Thumbnail 150 x 150 Post lists and archive snippets. Medium 300 x 300 Typical inline post content. Large 1024 x 1024 Hero sections and lightboxes.

docs.title: 🛠️ Security & File Integrity
docs.text:
• MIME Checking: GeniXCMS performs strict server-side MIME type validation to prevent malicious code execution through disguised image files.
  • Sanitization: All uploaded filenames are automatically cleaned to remove special characters and spaces, ensuring consistent paths across all platforms.
  • Access Control: You can restrict media upload capabilities to specific user levels using the ACL Manager.

[!CAUTION] Asset Deletion: Deleting an image from the Media Manager will permanently remove it from the server. Ensure that no posts or pages are actively linking to that resource before confirming the deletion.

[!TIP] Performance Hint: Enable WebP Conversion (if available on your server's GD/ImageMagick engine) within the media settings to significantly reduce your site's visual footprint and improve page-load speed.

docs.title: Site Navigation Builder
docs.text: The Menu Manager allows you to visually construct and organize your website's navigation. GeniXCMS supports multiple menu structures (header, footer, sidebar) that can contain static pages, categories, module links, or custom URLs.

docs.title: 🧭 Accessing the Menu Manager
docs.text: To manage your site's navigation: - Click the Menus link in the left administrative sidebar. - Choose a Menu Container to edit (default containers are mainmenu and footer).

docs.title: 🏗️ Adding & Organizing Items
docs.text:
Within a menu container, you can add, reorder, and nest navigation links.

  1. Select Container: Click the title of the menu you wish to modify.
  2. Add Item: Switch to the Add Item tab in the management view.
  3. Parenting: Leave blank for top-level links. Select a parent to create a dropdown or nested submenu.
  4. Labeling: Enter the Menu Name as it should appear to your visitors (e.g., Home, Our Services).
  5. Styling: Use the Menu Class field to apply specialized CSS classes for specific icons or custom designs.

docs.title: 🔗 Supported Menu Types
docs.text:
GeniXCMS features an intelligent link resolution system that keeps your navigations consistent across routing changes.

Menu Type Description Best Use Case Static Page Links to a single static page created in the Page Manager. About, Contact, Policy. Category Links to a category archive displaying all posts in that group. Blog, News, Tutorials. Module Links to a specific frontend hook provided by an active module. Contact Forms, Custom Plugins. Custom Link Allows you to specify any internal or external URL manually. Social media or external sites.

[!IMPORTANT] Menu IDs: When calling menus in your themes with Menus::getMenu('ID'), ensure you use the Menu ID (e.g., mainmenu) rather than the display title.

[!TIP] Latte Integration: In your theme's header.php, use {Menus::getMenu('mainmenu')|noescape} to render your primary navigation instantly.

docs.title: Extending with Modules
docs.text: The Modules system is the primary extensibility layer for GeniXCMS. It allows you to inject advanced functionality — from e-commerce stores to dynamic page builders and syntax highlighters — without ever modifying the core system files, ensuring a safe and clean upgrade path.

docs.title: 🧭 Module Lifecycle
docs.text: Managing your site's feature set is performed through the Modules dashboard in the administrative sidebar.

docs.title: 📥 Installing New Features
docs.text:
1. Deployment: Upload your module directory to inc/mod/{module-slug}/.
  2. Activation: Navigate to Admin > Modules.
  3. Registration: Find your new module in the list and click Install. GeniXCMS will register its hooks and optional database migrations.

docs.title: ⚙️ Module Configuration
docs.text: Many modules provide their own administration panel (defined in options.php). If available, a Settings button will appear next to the module entry, allowing you to fine-tune its behavior.

docs.title: 📦 Bulk Processing
docs.text: To manage multiple extensions efficiently, use the Bulk Action form at the bottom of the module list: - Activate: Batch enable multiple selected modules. - Deactivate: Batch disable multiple selected modules. - Remove: Permanently delete selected modules (Note: Only deactivated modules can be removed for safety).

docs.title: 📤 Uninstallation
docs.text: Clicking Uninstall deactivates the module's hooks and removes it from the active registry. Note that this action does not delete the physical files from your server.

docs.title: 🏹 High-Impact Bundled Modules
docs.text:
GeniXCMS v2.0.0 comes pre-equipped with an enterprise-grade suite of extensions:

Feature Business Value Dynamic Builder Professional, grid-based layout builder for complex pages. Contact Form High-performance, anti-spam form with email notifications. Newsletter Complete subscription management and email blasting engine. SEOnix Advanced metadata controls and SEO health checking. Code Highlight Prism/Highlight.js integration for developer-focused blogs. SSO Auth Integration with external identity providers (OAuth).

docs.title: 🔗 Navigating Module Content
docs.text:
If a module provides a frontend interface (e.g., a "Contact Us" or "Product Catalog" page), you can easily add it to your site's navigation:

  1. Navigate to Admin > Menus.
  2. Select your target menu (e.g., mainmenu) and click Add Item.
  3. Menu Type: Choose the Mod option.
  4. Selection: Use the dropdown to select the specific module page hook.
  5. Save: Click the save button to publish the link.

[!TIP] Developer Guide: If you are a developer looking to build your own extension, please refer to our deep-dive How to Create a Module guide for technical specifications and CLI scaffolding instructions.

docs.title: Global Localization & Multi-Language Settings
docs.text: GeniXCMS is designed for the international web. Its built-in Language Engine allows you to serve your content and administration dashboard in multiple languages, tailoring the user experience to different global regions.

docs.title: 🌎 System-Wide Localization
docs.text: To configure your site's linguistic behavior: - Navigate to Settings > General in the administrative sidebar. - Look for the System Language dropdown menu.

docs.title: 🏗️ Activating a Language
docs.text:
GeniXCMS supports Gettext-style PO and MO files for high-performance translations.

  1. Selection: Choose from the available system languages (e.g., English, Indonesian, Spanish).
  2. Activation: Once selected and saved, the Administration Dashboard and all core System Messages will instantly switch to the new locale.
  3. HMR Support: If you are in Dev Mode, translation updates are often picked up without a full page reload.

docs.title: 🌳 Managing Multi-Language Content
docs.text:
GeniXCMS features a distinctive Subdomain or Path-based locale detection system.

Locale Pattern Description Best Use Case Path-based yoursite.com/en/my-post.html Recommended for most SEO-friendly sites. Subdomain es.yoursite.com/my-post.html Ideal for large, regional-specific portals.

docs.title: 🛠️ Translation & Extension
docs.text:
• Theme Translations: Your active theme can provide its own translation files in inc/themes/{your-theme}/inc/lang/.
  • Module Support: Modules are fully internationalized, allowing for custom labels and messages in any language.
  • Language Class: Developers can use Language::get('KEY') or the shorthand _('Key') to ensure their code remains localized.

[!TIP] SEO Localization: Always ensure each language version of your site has the correct lang attribute in its <html> tag. GeniXCMS's header.php template logic handles this automatically: <html lang="{$website_lang}">.

[!IMPORTANT] PHP Extensions: For optimal performance and full feature support, ensure that the gettext extension is enabled in your server's PHP configuration.

docs.title: Static Page Management
docs.text: In GeniXCMS, Pages are used for static content that isn't chronological (like blog posts). Pages are ideal for foundational site infrastructure such as your About Us, Contact, Privacy Policy, or Terms of Service sections.

docs.title: 🧭 Navigating the Page Repository
docs.text: To manage your site's static infrastructure: - Click the Pages menu in the left administrative sidebar. - The Page Manager will display a list of all current pages, their publication status, and author information.

docs.title: 📝 Creating a New Static Page
docs.text:
Establishing new pages is a straightforward process similar to blog post creation but without the focus on temporal categorization.

  1. Add New: Click the Add New button in the top-right corner.
  2. Editorial Core:
    • Page Title: Define the main heading for your page.
    • Page Content: Use the integrated rich-text editor to craft your layout and text.
  3. Metadata & Controls:
    • Status: Toggle between Release (Live) or Draft (Private).
    • Publish Date: Set the official creation date or schedule it for future visibility.
  4. Completion: Click Submit to finalize the page.

docs.title: 🛠️ Modifying & Mass Actions
docs.text:
Managing existing pages is efficient through the management dashboard.

  • Edit/Delete: Use the action icons on the right of each page entry.
  • Bulk Operations: Select multiple pages using the master checkbox and use the bulk actions dropdown to Publish, Unpublish, or Delete assets in one click.

docs.title: 💡 Key Differences: Pages vs. Posts
docs.text:
While both use the same editor interface, they serve distinct purposes:

Feature Static Pages Blog Posts Chronology Timeless (Lasting content) Timely (Dated updates) Taxonomy Independent (No Categories) Categorized & Tagged Common Use Mission, Services, Policy News, Articles, Tutorials

[!TIP] Performance Tip: Since pages are static and change infrequently, they are excellent candidates for Output Caching. Ensure caching is enabled in Settings > General for faster page delivery.

docs.title: Permalink & Smart URL Settings
docs.text: The Permalink system in GeniXCMS (Smart URL) is essential for both your site's SEO value and the overall user experience. It allows you to transform complex PHP query strings into clean, human-readable URLs.

docs.title: 🎨 Transforming URLs
docs.text: To configure your site's Permalink behavior, you need to edit your system configuration file. This is an environment-level setting.

docs.title: 🏗️ Activating Smart URLs
docs.text:
Activating clean, SEO-friendly paths is a 2-step process that requires both CMS and server configuration.

  1. System Config: Open inc/config/config.php and change !defined('SMART_URL') ? define('SMART_URL', false) : null; to true.
  2. Server Engine:
    • Apache: Generally handled by the provided .htaccess file in your site root. Ensure mod_rewrite is enabled on your server.
    • Nginx: Requires a custom location block to handle URI rewriting effectively.

docs.title: 📐 Choosing Your URL Suffix
docs.text:
GeniXCMS 2.0.0 uses an intelligent, global suffix engine to ensure all routes remain consistent.

URL Prefix Example URL Best Use Case .html (Default) /my-post.html Recommended for most SEO-friendly sites. None (Trailing Slash) /my-post/ Focuses on a completely flat, directory-style URL structure.

docs.title: 🛠️ Security & Consistency
docs.text:
• Canonical URLs: GeniXCMS automatically generates canonical links to ensure search engines don't penalize your site for duplicate content caused by multiple URL patterns.
  • Routing Rules: The routing engine is powered by regex, allowing for complex, nested URL structures (e.g., /category/tech/post-slug.html).

[!CAUTION] Broken Links: If your site returns a 404 Page Not Found after enabling Smart URLs, your web server's "Rewrite Engine" is likely disabled. Check your .htaccess or nginx.conf settings immediately.

[!TIP] SEO Metadata: Combined with clean slugs, ensure your Website Description and Keywords in General Settings are filled out for maximum search engine impact.

docs.title: Post Types and Post Params
docs.text: GeniXCMS provides a flexible way to extend content beyond standard posts and pages using Post Types and Post Params. This allows developers to build complex content structures like Portfolios, Testimonials, or Product catalogs while maintaining a clean core.

docs.title: 1. Post Types
docs.text: A Post Type is a way to categorize different kinds of content in the posts table. By default, GeniXCMS primarily uses: - post: For standard blog articles. - page: For static pages.

docs.title: Using Custom Post Types
docs.text:
You can define your own post types (e.g., portfolio) when inserting content via the API:

$vars = [
    'title'   => 'Project Alpha',
    'content' => 'Full project description...',
    'type'    => 'portfolio', // Custom Type
    'author'  => 'admin',
    'status'  => '1'
];
Posts::insert($vars);

To fetch specific post types:

$projects = Query::table('posts')->where('type', 'portfolio')->get();

docs.title: 2. Post Params (Custom Fields)
docs.text: Post Params are metadata linked to a specific post ID, stored in the posts_param table. This is similar to "Custom Fields" in WordPress.

docs.title: API Methods
docs.text:
The Posts class provides several static methods to manage params:

Method Description Posts::addParam($key, $value, $post_id) Add a new parameter. Posts::editParam($key, $value, $post_id) Update an existing parameter. Posts::getParam($key, $post_id) Retrieve a parameter value. Posts::existParam($key, $post_id) Check if a parameter exists. Posts::delParam($key, $post_id) Delete a parameter.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
// Adding a custom "price" param to post #42
Posts::addParam('price', '150.00', 42);

// Retrieving it in a theme
$price = Posts::getParam('price', $post->id);
echo "Price: $" . $price;

docs.title: 3. Adding Params to the Admin Form
docs.text: GeniXCMS allows you to extend the post/page admin interface through two primary methods: the modern Params Builder and raw Hooks.

docs.title: Method 1: Using Params::register() (Recommended)
docs.text:
This is the standard, cleanest way to add custom fields. You define a configuration array, and GeniXCMS handles the rendering, styling, and data-binding for you.

// In your module's init file
Hooks::attach('init', function() {
    $vars['bottom'][] = [
        'grouptitle' => 'Advanced Details',
        'groupname' => 'extra_details',
        'post_type' => 'portfolio', // Optional: Show only for this type
        'fields' => [
            [
                'title' => 'Project Subtitle',
                'name' => 'post_subtitle',
                'type' => 'text',
                'boxclass' => 'col-md-6'
            ],
            [
                'title' => 'Project Status',
                'name' => 'project_status',
                'type' => 'dropdown',
                'value' => ['In Progress', 'Completed', 'Maintenance'],
                'boxclass' => 'col-md-6'
            ]
        ]
    ];

    Params::register($vars);
});
  • Locations: You can use ['sidebar'] or ['bottom'] to specify where the group appears.
  • Field Types: Supports text, textarea, checkbox, and dropdown.
  • Filtering: Use 'post_type' in the group or field to limit visibility to specific content types.

docs.title: Method 2: Using Raw Hooks
docs.text:
Use raw hooks for full control over the HTML layout or for complex UI elements like data tables or file uploaders.

  • post_param_form_bottom: Injects below the editor in the main content area.
  • post_param_form_sidebar: Injects at the bottom of the right-hand sidebar.
// In your module's init file
Hooks::attach('post_param_form_sidebar', function() {
    global $data;
    $post = $data[0]['post'][0] ?? null;
    $subtitle = ($post) ? Posts::getParam('post_subtitle', $post->id) : '';

    echo '
    <div class="card border-0 shadow-sm rounded-4 mb-4">
        <div class="card-header bg-white border-0 py-3">
            <h6 class="fw-bold m-0 text-info">Extra Details</h6>
        </div>
        <div class="card-body pt-0">
            <label class="form-label small text-muted text-uppercase fw-bold">Subtitle</label>
            <input type="text" name="param[post_subtitle]" class="form-control bg-light border-0" value="'.$subtitle.'">
        </div>
    </div>';
});

docs.title: Automatic Saving Logic
docs.text: Both methods leverage GeniXCMS's built-in metadata synchronization. As long as your input names follow the param[YOUR_KEY] convention, the system will automatically handle: 1. Saving/Updating: During post creation or update. 2. Mapping: Linking the data to the correct post_id in the posts_param table. 3. Sanitization: Values are automatically cleaned before storage.

docs.title: Manual Saving Logic (Advanced)
docs.text:
If you need custom processing before saving, you can hook into post_submit_add_action or post_submit_edit_action:

Hooks::attach('post_submit_edit_action', function($post_data) {
    // Custom logic after a post is saved
    $id = Typo::int($_GET['id']);
    if (isset($_POST['special_field'])) {
        // Do something special
    }
});

docs.title: Posts Management Guide
docs.text: The Posts module is the heart of your content generation workflow. It provides a robust, user-friendly interface for managing your site's articles and blog content.

docs.title: 🧭 Navigating the Posts Repository
docs.text:
To access the posts management area:

  1. On the left navigation menu, click POSTS.
  2. The Content Repository page will appear, displaying a list of your existing assets with real-time engagement insights.

docs.title: 📝 Creating New Content
docs.text:
To compose a fresh post:

  1. Click the New Post button in the header section.
  2. The Compose Post form with a premium editorial layout will be displayed.

docs.title: 🧩 Core Content Elements:
docs.text:
• Primary Heading: Enter a captivating title for your article.
  • Editor: Use the rich-text editor (Summernote or Editor.js) to craft your story.
  • Hero Asset: Select or upload an image that represents your content.
  • Classifiction Tags: Connect concepts using comma-separated terms.

docs.title: ⚙️ Side Controls & Metadata
docs.text:
The sidebar provides essential tools for the life-cycle of your post:

Option Description Primary Category Categorize your post to keep the content structured. Visibility Status Switch between Release (Live) or Draft (Private). Chronology Manually override the publication date if needed.

docs.title: 🛠️ Efficient Multi-Tasking (Bulk Actions)
docs.text:
GeniXCMS allows you detect and manage multiple assets simultaneously:

  1. Selection: Use the checkboxes in the first column to select posts.
  2. Select All: Click the master checkbox in the header to select all visible assets.
  3. Apply Action: From the bulk actions dropdown at the bottom, choose:
    • Commit to Live: Instantly publish all selected drafts.
    • Revert to Draft: Take live content offline.
    • Permanent Deletion: Remove multiple assets at once.

[!TIP] SEO Best Practice: Always pick a relevant Category and add multiple Tags to improve internal linking and search engine discoverability.

docs.title: Programmable Admin Dashboard
docs.text: GeniXCMS 2.0 introduces a powerful, architecture-driven administrative dashboard. Beyond just a static overview, the dashboard is now fully programmable, allowing developers to extend its functionality using the UiBuilder engine and the Hook System.

docs.title: Technical Overview
docs.text: The dashboard layout is generated from a centralized Schema Array. This array defines every element from stat cards and tables to interactive charts. By using the admin_dashboard_schema filter, modules can intercept and modify this structure before it renders.

docs.title: Core Components
docs.text:
• UiBuilder: The rendering engine that converts arrays into Bootstrap 5 components.
  • Hooks: Strategic points for data modification and HTML injection.

docs.title: 1. Simple Action Hooks
docs.text:
Traditional action hooks allow you to echo HTML directly into specific areas of the dashboard.

  • admin_page_notif_action: Renders at the very top (alerts/notifications).
  • admin_page_top_action: Renders below the welcome header.
  • admin_page_bottom_action: Renders at the footer of the dashboard content.

docs.title: 2. The Schema Filter (admin_dashboard_schema)
docs.text: This is the most powerful way to "program" the dashboard. It allows you to add complex components like grids, tables, and charts.

docs.title: Example: Adding a New Card
docs.text:
Hooks::attach('admin_dashboard_schema', function($schema) {
    $mySection = [
        'type' => 'card',
        'title' => 'Project Status',
        'body_elements' => [
            [
                'type' => 'raw',
                'html' => '<div class="alert alert-info">All systems go!</div>'
            ]
        ]
    ];

    // Append to dashboard content
    $schema['content'][] = $mySection;
    return $schema;
});

docs.title: Supported UI Elements
docs.text: You can utilize any standard UiBuilder element within your dashboard schema:

docs.title: Stat Cards
docs.text:
Renders a row of summary statistics. 

[
    'type' => 'stat_cards',
    'items' => [
        ['label' => 'Total Orders', 'value' => '150', 'icon' => 'bi bi-cart', 'color' => 'primary']
    ]
]

docs.title: Interactive Charts (New in 2.0)
docs.text:
GeniXCMS 2.0 integrates Chart.js support directly into the UiBuilder. 

[
    'type' => 'chart',
    'chart_type' => 'line', // bar, pie, doughnut
    'chart_data' => [
        'labels' => ['Mon', 'Tue', 'Wed'],
        'datasets' => [[
            'label' => 'Server Load',
            'data' => [10, 45, 30],
            'borderColor' => '#0d6efd'
        ]]
    ]
]

docs.title: Data Tables
docs.text:
Tables with automatic formatting and styling. 

[
    'type' => 'table',
    'headers' => ['ID', 'Module', 'Status'],
    'rows' => [
        ['1', 'SEO Manager', 'Active'],
        ['2', 'Cache Pro', 'Inactive']
    ]
]

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Use Semantic Colors: Always use GeniXCMS standard colors (primary, success, info, warning, danger) for visual consistency.
  2. Responsive Grid: Wrap your elements in row types to ensure they align correctly on mobile and desktop.
  3. Namespace IDs: When creating charts or custom HTML elements, use unique IDs (e.g., my_mod_chart_01) to prevent conflicts with other modules.

Info

For more information on building complex backend pages, see the documentation on Module Development.

docs.title: System Maintenance & Diagnostics
docs.text: GeniXCMS provides a centralized hub for monitoring your server environment and managing updates for the core system, modules, and themes. Access these tools via System in the administrative sidebar.

docs.title: 1. System Health
docs.text: The System Health page performing real-time diagnostics on your server environment to ensure it meets the GeniXCMS operational requirements.

docs.title: Key Diagnostics:
docs.text:
• PHP Version: Ensures your environment is running on PHP 8.2 or higher.
  • Critical Extensions: Verifies the presence of PDO, GD, ZipArchive, cURL, and OpenSSL.
  • Server Configuration: Checks for file_uploads capability, post_max_size, and max_execution_time.

docs.title: Extending Diagnostics
docs.text:
Developers can inject custom requirements via the system_health_requirements filter hook.

Example usage in a module: 

Hooks::add('system_health_requirements', function($req) {
    $req['Exif Extension'] = [
        'req' => 'Enabled',
        'val' => extension_loaded('exif') ? 'Enabled' : 'Disabled',
        'status' => extension_loaded('exif')
    ];
    return $req;
});

docs.title: 2. System Update Repository
docs.text: The Updates page centralizes version management for your entire ecosystem by communicating with the GeniXCMS Marketplace API.

docs.title: Core Updates
docs.text: GeniXCMS automatically checks for core updates in real-time. If a new version is available, a "Download Update" button will appear, providing a direct link to the latest stable release.

docs.title: Module & Theme Updates
docs.text:
The system performs a Batch Check for every installed module and theme.

  • Up-to-Date: Indicates your component matches the stable version in the cloud.
  • New Version Available: Triggers if a developer has released a newer version on the marketplace.

docs.title: Manual Sync
docs.text: You can force a fresh version check by clicking the Manual Sync button at any time.

docs.title: Extending Update Logic
docs.text:
You can manipulate or append update data using the following filter hooks:

  • system_updates_modules
  • system_updates_themes

Example: 

Hooks::add('system_updates_modules', function($modUpdates) {
    // Custom logic to modify the update list
    return $modUpdates;
});

docs.title: Theme & Appearance Management
docs.text: GeniXCMS features a sophisticated, decoupled theme engine that allows you to instantly change the visual presentation of your site without affecting your underlying content or data structure.

docs.title: 🎨 Activating a New Theme
docs.text:
Changing your site's aesthetic is an instantaneous process through the Appearance dashboard.

  1. Deployment: Upload your theme directory to inc/themes/{theme-slug}/.
  2. Selection: Navigate to Admin > Appearance.
  3. Activation: A list of available themes will be displayed along with their name, description, and preview icon. Click Activate to instantly make the theme live.

docs.title: ⚙️ Theme Configuration (Options)
docs.text:
Premium themes often provide a dedicated administration panel (options.php) for granular control over colors, layouts, and typography.

  • Access Level: If a theme supports options, a Theme Settings link will appear in the left sidebar menu under the Appearance group.
  • Dynamic Updates: Changes made in the theme options are applied instantly to the frontend.

docs.title: 🏗️ Managing Widget Placement
docs.text:
Widgets provide dynamic functional blocks for your theme's sidebars and footers (e.g., Categories, Recent Posts, Search).

  1. Navigate to Admin > Appearance > Widgets.
  2. The dashboard displays all Available Widgets and the core Widget Areas defined by your active theme.
  3. Assigning: Use the Select Widget and Select Area dropdowns to dispatch a widget to a specific sidebar or footer zone.

docs.title: 🚀 Theme Development Quick Start
docs.text:
If you are a developer or designer interested in crafting your own unique experience:

  • CLI Engine: Use php genix make:theme my-theme to scaffold an entire theme structure in seconds.
  • Engine: GeniXCMS consumes Latte files for lightning-fast, secure template rendering.
  • Guide: Read the Create a Theme documentation for a technical deep-dive.

[!IMPORTANT] Asset Loading: Modern GeniXCMS themes (v2.0.0+) often leverage Vite for asset bundling. If your theme assets are not loading correctly, ensure your Vite dev server is running or that a production npm run build has been executed.

docs.title: User & Role Management
docs.text: GeniXCMS features a robust user management system that allows you to control who can access your site and define their operational capabilities through a group-based permissions architecture.

docs.title: 👥 Navigating the User Repository
docs.text: To manage your site's human resources: - Navigate to the Users menu in the left administrative sidebar. - The User Manager dashboard will display a searchable list of all registered accounts, including their email, access level, and active status.

docs.title: 🏗️ Registering New Users
docs.text:
Adding a new administrator or collaborator is a centralized process performed within the dashboard.

  1. Add User: Click the Add User button in the top-right corner.
  2. Configuration Modal: A user registration window will appear.
  3. Account Identity:
    • Username: The unique identifier for login.
    • E-Mail: The primary address for communication and password recovery.
    • Password: Secure authentication string for the user.
  4. Access Level: Choose the appropriate Group Level (e.g., Administrator or General Member).
  5. Completion: Click Save Changes to commit the user to the database.

docs.title: 📝 Modifying Member Profiles
docs.text: To update existing user information: 1. Select User: Find the target member in the user list. 2. Action: Click the Edit (Pencil) icon on the right associated with their account. 3. Update: Modify the profile details, change their password, or adjust their role permissions. 4. Save: Click the Update button (usually located in the top-right header area) to apply changes.

docs.title: ⚖️ Understanding Access Groups (Roles)
docs.text:
GeniXCMS uses a fine-grained, numerical-based Access Control Matrix (ACL) to define roles:

Role Name Access Level Responsibilities Administrator 0 Full system control, including settings and user management. Supervisor 1 Has site-wide management permissions. Editor 2 Manages content and user interactions (Moderation). Author 3 Creation and editing of their own content. Contributor 4 Can submit content for review. VIP Member 5 Premium subscriber with special access. General Member 6 Basic profile management and commenting.

docs.title: 🛠️ Security & Deletion
docs.text:
• Permanent Deletion: Use the Delete (Trash) icon on the right of any user entry.
  • Bulk Removal: Use the master checkbox to select multiple accounts and apply the bulk action to remove them simultaneously.

[!CAUTION] Account Removal: Deleting a user is a permanent action. Ensure you have transferred their assets (Posts/Pages) to another account if you wish to retain their content before proceeding with deletion.

[!TIP] Audit Tip: Regularly audit your user list and ensure that only trusted individuals have Level 0 (Administrator) access to protect the integrity of your site's core configurations.

docs.title: Display Management with Widgets
docs.text: Widgets provide a flexible, modular way to add content and functionality to your site's sidebars and footers. GeniXCMS's Widget Engine supports 3 core types: Built-in, Module-based, and Custom HTML.

docs.title: 🎨 Managing Widget Placement
docs.text:
To configure your site's dynamic functional blocks:

  • Navigate to Appearance > Widgets in the left sidebar.
  • The Widget Manager displays two primary columns:
    • Available Widgets: A library of all registered widgets (e.g., Categories, Recent Posts, Search).
    • Widget Areas: The specific sidebar or footer zones defined by your current active theme.

docs.title: 🏗️ Assigning a Widget
docs.text:
Assigning a widget to your theme's frontend is a 3-step process.

  1. Select Widget: In the Available Widgets column, choose the widget you wish to activate.
  2. Dispatch: Use the dropdown associated with that widget to select the Target Widget Area (e.g., Main Sidebar).
  3. Completion: Click Add Widget to instantly publish that functional block to the frontend.

docs.title: 🛠️ Specialized Widget Types
docs.text:
GeniXCMS 2.0.0 supports several distinctive widget types:

Widget Type Best Use Case System Managed Core features like Recent Posts, Categories, and Site Search. Module-bound Functionality provided by active modules (e.g., Newsletter Signup, Contact Map). Custom HTML Allows users to paste their own code (e.g., Advertisement scripts, Social feeds).

docs.title: ⚙️ Widget Configuration
docs.text:
Many widgets can be fine-tuned after placement. In the Widget Areas column, you can:

  • Configure: Adjust specific settings like titles, number of posts to display, or custom HTML content.
  • Reorder: Use the up/down arrows to change the vertical stack within a sidebar.
  • Remove: One-click deletion of a widget from its assigned area.

[!TIP] Developer Hint: If your active theme doesn't show enough sidebar areas, you can register new ones in your theme's function.php using Theme::registerSidebar('ID', 'Name');.

[!IMPORTANT] Security: When using Custom HTML widgets, the framework's Content Security Policy (CSP) still applies. If you are pasting external scripts (like Google Adsense), ensure the provider's domain is whitelisted in Security Settings.
